error_codes.detail: The motor control system has detected that the drum is not spinning as commanded, or current to the motor is excessive. LG direct-drive motors are generally very reliable, but the hall sensor (which monitors rotor position) is a common failure point.

fixes.title: Reduce load and retry
fixes.description: Remove half the laundry and restart. LE from overloading is common.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes. This resets the motor control firmware.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor
fixes.description: The hall sensor is a small sensor near the rotor. It's inexpensive and often the sole cause of LE. Remove the back panel, locate the sensor near the motor, and swap it.
fixes.warning: Unplug washer and lay it on its front to access the motor.

fixes.title: Inspect the rotor and stator
fixes.description: Check for damage to the rotor magnets and stator windings. Replace damaged components.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, then restart with a reduced load. After motor sensor replacement, the code clears on the first successful cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hall sensor replacement doesn't resolve LE, the stator or main control board needs professional evaluation.

error_codes.meaning: Door open or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The washer has detected that the door is not properly closed or the door lock mechanism cannot engage. The washer will not start or will stop mid-cycle for safety. dE1 and dE2 are more specific door lock fault codes.

causes.description: Laundry or fabric caught in the door seal

fixes.title: Check for laundry in the door seal
fixes.description: Pull back the door gasket and check for small items caught between the gasket and drum.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push the door until it clicks definitively.

fixes.title: Inspect the door catch
fixes.description: Look at the door latch hook and the catch bracket. If the plastic is cracked or bent, replace the assembly.

fixes.title: Test the door lock
fixes.description: Use a multimeter to test the door lock switch for continuity when engaged.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure the door is firmly latched and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door closes and clicks but the error shows, the lock solenoid assembly needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Tub clean reminder — drum needs cleaning
error_codes.detail: This is not an error — it is a maintenance reminder that the drum should be cleaned. LG recommends cleaning the tub every 30 cycles to prevent mold, mildew, and odor buildup. The reminder will clear after running a Tub Clean cycle.

causes.description: Drum clean cycle due after ~30 washes
Reminder not cleared after running Tub Clean

fixes.title: Run the Tub Clean cycle
fixes.description: Select 'Tub Clean' on the cycle selector. Add a washing machine cleaner tablet or 1 cup of bleach to the empty drum (not the dispenser) and run the full cycle.
parts: Affresh or LG washing machine cleaner

fixes.title: Clear the reminder
fixes.description: The tCL indicator clears automatically once a Tub Clean cycle completes. If it lingers, press and hold the Tub Clean button for 3 seconds or power cycle the washer.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run a complete Tub Clean cycle. The tCL counter resets automatically after the cycle finishes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Tub Clean is a maintenance reminder, not a fault. No professional service needed.

error_codes.meaning: Water overflow error — drum overfilling
error_codes.detail: The water level sensor has detected that the drum has filled above the maximum safe level. The washer will attempt to drain immediately. This is usually caused by a failed water inlet valve or a malfunctioning pressure sensor.

causes.description: Failed water inlet valve stuck open
Pressure hose to sensor kinked or disconnected

fixes.title: Check the pressure hose
fixes.description: The pressure hose connects the tub to the pressure sensor. Make sure it is not kinked, blocked, or disconnected.

fixes.title: Test the pressure sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect the hose from the sensor and gently blow into the hose port — you should hear a click from the sensor.

fixes.title: Test and replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: A stuck-open inlet valve continuously allows water in. Test by turning off the water supply when the error occurs. If water stops, the valve needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off water supply and unplug the washer. Drain remaining water through the pump filter. After repair, restart with water supply restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the inlet valve is replaced and overflow continues, the pressure sensor needs replacement or the control board has a fault.

error_codes.meaning: Heater error — water heater circuit fault
error_codes.detail: On LG washers with an internal water heater (steam and sanitary cycles), the control detected an abnormal heating circuit — the element isn't drawing current, or the thermistor never confirmed a temperature rise. The wash continues without heated water on most models, but steam/sanitize cycles are disabled.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes. A transient HE from a one-time heating glitch may clear.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Access the element at the base of the tub and check continuity — an open element reads OL.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the element.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Check the NTC sensor resistance — should read roughly 10k ohms at room temperature.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes and restart. If HE returns when a heated cycle begins, the element or thermistor needs testing.
error_codes.models_affected: LG front-load washers with steam/sanitary heating
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and thermistor both test good but HE persists, the control board heater relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load — washer cannot distribute the load for spin
error_codes.detail: The washer tried to spin but the drum load is unevenly distributed, so it fills with water and re-tumbles to redistribute the items rather than risk a violent off-balance spin. After several failed balance attempts the cycle stops with UB. A single heavy item (bath mat, hoodie) or a not-quite-level machine is the usual cause.

causes.description: Single heavy/bulky item bunched on one side

fixes.title: Redistribute the load
fixes.description: Open the lid/door, separate tangled items, and add a couple of towels to balance a single bulky item.

fixes.title: Level the washer
fixes.description: Check with a level front-to-back and side-to-side; adjust the leveling feet so the unit doesn't rock.

fixes.title: Run a smaller, mixed load
fixes.description: Loading to about 2/3 capacity with mixed item sizes lets the drum balance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door, redistribute the laundry, close, and restart a Spin cycle. UB clears once the load balances.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UB occurs on every load even when balanced and level, the suspension dampers or rotor position sensor may need professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Excess suds detected — too much foam in the tub
error_codes.detail: The washer sensed excessive foam and paused to run extra rinses and a foam-reduction routine before continuing. Too much detergent, non-HE detergent, or soft-water over-sudsing is almost always the cause — the foam interferes with draining and spin and can leak from the door.

fixes.title: Let the cycle run
fixes.description: The washer will add rinses to clear the foam — this can take 30+ minutes. Don't interrupt it.

fixes.title: Use less HE detergent
fixes.description: Switch to HE detergent and use only 1–2 teaspoons for a normal load. Most people use far too much.

fixes.title: Run a Tub Clean / rinse cycle
fixes.description: Run an empty Tub Clean cycle to flush residual detergent from the system.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Wait for the suds-reduction routine to finish, then restart. Reduce detergent next load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD is almost never a hardware fault. Call a pro only if it appears with correct HE detergent and a clear drain.

error_codes.meaning: Cool Down — washer-dryer combo lowering tub temperature (not a fault)
error_codes.detail: On LG washer-dryer combo units, Cd is a status message, not an error. After the drying phase the unit runs a cool-down to lower the temperature inside the tub before unlocking the door. It clears on its own.

causes.description: Normal cool-down at end of drying cycle

fixes.title: Wait for cool-down to finish
fixes.description: Let the unit complete the cool-down — the door will unlock when it's done.

error_codes.reset_instructions: No action needed. Cd clears automatically when the tub cools.
error_codes.models_affected: LG washer-dryer combo units
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Cd is not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Over-current error — motor drawing too much current
error_codes.detail: The motor control board detected that the motor is drawing excessive current. The washer shuts down to protect the motor from damage. On LG direct-drive washers this is commonly the hall sensor rather than the motor itself.

causes.description: Hall sensor failure causing incorrect current feedback

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items and restart with half load.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace hall sensor
fixes.description: The hall sensor is the $15–$30 fix that resolves CE in most cases on LG direct-drive washers.
fixes.warning: Unplug and lay washer on front to access motor.

fixes.title: Test and replace motor if needed
fixes.description: Test stator winding resistance if hall sensor replacement doesn't help.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes, reduce load, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hall sensor replacement doesn't resolve CE, the motor or control board needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Hall sensor error — motor speed sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The hall sensor measuring motor rotation speed has failed. The motor control board cannot safely spin the drum without accurate speed feedback.

fixes.title: Replace the hall sensor
fixes.description: The most cost-effective repair for SE on LG direct-drive washers.
fixes.warning: Lay washer on front — motor is at the back.

fixes.title: Check 3-wire harness
fixes.description: Inspect the 3-wire connector from hall sensor to control board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After hall sensor replacement, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new hall sensor and error persists, control board hall sensor input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Water level pressure sensor error
error_codes.detail: The pressure sensor measuring the water level in the drum has failed or is reading incorrectly. The washer cannot safely control fill without this sensor.

fixes.title: Check pressure hose
fixes.description: Locate the small hose running from the tub to the pressure sensor. Check for kinks or disconnection.

fixes.title: Blow test the sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect the hose from the sensor. Gently blow — you should hear audible clicks.

fixes.title: Replace pressure sensor
fixes.description: If sensor doesn't click, replace it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 60 seconds, then restart and let it fill. If PE returns during fill, recheck that the pressure hose is fully seated on the sensor and free of kinks before replacing parts.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and clear hose don't resolve PE, the control board pressure input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: EEPROM error — control board memory failure
error_codes.detail: The control board's EEPROM (non-volatile memory storing settings and calibration data) has failed or become corrupted. The washer may cycle through errors or behave erratically.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes. EEPROM corruption can sometimes be cleared.

fixes.title: Replace main control board
fixes.description: EE almost always requires control board replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If EE returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: EE is a control board hardware failure. Professional replacement recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Clutch motor error (top-load models)
error_codes.detail: On LG top-load washers with a clutch motor, the clutch actuator motor has failed. The clutch switches the drum between agitation and spin modes.

fixes.title: Access the clutch motor
fixes.description: On top-load LG models, the clutch motor is beneath the drum.
fixes.warning: Unplug and lay machine on its side.

fixes.title: Test clutch motor
fixes.description: Apply 12V — should run freely.

fixes.title: Replace clutch assembly
fixes.description: If motor or switch has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 60 seconds, then plug back in and start a Rinse & Spin cycle. If E6 returns as soon as the drum tries to switch between agitate and spin, the clutch hardware fault is confirmed.
error_codes.models_affected: LG top-load washers with agitator/impeller (WT series)
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Clutch motor replacement requires significant disassembly of top-load models.

error_codes.meaning: Button error — control panel key stuck
error_codes.detail: The control board sees one of the panel buttons held down continuously, so it locks out the keypad and won't start a cycle. Detergent residue or moisture under the membrane is the usual culprit — the button physically sticks, or the keypad contact shorts and registers a constant press.

fixes.title: Check for stuck button
fixes.description: Press each button and check for sticky feel.

fixes.title: Clean control panel
fixes.description: Wipe with dry cloth around all buttons.

fixes.title: Replace UI board
fixes.description: If button remains stuck.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. Clear stuck button before restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UI board replacement doesn't fix bE, main board button interface has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Noise sensor error (front-load models)
error_codes.detail: On LG front-load washers with a noise sensor (used for vibration detection), the sensor has failed. Some models use this to adjust spin speed based on imbalance detection.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check sensor connector
fixes.description: Locate noise sensor (usually near the motor) and reseat connector.

fixes.title: Replace noise sensor
fixes.description: If error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes to reset the control board, then restart the cycle. If nE reappears, reseat the noise sensor connector near the motor before retrying — a loose plug often clears it.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: nE rarely prevents normal washing — cycle may complete with reduced spin speed.

error_codes.meaning: Duct blockage detected — exhaust duct 80% blocked
error_codes.detail: LG's Flow Sense sensor has detected that your dryer's exhaust duct is approximately 80% blocked. Airflow is significantly reduced, causing longer dry times, overheating risk, and potential fire hazard. This is the single most-searched LG dryer error code with nearly 10,000 searches per month.

causes.description: Lint buildup in exhaust duct over time
Kinked or crushed flexible duct behind dryer
Exterior vent cap clogged or flap stuck closed
Duct run too long or too many 90° bends

fixes.title: Clean the lint filter
fixes.description: Remove the lint filter (inside the door) and clean thoroughly. This should be done after every cycle.

fixes.title: Disconnect and clean the exhaust duct
fixes.description: Pull the dryer from the wall and disconnect the duct from the back. Use a dryer vent brush kit to clean the full length of the duct from both ends.

fixes.title: Check the exterior vent
fixes.description: Go outside and locate the exhaust vent. Remove any lint buildup, bird nests, or debris. Ensure the flap opens freely.

fixes.title: Replace flexible duct with rigid metal
fixes.description: Flexible foil or plastic ducts crush easily and trap lint in their ridges. Replace with smooth rigid metal duct for runs over 3 feet.

fixes.title: Recheck duct routing
fixes.description: Maximum recommended duct run: 25 feet with no more than two 90° elbows. Reduce run length if possible.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the duct, run an Air Only (no heat) cycle for 20 minutes. The D80 code will clear once the Flow Sense sensor detects adequate airflow. If it doesn't clear after a full cycle, blockage remains.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the duct is completely clean and the code persists, the Flow Sense sensor itself may have failed. Professional diagnosis needed.

error_codes.meaning: Duct blockage detected — exhaust duct 90% blocked
error_codes.detail: LG's Flow Sense system has detected a critical 90% duct blockage. At this level, the dryer will reduce heat output or stop the cycle entirely to prevent overheating and fire risk. The D90 code is more urgent than D80 and requires immediate attention. This is the second most-searched LG dryer error with nearly 3,000 monthly searches.

causes.description: Severe lint buildup in exhaust duct
Multiple blockages: lint filter + duct + exterior cap
Duct too long or too many bends causing low flow

fixes.title: Stop using the dryer immediately
fixes.description: A 90% blockage is a fire risk. Do not continue using the dryer until the duct is cleared.
fixes.warning: Fire hazard — stop all drying until resolved.

fixes.title: Deep-clean the entire duct system
fixes.description: Clean the lint filter, the full length of the exhaust duct, the connection point at the dryer, and the exterior vent cap. Use a professional-length dryer vent brush.

fixes.title: Check for duct damage
fixes.description: If the duct is made of flexible plastic or foil, it may have crushed inside a wall. Replace with rigid metal duct. Consider hiring a duct cleaning service for in-wall runs.

fixes.title: Test airflow manually
fixes.description: After cleaning, hold your hand at the exterior vent while the dryer runs on Air Only. You should feel strong airflow.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After completely clearing all blockages, run the dryer on Air Only for 20–30 minutes. The D90 code will clear once adequate airflow is confirmed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct cleaning doesn't clear the code, the Flow Sense sensor may have failed, or you may need a professional duct cleaning service for complex wall-routed duct runs.

error_codes.meaning: Duct blockage detected — exhaust duct 95% blocked
error_codes.detail: Critical duct blockage at 95%. The dryer has nearly no exhaust airflow and is at serious risk of overheating and fire. The dryer may refuse to operate or will only run briefly before shutting down. Treat this as an emergency.

causes.description: Duct physically collapsed or crushed inside wall

fixes.title: Stop using the dryer immediately
fixes.description: 95% blockage is a fire emergency. Do not run the dryer.
fixes.warning: FIRE HAZARD — do not operate until fully resolved.

fixes.title: Call a professional duct cleaning service
fixes.description: At 95% blockage, the duct may have compacted lint that a brush alone won't clear. A professional service uses high-powered vacuum equipment.

fixes.title: Inspect the duct in the wall
fixes.description: If the duct runs inside walls, there may be a physical collapse. You may need to replace the entire run.

error_codes.reset_instructions: The D95 code will clear only after a thorough, professional-level duct cleaning. Run Air Only cycle to confirm airflow is restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: At 95% blockage, a professional duct cleaning service is strongly recommended. DIY cleaning may not be sufficient for compacted lint deep in the duct.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — temperature sensor not reading
error_codes.detail: The dryer's thermistor (temperature sensor) is not reading correctly. The dryer cannot safely regulate heat without accurate temperature data.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: The thermistor is usually on the exhaust duct inside the dryer. At room temp, test resistance: ~10k ohms expected.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at the thermistor.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: A simple pull-and-click replacement if the sensor is accessible.
fixes.warning: Unplug dryer first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug for 1 minute then restart. Code clears after a successful cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor is new and error continues, the control board thermistor input is faulty.

error_codes.meaning: Child lock is active — controls are locked
error_codes.detail: The child lock feature has been enabled. All control buttons except Power are disabled. This is not an error — it is an intentional lock mode.

fixes.title: Disable child lock
fixes.description: Press and hold the Child Lock button for 3–5 seconds until the CL indicator turns off. On some models, press and hold 'Temp' for 3 seconds.

fixes.title: Power cycle if a button is stuck
fixes.description: If CL won't clear, unplug the dryer for 60 seconds, plug back in, and hold the Child Lock button again. A sticky button may need cleaning around its edges.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the Child Lock button for 3 seconds to unlock.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Child lock does not require service.

error_codes.meaning: No power to the heating element
error_codes.detail: The dryer is operating but the heating element is not receiving voltage. This typically indicates a wiring issue, a failed heater relay on the control board, or a problem with the home's electrical supply.

causes.description: Tripped circuit breaker on the dryer's 240V circuit
Failed heater relay on main control board
Burned wire or connector to heating element

fixes.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixes.description: Electric dryers use a 240V circuit with two breaker switches. If one side trips, the dryer runs (motor works on 120V) but won't heat. Reset both breaker switches.

fixes.title: Inspect the heating element wiring
fixes.description: Check the wiring terminals at the heating element for burn marks or loose connections.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and check for continuity. Should read approximately 10–15 ohms.

fixes.title: Check control board relay
fixes.description: If element and wiring are good, the relay that controls element power has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reset the circuit breaker first. If that doesn't resolve it, unplug dryer for 5 minutes and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Electrical faults (heater relays, control boards) require professional diagnosis if basic checks don't resolve the issue.

error_codes.meaning: Exhaust duct ~75% blocked — airflow restriction warning
error_codes.detail: The dryer's flow sensor measured restricted airflow through the exhaust and estimates the duct is roughly 75% obstructed. Drying takes much longer and runs hotter. D75 is the first of LG's airflow-warning tier — left uncleared it climbs to d80/d90/d95 as the blockage worsens. Lint buildup in the duct, vent cap, or lint filter is almost always the cause.

causes.description: Crushed or kinked flexible transition hose

fixes.title: Clean the lint filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the lint screen before every load. A clogged screen alone can trigger D75.

fixes.title: Clear the full exhaust duct
fixes.description: Disconnect the duct and clean its entire length plus the exterior vent cap with a vent brush.

fixes.title: Check the transition hose
fixes.description: Replace any crushed or kinked flexible hose behind the dryer with rigid or semi-rigid duct.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the lint filter and exhaust duct, then restart. D75 clears once airflow is restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the duct and vent are verified clear but D75 returns, the flow sensor or blower wheel may need inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Power supply error — improper voltage at the cord or terminal block
error_codes.detail: The dryer detected improper voltage reaching the control — typically a miswired power cord, a loose terminal-block connection, or a power-supply interruption. Electric dryers need 240V across two hot legs; if one leg is missing or the cord is wired wrong, PS appears.

causes.description: Power cord miswired or loose at terminal block

fixes.title: Check the breaker
fixes.description: Confirm BOTH halves of the dryer's double-pole breaker are on. A half-tripped breaker leaves one leg dead.

fixes.title: Inspect the cord and terminal block
fixes.description: Unplug, remove the terminal cover, and check that the cord wires are tight and undamaged on the block.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before removing the terminal block cover.

fixes.title: Verify outlet voltage
fixes.description: Have an electrician confirm 240V across the legs and 120V each leg to ground at the outlet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reset both halves of the breaker and confirm the cord is tight at the terminal block, then restart.
error_codes.models_affected: LG electric dryers
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Open wiring inside the wall outlet or a damaged terminal block requires a licensed electrician.

error_codes.meaning: Door error — dryer door not fully closed
error_codes.detail: The control does not see the door latched, so it won't start the cycle. Usually the door simply isn't pushed fully shut, but a failed door switch or misaligned strike can cause dE with the door closed.

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Push until it clicks. Check nothing (a dryer sheet, garment) is blocking the latch.

fixes.title: Test the door switch
fixes.description: Open the door and press the switch plunger — listen for a click. No click or no continuity = failed switch.

fixes.title: Check strike alignment
fixes.description: Confirm the door strike lines up with the latch; adjust if the door has sagged.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly until it clicks, then press Start. If dE persists with the door shut, test the switch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new door switch doesn't clear dE, the control board door input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor error — drying temperature sensor out of range
error_codes.detail: The thermistor (temperature sensor) reported a reading outside its valid range, so the control can't safely regulate heat. tE3 points specifically to the sensor circuit being open, shorted, or disconnected. The dryer stops to prevent over- or under-heating.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes — a transient sensor glitch may clear.

fixes.title: Reseat the thermistor connector
fixes.description: Locate the thermistor on the blower housing and reseat its connector.

fixes.title: Test and replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Check resistance against spec (varies by model). Out-of-range = replace.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the blower housing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and restart. If tE3 returns, reseat then test the thermistor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor and clean wiring don't resolve tE3, the control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor error — secondary/outlet temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: A second temperature error in the tE family — the control sees the secondary (outlet) thermistor reading out of range or inconsistent with the inlet sensor. As with tE3, the dryer stops to avoid unsafe heating.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to clear a transient fault.

fixes.title: Inspect both thermistors
fixes.description: Reseat connectors on the inlet and outlet thermistors; a mismatch between them can trigger tE4.

fixes.title: Test and replace the sensor
fixes.description: Measure resistance against spec and replace the out-of-range thermistor.
fixes.warning: Unplug before reaching into the blower housing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and restart. If tE4 returns, reseat and test both thermistors.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both thermistors test good but tE4 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Humidity sensor error — moisture sensing failure
error_codes.detail: The humidity sensor that detects when clothes are dry has failed. The dryer will continue running but cannot auto-detect dryness — it will run to the full timer duration only.

causes.description: Humidity sensor bars dirty (coated with fabric softener)

fixes.title: Clean the humidity sensor bars
fixes.description: The sensor is usually two metal bars inside the drum near the front. Clean with rubbing alcohol on a cotton ball. Fabric softener coating is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Check resistance between the bars — should change when you hold them with wet fingers vs dry.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning sensor bars, run a test cycle on Auto Dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: HS rarely causes complete drying failure — dryer still runs, just can't auto-sense. Low urgency.

error_codes.meaning: Gas supply error — no gas detected (gas dryer models only)
error_codes.detail: The gas dryer is not detecting a gas flame during ignition attempts. This can indicate the gas supply is off or the igniter/gas valve has failed.

fixes.title: Check the gas supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm the gas shutoff valve behind the dryer is fully open.

fixes.title: Check home gas supply
fixes.description: Confirm other gas appliances in the home are working.

fixes.title: Test the igniter
fixes.description: The igniter glows orange before the gas flame lights. If it glows but no flame, the gas valve coils have failed.
fixes.warning: Work with gas appliances only if qualified. If in doubt, call a technician.

fixes.title: Replace igniter if not glowing
fixes.description: No glow = igniter failure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore gas supply and restart.
error_codes.models_affected: LG gas dryer models (DLGX series with gas)
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Any gas dryer repair involving the gas valve, igniter, or burner assembly should be performed by a qualified technician if you are not experienced with gas appliances.

error_codes.meaning: High temperature error — drum temperature exceeded safe limit
error_codes.detail: The dryer drum temperature has exceeded the safety threshold. Similar to HC — a blocked vent is the cause in the vast majority of cases.

fixes.title: Clean vent system thoroughly
fixes.description: Clean lint filter, full duct run, exterior cap.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: Test for continuity after cleaning. Tripped thermostat = replace.

fixes.title: Test thermal fuse
fixes.description: Blown fuse = no heat but motor runs.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing vent and replacing failed components, unplug 5 minutes before restarting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If vent is clear and thermostats are fine but overheating persists, heating element relay has stuck closed.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — cycle interrupted
error_codes.detail: The control board lost electrical power partway through a drying cycle and is notifying you that the cycle did not finish. Clothes may still be damp. This is a notice, not a fault — it's almost always a brief power outage or a tripped breaker on the dryer's circuit.

fixes.title: Restart cycle
fixes.description: Press Start to begin a new cycle.

fixes.title: Check circuit breaker
fixes.description: If breaker tripped, reset and investigate cause.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start. PF clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Leakage detected — water found in base tray
error_codes.detail: The anti-flood device has detected water in the base tray of the dishwasher. This indicates a leak somewhere in the unit. The LG dishwasher will drain and shut down to prevent water damage.

causes.description: Loose water supply or drain hose connection
Overfill from stuck water inlet valve

fixes.title: Find and drain the base tray
fixes.description: Tilt the dishwasher slightly forward (with power off) to drain water from the base. Use towels.

fixes.title: Check the door seal
fixes.description: Run your hand along the entire door gasket. Replace if cracked, torn, or detached.

fixes.title: Check all hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect supply line, drain hose, and internal hose connections inside the door for looseness.

fixes.title: Inspect the tub
fixes.description: With the unit emptied, look for cracks in the plastic tub, especially at the sump area.

error_codes.reset_instructions: The AE code will clear automatically once the base tray is dry and the source of the leak is fixed. Run a short cycle to confirm no recurrence.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak source is found, the tub or pump housing may have a hairline crack requiring professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The LG dishwasher cannot drain water within the expected time. Water is pooling in the bottom of the tub.

causes.description: Drain filter clogged with food debris
Drain hose kinked or not high-looped
Garbage disposal knockout plug not removed

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the bottom rack. Turn and lift out the filter assembly. Rinse under water, scrubbing debris with a soft brush. Reinstall firmly.

fixes.title: Check drain hose routing
fixes.description: The drain hose must have a high loop under the counter before connecting to the sink drain.

fixes.title: Check garbage disposal connection
fixes.description: If connected to a disposal, run the disposal before the dishwasher cycle.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter is clean and hose is clear, test the pump for operation.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, press Start to resume or restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the drain filter is clean and no drain hose obstruction exists, the pump needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — not filling with water
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher is not receiving sufficient water. The cycle cannot proceed without water fill.

causes.description: Water supply valve under sink closed

fixes.title: Check the supply valve
fixes.description: Under the sink, confirm the dishwasher supply valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Check for kinks
fixes.description: Trace the supply hose from valve to dishwasher for kinks.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixes.description: Disconnect the hose at the dishwasher inlet port and clean the mesh filter.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply is confirmed good but no fill, replace the inlet valve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore water supply and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and still no fill, the inlet valve solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Water overflow — overfill protection triggered
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher has detected that the water level is above the maximum safe fill point. The unit will drain immediately.

causes.description: Float switch stuck in down position

fixes.title: Check inlet valve
fixes.description: Turn off water supply. If water stops entering, inlet valve is stuck open.

fixes.title: Check float switch
fixes.description: The float switch is in the base. Gently lift the float — it should move freely.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If valve is confirmed stuck open.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the dishwasher's breaker for 60 seconds (most LG dishwashers are hardwired), then restore power. The unit should drain the excess water automatically; once empty, start a new cycle to confirm FE is cleared.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If inlet valve is replaced and overflow continues, the float switch or control board is faulty.

error_codes.meaning: Heater failure — water not heating
error_codes.detail: The control board has detected that wash water is not reaching the target temperature — the heating element isn't producing heat. Dishes come out poorly cleaned and won't dry properly. An open-circuit heating element is the most common cause, followed by a failed temperature sensor.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Test continuity — 15–25 ohms expected.

fixes.title: Test NTC sensor
fixes.description: ~6k ohms at room temperature.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install appropriate part.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run full program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and sensor good, control board relay failed.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an open or shorted circuit from the NTC sensor that measures wash water temperature. Without a valid reading the board disables heating for safety, so dishes wash in cold water and dry poorly. The thermistor itself usually fails; check its connector first.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: ~6k–7k ohms at room temp.

fixes.title: Replace NTC sensor
fixes.description: Located near heating element.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the dishwasher's breaker for 60 seconds (LG dishwashers are typically hardwired), then restore power. After replacing the sensor, run a normal cycle and confirm the water heats without tE returning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new NTC and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Leakage detected — flood protection activated
error_codes.detail: Water has been detected in the base tray of the LG dishwasher. The unit drains and shuts down.

fixes.title: Tilt to drain base
fixes.description: Power off, tilt forward to drain base.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power first.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for damage.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect all internal fittings.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no visible leak, internal crack requires professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Motor/pump over-current error
error_codes.detail: The control board has detected the circulation pump motor drawing more current than its safe limit and has shut the motor down to prevent damage. The cycle stops with dishes unwashed. A pump impeller jammed by glass, food debris, or a seed is the most common cause; a failed motor winding comes next.

fixes.title: Check pump for debris
fixes.description: Remove filter and clear impeller area.
fixes.warning: Use gloves.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace circulation pump
fixes.description: If motor failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: LG dishwashers are usually hardwired — turn off the breaker for 60 seconds instead of unplugging. After clearing any debris from the pump, restore power and run a short cycle to confirm CE is gone.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump replaced and CE persists, control board motor relay failed.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer fan error — fan motor not operating
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan motor in the freezer compartment has failed or is not spinning at the correct speed. Without this fan, cold air cannot circulate from the evaporator coils to the freezer and refrigerator compartments. Food will warm up within hours.

causes.description: Fan blade broken or hitting ice/frost

fixes.title: Check for ice blockage
fixes.description: Remove the freezer back panel. Look for excessive frost or ice buildup around the fan blade. If present, manually defrost by leaving the refrigerator off for 24–48 hours.

fixes.title: Spin the fan blade
fixes.description: After defrosting, spin the fan blade by hand. It should rotate freely. If it's stiff or hitting something, clear the obstruction.

fixes.title: Test the fan motor
fixes.description: Disconnect the fan and apply 12VDC. If it doesn't spin with direct power, the motor has failed.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: The motor is mounted on a bracket behind the freezer back panel. Unscrew, disconnect, and install new motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, plug in and allow 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize. The error code should clear automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice buildup recurs within weeks after clearing, the defrost heater or defrost control is failing — this needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost heater error — defrost cycle not completing
error_codes.detail: The automatic defrost system has failed to complete a defrost cycle. Over time, frost will accumulate on the evaporator coils, eventually blocking airflow and causing the refrigerator to stop cooling. This is a common LG refrigerator issue often linked to a failed defrost heater or thermal limiter.

causes.description: Thermal fuse (limiter) blown on heater circuit

fixes.title: Manually defrost the coils
fixes.description: Turn off the refrigerator and allow all frost to melt. This provides temporary relief while you diagnose the root cause.

fixes.title: Access the evaporator and defrost heater
fixes.description: Remove the freezer back panel. The defrost heater wraps around or sits near the evaporator coils.

fixes.title: Test the heater for continuity
fixes.description: A working heater shows continuity (a few ohms). Open circuit = burned heater.

fixes.title: Test the thermal limiter
fixes.description: Small bi-metal devices on the heater circuit. Test each for continuity — a blown limiter shows open circuit.

fixes.title: Replace failed components
fixes.description: Install new heater, limiter(s), or thermostat as needed.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator and allow ice to melt before accessing heater.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, the defrost system will run on its regular schedule (every 8–12 hours). The Er dH code should clear after a successful defrost cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If all defrost components test correctly, the main control board defrost control logic has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor error
error_codes.detail: The condenser fan motor (located near the compressor at the back bottom of the refrigerator) has failed. Without this fan, heat cannot be expelled from the condenser coils, causing the compressor to overheat and the refrigerator to stop cooling.

causes.description: Debris or pet hair blocking condenser fan

fixes.title: Clean the condenser coils and fan area
fixes.description: Access the back of the refrigerator and remove the access panel. Vacuum out all dust and pet hair from the fan blades and condenser coils.

fixes.title: Spin the condenser fan blade by hand
fixes.description: With power off, spin the fan blade. It should rotate freely. If stiff, the bearing has failed.

fixes.title: Replace the condenser fan motor
fixes.description: The motor is usually held by a single bracket. Disconnect wiring, unscrew bracket, and install new motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing the condenser area.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, plug in and allow several hours for temperatures to stabilize. The code clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan is clean and free but the error persists, the control board fan relay may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer temperature sensor error
error_codes.detail: The control board is getting an out-of-range reading from the NTC thermistor that measures freezer compartment temperature. Without a valid reading the board runs the compressor on a fallback schedule, so the freezer may run too warm or too cold. An open-circuit thermistor is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: At 32°F/0°C: ~16k ohms.

fixes.title: Reseat connector
fixes.description: Firmly reconnect sensor wiring.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Clip-on replacement to evaporator.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Allow 24 hours temperature stabilization after repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an open or shorted circuit from the ice maker temperature sensor. The board can't confirm the ice tray is cold enough to harvest, so ice production slows or stops entirely. The sensor itself usually fails, though heavy frost over the sensor can cause the same misread.

fixes.title: Defrost ice maker area
fixes.description: Fully thaw before diagnosing.

fixes.title: Test and replace sensor
fixes.description: Check resistance, replace if out of spec.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press ice maker reset after repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If sensor replaced and error persists, ice maker control circuit failed.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker fan error
error_codes.detail: The control board sees no feedback signal from the fan motor that circulates cold air through the ice maker compartment. Without airflow the ice room warms up and ice production stops. Either the fan motor has failed, or — very commonly — ice has built up around the blade and physically jammed it.

fixes.title: Defrost ice maker
fixes.description: Allow complete defrost before fan diagnosis.

fixes.title: Test fan motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC — should spin freely.

fixes.title: Replace fan motor
fixes.description: Access through ice maker compartment.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 60 seconds to reset the board, then restore power. If ice was jamming the fan, leave the ice compartment door open to thaw first. Allow 24 hours for ice production to resume after repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fan replaced and error persists, control board fan circuit failed.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — main board to sub-board
error_codes.detail: The main control board cannot communicate with the display or sub-control board.

causes.description: Wire harness connector loose between boards

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat all connectors
fixes.description: Check all multi-pin connectors at both boards.

fixes.title: Replace sub-board
fixes.description: Cheaper than main board to try first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If Er CO returns, check all connections.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both boards replaced and error persists, wiring harness between them has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an open circuit from the defrost temperature sensor on the evaporator. Without it, the board cannot tell when defrost is complete, so frost builds on the coils and cooling gradually weakens. A burned-out sensor is the most common cause; a loose connector comes next.

fixes.title: Manually defrost
fixes.description: Unplug, allow complete defrost.

fixes.title: Test sensor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~6k–8k ohms. At 32°F: ~16k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace sensor
fixes.description: Unclip from evaporator and replace.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Allow 24 hours for normal operation after repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If defrost sensor is fine and dS persists, defrost heater or control board defrost circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Heat error — oven failed to reach temperature in time
error_codes.detail: The oven did not reach the target temperature within the allowed preheat window (about 5 minutes to ~150°F), so the control aborts with F11. On gas ranges a weak or failed igniter is the usual culprit; on electric models a failed element or temperature sensor. A failing control board can also report F11.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. A transient F11 from a one-time slow ignition may clear.

fixes.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature the sensor should read ~1,080 ohms. Open or out-of-spec = replace.

fixes.title: Inspect the igniter (gas)
fixes.description: Watch ignition — a glow igniter that's dim/orange and slow to light the burner is failing.
fixes.warning: Allow oven to cool and shut off gas before servicing the igniter.

fixes.title: Inspect the element (electric)
fixes.description: Check the bake element for breaks or no continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off at the breaker for 5 minutes and retry a bake cycle. If F11 returns during preheat, test the sensor/igniter.
error_codes.models_affected: LG electric (LRE/LSE) and gas (LRG/LSG) ranges
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor and igniter/element all test good but F11 persists, the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor error — sensor or wiring fault
error_codes.detail: The control detected the oven temperature sensor reading out of range — typically shorted, open, or with a damaged wiring run. On some LG models a stuck control key can also raise F19. The oven won't heat safely until the sensor circuit reads correctly.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes, then retry.

fixes.title: Check for a stuck key
fixes.description: Press each control-panel button to confirm none is sticking.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature, ~1,080 ohms. Near 0 = shorted, OL = open. Replace if out of spec.
fixes.warning: Allow oven to cool before reaching inside.

fixes.title: Inspect the wiring
fixes.description: Trace the sensor harness for pinch points or chafing against the cavity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off 5 minutes, then test the sensor. After replacing it, confirm with a 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor and clean wiring don't resolve F19, the control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Hot Surface indicator — surface still hot (not a fault)
error_codes.detail: HS is an informational indicator, not an error. It warns that a cooking surface or the oven cavity is still hot enough to cause burns. It clears on its own once the surface cools.

causes.description: Surface still above the hot-to-touch threshold

fixes.title: Allow the surface to cool
fixes.description: Wait for the indicator to clear — keep hands and children away until it does.

error_codes.reset_instructions: No action needed. HS clears automatically once the surface cools.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: HS is a safety indicator, not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — unit lost power during operation (not a fault)
error_codes.detail: PF means the range experienced a power interruption — a brief outage, a tripped breaker, or a loose connection. On electric models the display turns back on showing PF so you know a cooking program was interrupted. It's a notice, not a hardware fault.

fixes.title: Clear the code
fixes.description: Press Clear/Off (or Cancel) to dismiss PF and resume normal use.

fixes.title: Check the breaker
fixes.description: If PF recurs, confirm the range's breaker isn't tripping and the terminal-block connections are tight.
fixes.warning: Unplug or shut off the breaker before checking the terminal block.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Clear/Off. PF clears once power is stable.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: PF is normally not a fault. Call a pro only if it recurs without any outage — a wiring or board issue may be present.

error_codes.meaning: Sabbath Mode active — controls intentionally disabled (not a fault)
error_codes.detail: Sb indicates Sabbath Mode is turned on. In this mode the oven keeps a set bake temperature with the display, tones, and most controls disabled for religious observance. It is not an error. Note: after a power failure the display returns in Sabbath mode with the oven off.

fixes.title: Exit Sabbath Mode
fixes.description: Press and hold the Sabbath/Clock controls per your model's manual until Sb clears (typically a 3-second hold).

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the Sabbath/Clock button combination to exit. Sb is not a fault.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Sb is a feature, not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor (resistance temperature detector) has failed with an open circuit. LG ovens will not heat without a functioning temperature sensor as temperature control becomes unsafe.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect the sensor and test resistance. LG oven sensors read approximately 1,080 ohms at room temperature (75°F). An open circuit (OL/∞) means sensor failure.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at both the sensor and the control board.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: The sensor is in the upper-back of the oven cavity, secured with 1–2 screws.
fixes.warning: Allow oven to cool completely before reaching inside.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, turn on and test with a 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: LG electric ranges (LRE/LSE series) and gas ranges (LRG/LSG series)
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor doesn't resolve F1, the control board temperature input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven over-temperature — exceeded maximum safe temperature
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature has exceeded the safe operating limit (typically 590°F during normal operation). The oven has shut down as a safety measure.

causes.description: Temperature sensor failure causing incorrect readings
Heating element relay stuck on control board

fixes.title: Power off immediately
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker. Allow oven to cool for 1 full hour.
fixes.warning: DO NOT open the oven door during overheating. Stand clear of the appliance.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor
fixes.description: After cooling, test sensor (should be ~1,080 ohms). If sensor is OK, the issue is a stuck relay.

fixes.title: Inspect heating elements
fixes.description: Check both bake and broil elements for visible signs of arcing or burn-through.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: If element failed, replace it. If relay is stuck on control board, board replacement is needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker immediately. Allow full cool-down before inspecting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F2 requires professional inspection. A stuck relay is a control board failure that must be repaired by a qualified technician.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor is reading abnormally low resistance (short circuit). This can cause the oven to incorrectly detect very high temperatures.

causes.description: Pinched wiring causing short to chassis

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temp, LG sensor should read ~1,080 ohms. Near 0 = shorted.

fixes.title: Inspect wiring
fixes.description: Check sensor wiring from probe to connector for any pinch points.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: A shorted sensor cannot be repaired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, test with 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor shows correct resistance but F3 persists, control board sensor input is shorted.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch failure during self-clean cycle
error_codes.detail: The door latch mechanism did not complete the lock or unlock operation during a self-clean cycle.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check door alignment
fixes.description: Door must close completely flush. Adjust door hinges if needed.

fixes.title: Test door latch assembly
fixes.description: The latch motor is at the back-top of the oven. Test the motor solenoid.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing.

fixes.title: Replace door latch assembly
fixes.description: If motor or switch failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F5 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new door latch assembly and F5 persists, control board door lock circuit failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock switch open — door latch not detecting locked position
error_codes.detail: The door latch is physically in the locked position but the switch is not confirming this to the control board.

causes.description: Door latch switch contacts worn or open

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Inspect door latch mechanism
fixes.description: Check for physical obstructions or misalignment.

fixes.title: Replace door latch/lock assembly
fixes.description: If switch contacts have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F6 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new latch assembly and F6 persists, control board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure — internal fault
error_codes.detail: The main electronic control board (ERC — Electronic Range Control) has experienced an internal fault.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 15 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: F9 almost always requires control board replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 15 minutes. If F9 returns, board must be replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement involves high-voltage connections. Professional installation is recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor error — over-temperature detected
error_codes.detail: The microwave's cavity temperature sensor has detected an over-temperature condition or has failed.

fixes.title: Check ventilation
fixes.description: Ensure the microwave has adequate clearance on all sides. Clean the grille and vent areas.

fixes.title: Allow to cool
fixes.description: Unplug for 20 minutes.

fixes.title: Test and replace sensor
fixes.description: If error returns after cooling, sensor has likely failed.
fixes.warning: DANGER: Microwave capacitors hold lethal charge. Only professionals should open microwave cases.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 20 minutes and ensure ventilation is clear. If F-3 returns, call a technician.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: ALWAYS call a professional for internal microwave repairs. Microwave capacitors retain lethal charge even when unplugged.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad/touchpad failure
error_codes.detail: A key on the microwave's touchpad membrane has failed or is being held continuously.

fixes.title: Clean touchpad
fixes.description: Wipe with a barely-damp cloth, dry completely.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 2 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace touchpad
fixes.description: Most common F-6 repair.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 2 minutes. Replace touchpad if F-6 persists after cleaning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F-6 persists, control board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted key on control panel
error_codes.detail: The control board has detected a short circuit on one of the touch keys, reading it as permanently pressed. The microwave will refuse input or behave erratically until the short clears. Moisture or grease that has worked under the membrane keypad is the most common cause, followed by membrane wear.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 2 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace control panel/touchpad
fixes.description: F-11 indicates membrane failure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 2 minutes. Replace touchpad if F-11 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F-11 persists, control board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low water fill detected — water inlet problem
error_codes.detail: The Whirlpool washer is not filling with enough water within the expected time. This indicates a water supply issue, a clogged inlet screen, or a failing water inlet valve. This is one of the more common Whirlpool front-load washer errors.

causes.description: Inlet hose screen clogged with sediment or debris

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Ensure both hot and cold supply valves behind the washer are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet hose screens
fixes.description: Disconnect the inlet hoses at the back of the washer. Inside each port are mesh screens — remove and clean them.

fixes.title: Check water pressure
fixes.description: A minimum of 20 PSI is required. Test by running a faucet while the washer fills.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: Disconnect wires from the valve solenoids and test continuity. Should read 200–500 ohms. Replace if open.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After resolving the water supply issue, press and hold the Start button for 3 seconds to clear and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good and inlet valve is new but error persists, the flow meter or pressure switch needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — cycle paused, water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Whirlpool washer attempted to drain but could not complete the drain within the expected time. Standing water will remain in the drum. The drain pump filter is the most common cause and takes only minutes to clean.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: Locate the filter behind the access panel on the lower front of the washer. Prepare towels and a shallow pan. Turn cap counterclockwise slowly — water will flow out. Remove and clean the filter.
fixes.warning: Have several towels ready — water will spill.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Verify the drain hose is not kinked and has a proper high loop. The hose should not be inserted more than 8 inches into the standpipe.

fixes.title: Check the household drain
fixes.description: Verify the standpipe or sink drain isn't blocked.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear but the washer doesn't drain, test the pump motor for continuity and replace if failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a Drain & Spin cycle. The F9E1 code clears once draining completes successfully.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump replacement doesn't resolve the issue, the main control board drain relay may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure — latch cannot engage
error_codes.detail: The door lock mechanism cannot properly engage or disengage. The washer will not start without a confirmed locked door, and will stop immediately if the lock fails mid-cycle.

causes.description: Worn door latch hook or strike plate
Laundry caught between door and gasket
Wiring harness to door lock damaged

fixes.title: Check for items in door seal
fixes.description: Look for clothing or small items lodged in the door gasket preventing full closure.

fixes.title: Inspect the door latch
fixes.description: Check the door latch (on the door) and the strike on the machine for wear or damage.

fixes.title: Test the door lock assembly
fixes.description: Remove the door lock assembly and test the solenoid for continuity.

fixes.title: Check wiring
fixes.description: Inspect wiring harness from control board to door lock for damage or loose connectors.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is fully closed and restart. If error persists, the door lock assembly needs inspection.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Door lock assembly replacement is a straightforward repair, but if the problem is a wiring harness issue, professional diagnosis is recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Main control board failure
error_codes.detail: The primary electronic control board has experienced an internal fault or component failure. This controls all functions of the washer. This error typically requires control board replacement.

causes.description: Voltage spike or power surge damaging control board
Failed component on control board (capacitor, relay)
Moisture or corrosion on control board

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10–15 minutes and restart. Some transient control board faults clear on power cycle.

fixes.title: Check for moisture
fixes.description: Inspect the control board area for signs of water intrusion or corrosion.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If the error persists after power cycling, the main PCB needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wire positions before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If F1E1 returns immediately, the board has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement is straightforward but if error returns on new board, there may be a power supply issue causing the failure.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds detected — too much detergent
error_codes.detail: The washer has detected excess foam. It will pause and wait for suds to reduce before resuming. Over-sudsing can damage the pump and bearings over time.

causes.description: Too much detergent — using more than manufacturer's minimum recommended dose
Non-HE detergent used in HE front-load washer
Detergent residue buildup in dispenser and drum

fixes.title: Wait for suds to clear
fixes.description: The washer will continue automatically once suds subside. This may take up to 30 minutes.

fixes.title: Run Rinse+Spin
fixes.description: If cycle doesn't resume, run a Rinse+Spin only cycle to clear suds.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent going forward
fixes.description: Use only HE-labeled detergent. Use no more than 1 tablespoon per load. Less is more with HE washers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: SUD clears automatically. No manual reset needed. If it recurs, reduce detergent amount significantly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD is not a mechanical fault. Persistent SUD with minimal detergent may indicate a water level sensor issue.

error_codes.meaning: Dispenser cannot dispense detergent or additive
error_codes.detail: The dispenser system (drawer or automatic dispenser) is unable to dispense laundry products as programmed. This may affect wash quality but does not stop the cycle on all models.

causes.description: Dispenser clogged with hardened detergent or fabric softener

fixes.title: Clean the dispenser drawer
fixes.description: Remove the dispenser drawer completely and clean thoroughly with warm water, removing all hardened detergent and fabric softener buildup.

fixes.title: Clean the dispenser housing
fixes.description: Use a small brush to clean the compartment where the drawer sits, including the spray nozzles.

fixes.title: Inspect dispenser actuator
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve it, the dispenser actuator motor or solenoid may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, restart the cycle. Run a Rinse+Spin to confirm the dispenser is now functioning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the dispenser is clean but still doesn't function, the dispenser solenoid or motor needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Motor speed error — motor not reaching target RPM
error_codes.detail: The washer motor is not spinning at the required speed. This prevents the spin cycle from reaching the target RPM, leaving clothes wetter than normal.

fixes.title: Reduce load and retry
fixes.description: An overloaded drum prevents the motor from reaching speed. Remove half the laundry.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Test motor and control board
fixes.description: If error persists, professional testing of motor control module is needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, reduce load, and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor and motor control board diagnosis requires professional testing if basic steps don't resolve the error.

error_codes.meaning: Inlet water supply problem — excessive water or suds overflow
error_codes.detail: The washer has detected a water supply issue. F0E1 specifically indicates overflow or excessive suds causing the control board to detect an over-water condition.

causes.description: Water inlet valve stuck open allowing overfill

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: Use HE detergent only, max 1 tablespoon. This is the cause in over half of F0E1 cases.

fixes.title: Check inlet valve
fixes.description: If no suds, test whether the inlet valve stays closed when not commanded.

fixes.title: Test pressure sensor
fixes.description: If water level seems fine, the sensor may be misreading.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing the cause, run Rinse+Spin to clear suds, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If overfill continues after inlet valve replacement, pressure sensor needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad disconnected or key panel failure
error_codes.detail: The main control board lost communication with the keypad/user interface panel, so button presses no longer register and the cycle cannot be started or changed. A loose wire harness connector between the UI panel and the main board is the most common root cause.

causes.description: Wire harness between UI and main board loose

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check wire harness connector
fixes.description: Reseat the connector between the UI panel and main board.

fixes.title: Replace UI board
fixes.description: If connector is secure but error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F2E1 returns, check wiring connections.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UI board replacement doesn't fix F2E1, main board communication port has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure sensor failure — water level sensing fault
error_codes.detail: The water level pressure sensor has failed. The washer cannot accurately detect fill level.

fixes.title: Check pressure hose
fixes.description: Inspect hose from tub to sensor for kinks or blockages.

fixes.title: Blow-test the sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect hose, gently blow into port — should hear click.

fixes.title: Replace pressure sensor
fixes.description: If sensor fails blow test.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and clear hose don't resolve F3E1, control board pressure circuit failed.

error_codes.meaning: Communication failure between main control board and motor control board
error_codes.detail: The main control board and motor control module cannot communicate. The washer will not spin or agitate.

causes.description: Wire harness between main and motor boards loose or damaged
Main control board communication port failure

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes.

fixes.title: Check motor board wire harness
fixes.description: Inspect and reseat the harness connecting main board to motor control board.

fixes.title: Replace motor control board
fixes.description: Usually cheaper to replace than main board.

fixes.title: Replace main control board
fixes.description: If motor board is new but error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F6E1 returns, check all board connections.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board-to-board communication failures can affect either board. Test motor board first.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure switch failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The pressure switch (water level sensor) has an open circuit — it is not making electrical contact in any position.

fixes.title: Inspect pressure hose
fixes.description: Check hose connection at both tub and switch.

fixes.title: Test switch
fixes.description: Should change continuity at different air pressure levels.

fixes.title: Replace pressure switch
fixes.description: If switch has open circuit in all positions.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new switch and clear hose don't resolve F3E2, control board pressure input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Flow meter error — water volume sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The flow meter measuring how much water has entered the drum has failed. Whirlpool uses this for precise water volume control.

causes.description: Flow meter impeller stuck or broken

fixes.title: Check flow meter for scale or debris
fixes.description: The flow meter is inline on the cold water inlet. Scale buildup can jam the impeller.

fixes.title: Test flow meter
fixes.description: Disconnect and check continuity of the sensor coil.

fixes.title: Replace flow meter
fixes.description: If impeller is stuck or electrical test fails.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If flow meter replaced and error persists, control board flow meter input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Control lock active — all buttons disabled
error_codes.detail: The control lock (child lock) feature is enabled. The display shows LOC and all buttons are disabled except Power.

fixes.title: Disable control lock
fixes.description: Press and hold the Control Lock button for 3 seconds. On some models, hold the End of Cycle button for 3 seconds.

fixes.title: Check your manual
fixes.description: If the above doesn't work, consult your model's manual for the specific button combination.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the Control Lock button for 3 seconds to unlock.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: LOC is not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Door switch failure — lid/door switch not actuating
error_codes.detail: The door switch that detects whether the door is closed has failed or is not making contact.

causes.description: Door switch contacts worn or burned

fixes.title: Check door closure
fixes.description: Make sure the door is fully closed.

fixes.title: Inspect door strike
fixes.description: The strike tab on the door that presses the switch may be broken.

fixes.title: Test door switch
fixes.description: Test switch continuity with door open and closed.

fixes.title: Replace door switch/lock assembly
fixes.description: If switch contacts have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is fully closed. Restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F5E1 on front-loads often means the door latch hook or catch needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock not engaging — lock timeout
error_codes.detail: The door lock solenoid has not completed the lock operation within the required time.

fixes.title: Inspect door latch alignment
fixes.description: Check that the door latch aligns with the strike in the machine.

fixes.title: Test lock solenoid
fixes.description: Apply voltage — should hear/feel the lock engage.

fixes.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixes.description: Straightforward replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door lock is new and F5E3 persists, the control board lock relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor control board failure
error_codes.detail: The motor control board (separate from the main control board on many Whirlpool models) has experienced an internal fault.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace motor control board
fixes.description: Located behind the drum or in the bottom cabinet.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wire connections before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If F1E2 returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new motor board returns F1E2 immediately, main board is sending incorrect commands.

error_codes.meaning: Main control board failure
error_codes.detail: The electronic control board has experienced an internal fault. This is the most searched Whirlpool dryer error code. The control board manages all functions — without it, nothing operates. SERP weakness for this query is 27/100 — a clear opportunity for a comprehensive written guide.

fixes.title: Check for a tripped circuit breaker
fixes.description: Before replacing the board, confirm the dryer is getting full 240V power. A partially tripped breaker can cause board errors.

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. Some transient board faults clear this way.

fixes.title: Inspect the control board visually
fixes.description: Access the board (usually behind the front or back panel). Look for burn marks, blown capacitors, or corrosion. If visible damage exists, replacement is needed.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: Remove the old board, photograph all wiring connections, and install new board in reverse order.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wire connections before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If F01 reappears immediately, the board has failed and needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F01 returns on a new board, there may be an underlying voltage problem (power surge, bad wiring) causing premature board failure.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The exhaust temperature sensor (thermistor) at the dryer outlet has failed or is reading incorrectly. The dryer cannot properly regulate heat without this sensor.

fixes.title: Locate the thermistor
fixes.description: The outlet thermistor is typically located in the exhaust duct inside the dryer cabinet.

fixes.title: Test resistance
fixes.description: At room temperature: approximately 10,000–12,000 ohms. Test with a multimeter.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect connector and remove sensor mounting screw. Install new sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug for 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor is new and error persists, the control board thermistor input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Restricted airflow — vent blockage
error_codes.detail: The dryer has detected insufficient airflow through the exhaust system. This is a safety shutdown — running with blocked airflow causes overheating and is a fire risk.

causes.description: Duct run too long or too many bends

fixes.title: Clean lint filter
fixes.description: Clean after every load.

fixes.title: Clean exhaust duct
fixes.description: Disconnect and clean full duct length with a dryer vent brush.

fixes.title: Clear exterior vent
fixes.description: Check exterior vent for lint, debris, or animal nesting.

fixes.title: Upgrade to rigid duct
