fixes.description: Replace flexible foil or plastic duct with rigid metal for any run over 3 feet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the duct, run a 20-minute Air Only cycle. The F30 code clears once adequate airflow is detected.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct is completely clear and code persists, the airflow sensor or high-limit thermostat may need replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor failure
error_codes.detail: The drum drive motor has failed. The drum will not rotate, clothes will not tumble, and the dryer cannot complete a cycle.

fixes.title: Test the drive motor
fixes.description: Access the motor (inside the drum cabinet). Test motor windings for continuity. No continuity = burned motor.

fixes.title: Check the thermal overload
fixes.description: Some motors have a thermal overload reset button. Press it and try restart.

fixes.title: Replace the drive motor
fixes.description: Remove the drum to access the motor. Disconnect wiring and drive belt. Install new motor.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before accessing motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After motor replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart. Error clears once motor runs successfully.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive motor replacement requires significant disassembly. If not experienced with appliance repair, professional service is recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Moisture sensor bar failure — primary sensor
error_codes.detail: The primary moisture sensor bars in the drum have failed. Auto-dry cycles will not work correctly.

causes.description: Sensor bars coated with fabric softener residue
Sensor bar corrosion or physical damage

fixes.title: Clean sensor bars
fixes.description: The bars are two metal strips inside the front of the drum. Clean with rubbing alcohol.

fixes.title: Test sensor bars
fixes.description: Check continuity between bars — should vary with moisture.

fixes.title: Replace sensor bar assembly
fixes.description: If bars are physically damaged or corroded.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, run Auto-Dry cycle to test.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If cleaning doesn't fix F28, sensor assembly replacement is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Moisture sensor bar failure — secondary sensor
error_codes.detail: The control detected an implausible reading from the secondary moisture sensor bars inside the drum, so auto-dry cycles can't judge when clothes are dry — loads stop damp or run far too long. Fabric softener residue coating the metal bars is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Clean sensor bars
fixes.description: Clean both sets of sensor bars with rubbing alcohol.

fixes.title: Replace sensor assembly
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, run Auto-Dry cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Sensor bar cleaning resolves F29 in most cases.

error_codes.meaning: Exhaust thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an open or shorted circuit from the thermistor that measures exhaust air temperature, so it cannot regulate heat safely and stops the cycle. Most often the thermistor itself has drifted out of spec or failed; a loose connector is the next suspect.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat at thermistor.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct inside cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board thermistor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low or missing voltage on one leg of 240V supply
error_codes.detail: Electric dryers run on 240V (two 120V legs). If one leg loses power, the motor runs (on 120V) but the heater does not (needs both legs). The dryer tumbles but produces no heat, and may display L2.

causes.description: Loose wire connection in dryer outlet or at panel

fixes.title: Reset the circuit breaker
fixes.description: Go to the panel and find the double-pole breaker for the dryer. Toggle it fully OFF then back ON.

fixes.title: Check the outlet voltage
fixes.description: With a multimeter on the outlet, between both hot terminals: should read ~240V. Each hot to neutral: ~120V. If one reads 0V, that leg has no power.

fixes.title: Have an electrician check the wiring
fixes.description: If the breaker reset and outlet checks don't resolve L2, the outlet wiring or breaker needs professional inspection.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reset the circuit breaker. L2 clears when full 240V is restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: L2 is almost always an electrical supply problem, not a dryer fault. An electrician should check the outlet and panel wiring if the breaker reset doesn't fix it.

error_codes.meaning: Restricted airflow — vent partially blocked
error_codes.detail: The dryer's airflow sensor or thermal system has detected reduced exhaust airflow. A partial vent blockage is causing inefficient drying and potential overheating.

causes.description: Exterior vent cap flap stuck closed or clogged

fixes.title: Clean lint filter
fixes.description: After every load.

fixes.title: Disconnect and clean exhaust duct
fixes.description: Full length cleaning with vent brush kit.

fixes.title: Check exterior vent
fixes.description: Clear any lint or debris from exterior vent cap.

fixes.title: Replace flexible duct with rigid metal
fixes.description: For any run over 3 feet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing duct, run Air Only cycle for 20 minutes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If duct is clear and AF persists, the airflow sensor may have failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher drain pump is not draining water within the expected time. Standing water in the tub indicates this error.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or improperly routed

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the cylindrical filter in the bottom of the tub. Rinse and replace.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure the drain hose has a high loop and is not kinked.

fixes.title: Check disposal knockout
fixes.description: If connected to disposal, confirm knockout plug is removed.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter is clean, test the pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, press Cancel then Start to run a new cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails continuity test, replace it. Persistent errors after replacement point to a control board relay fault.

error_codes.meaning: Wash heater or heating element failure
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher is unable to heat water to the target wash temperature. Dishes will not be sanitized and drying performance will suffer.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Access from under the dishwasher. Disconnect wires and test continuity — should read 15–30 ohms.

fixes.title: Test the NTC temperature sensor
fixes.description: Resistance at room temp should be approximately 6k ohms.

fixes.title: Check control board
fixes.description: If both element and sensor are good, the relay on the control board controlling the heater has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run a test cycle. Error clears after a successful heated wash cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If heating element replacement doesn't resolve F6E4, the control board needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad error — control panel not communicating
error_codes.detail: The main control board has lost communication with the keypad/user interface, so button presses don't register and cycles can't be selected or started. A loose wire harness connector behind the door panel is the most common root cause, followed by a failed UI board.

causes.description: Wire harness connector loose between UI and main board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reseat UI connector
fixes.description: Check and firmly reseat the connector at the UI panel.

fixes.title: Replace UI board
fixes.description: If connector is secure but error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F2E2 returns, replace UI board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UI board replacement doesn't fix F2E2, main board UI port failed.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — low resistance reading
error_codes.detail: The NTC temperature sensor is reading a resistance that indicates it has shorted.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Should read ~6k ohms at room temperature. Near 0 ohms = shorted.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Simple replacement near heating element.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel and let the 2-minute drain finish, then unplug the dishwasher (or flip the breaker) for 60 seconds. After replacing the thermistor, run a normal cycle to confirm F3E1 stays clear.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor shows F3E1, control board thermistor input is shorted.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — high resistance / open circuit
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an open circuit or impossibly high resistance from the NTC temperature sensor, so it cannot verify water temperature and may skip heated wash or sani phases. A failed sensor or a loose connector at the thermistor is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Should read ~6k ohms at room temp. Infinite = open circuit.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat firmly.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Install new sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel and allow the 2-minute drain to complete, then cut power at the breaker for 60 seconds. After reseating the connector or fitting a new thermistor, run a normal cycle to verify the code is gone.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, wiring harness to sensor has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Water heating failure — not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The control commanded the heater but water temperature never reached the programmed target within the allowed time. Dishes come out poorly washed and the cycle runs long or aborts. A burned-out heating element is the most common root cause, followed by a bad thermistor.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Test continuity — 15–30 ohms expected.

fixes.title: Test NTC sensor
fixes.description: ~6k ohms at room temp.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run full program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and sensor good, control board heater relay failed.

error_codes.meaning: Circulation pump motor error
error_codes.detail: The main wash circulation pump motor has failed or is drawing incorrect current.

fixes.title: Check for debris
fixes.description: Remove filter and check pump impeller area for hard debris.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — glass may be present.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace circulation pump
fixes.description: If motor has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel and let the 2-minute drain run, then unplug or switch off the breaker for 60 seconds. Remove the filter, clear any debris around the pump impeller, and start a short cycle to test.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement is complex. Professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Calibration needed — sensor recalibration required
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher's sensors need to be recalibrated. This typically appears after a power outage or when settings have been reset.

fixes.title: Let the calibration cycle finish
fixes.description: CAL is a status display, not a fault. Run the dishwasher empty and let the calibration complete uninterrupted — typically 10–20 minutes.

fixes.title: Power cycle if stuck
fixes.description: If CAL stays on the display for more than an hour, switch off power at the breaker for 60 seconds, then run an empty Heavy cycle to recalibrate.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run a heavy cycle empty. CAL clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: CAL is not a fault. No parts needed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board / thermistor stuck — electronic control fault
error_codes.detail: The main control board has detected a stuck relay or a thermistor reading that is electronically impossible, indicating an internal control fault. The dishwasher will not run cycles reliably.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off power at the breaker for 60 seconds, then restore.

fixes.title: Check thermistor wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the thermistor harness for shorts or pinched wires to the board.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If F1E1 returns, the main control board has failed.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power at the breaker for 60 seconds. If F1E1 persists, replace the control board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F1E1 after a power cycle indicates the control board must be replaced.

error_codes.meaning: User interface stuck key — button held or touchpad fault
error_codes.detail: The control detected a stuck key on the user interface — a button reading as pressed for too long. This is usually a stuck membrane button or a failed UI board.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off power at the breaker for 60 seconds.

fixes.title: Press each button
fixes.description: Work each button to free a stuck key, and dry any moisture behind the panel.

fixes.title: Replace the UI board
fixes.description: If a button stays stuck, replace the user interface board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power 60 seconds. Work each button. If F2E1 persists, replace the UI board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new UI board and F2E1 persists, the main board UI port has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door switch fault — control cannot confirm door state
error_codes.detail: The door switch (door latch switch) is not reporting the correct open/closed state to the control, so the dishwasher will not start or stops mid-cycle. A failed latch switch or its wiring is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Check the door latches firmly
fixes.description: Ensure the door clicks shut and the latch engages.

fixes.title: Test the door switch
fixes.description: Test continuity of the door latch switch as the door is latched.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: If the switch has failed.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cut power 60 seconds. Check the door latches firmly. Replace latch switch if F5E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch switch is good and F5E1 persists, the control board door input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door will not latch / lock — latch mechanism fault
error_codes.detail: The control cannot confirm the door has latched and locked. The dishwasher will not begin a cycle because it cannot verify the door is secure.

causes.description: Door latch mechanism stuck or broken

fixes.title: Check for obstructions
fixes.description: Make sure no rack, dish, or item blocks the door from latching.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch mechanism
fixes.description: Look for a broken or sticking latch.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: If the latch is broken.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear obstructions and cut power 60 seconds. Replace the latch if F5E2 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch is sound and F5E2 persists, the control board latch input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: No water detected — inlet / fill problem
error_codes.detail: The control did not detect water entering the tub within the expected time, so it aborts to avoid running dry. A closed supply valve, clogged inlet valve, or kinked fill line is the usual cause.

causes.description: Water inlet valve clogged or failed
Supply shutoff closed or low pressure

fixes.title: Check the water supply
fixes.description: Confirm the under-sink supply valve is fully open and the fill line is not kinked.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet valve screen
fixes.description: Shut off water, disconnect the fill line, and clean the inlet valve screen.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply first.

fixes.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: If the valve is clogged or not opening.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power and water before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Confirm supply valve open, clean the inlet screen, cut power 60 seconds. Replace inlet valve if F6E3 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water supply and inlet valve are good and F6E3 persists, the flow meter or control has failed.

error_codes.meaning: No / low water fill — slow or insufficient fill
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher is filling too slowly or not reaching the required water level within the allowed time. Low supply pressure, a partially clogged inlet valve, or a float/flow-meter fault are the common causes.

causes.description: Water inlet valve partially clogged or weak

fixes.title: Check water pressure and supply
fixes.description: Confirm the supply valve is fully open and pressure is adequate.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet valve screen
fixes.description: Shut off water and clean the inlet valve inlet screen.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply first.

fixes.title: Replace the water inlet valve
fixes.description: If the valve is weak or clogged.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power and water before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Confirm supply pressure, clean the inlet screen, cut power 60 seconds. Replace inlet valve if F8E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply and valve are good and F8E1 persists, the flow meter or control has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Diverter motor fault — diverter stuck or not detected
error_codes.detail: The diverter motor, which directs spray pressure between the upper and lower wash arms, is not operating correctly or its position cannot be detected. Wash performance suffers because spray is not distributed properly.

fixes.title: Clean the sump and diverter area
fixes.description: Remove the filter and clear debris around the diverter disk.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — glass may be present.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Cut power at the breaker for 60 seconds.

fixes.title: Replace the diverter motor
fixes.description: If the diverter does not actuate.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the diverter area and cut power 60 seconds. Replace the diverter motor if F9E2 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the diverter is clean and F9E2 persists, the diverter motor or control has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan motor in the freezer has stopped functioning. This fan circulates cold air from the evaporator to both the freezer and refrigerator sections. Without it, temperatures will rise throughout the refrigerator.

fixes.title: Check for ice blockage
fixes.description: Frost buildup is very common. Unplug and let defrost completely before replacing the motor.

fixes.title: Access the evaporator fan
fixes.description: Remove freezer back panel. Fan is mounted on a bracket above or beside the evaporator coils.

fixes.title: Test the motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC to test. If motor doesn't spin, replace it.

fixes.title: Replace the motor
fixes.description: Disconnect wiring, unscrew bracket, swap motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow 24 hours for temperature stabilization. Code clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice accumulates again within weeks, the defrost heater is likely failing.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The condenser fan (near the compressor at the bottom rear of the refrigerator) has stopped running. The compressor will overheat and shut down, causing the entire refrigerator to stop cooling.

causes.description: Debris or pet hair blocking fan

fixes.title: Clean the condenser area
fixes.description: Remove rear access panel. Vacuum out dust and pet hair from condenser fan and coils.

fixes.title: Spin fan blade by hand
fixes.description: Should rotate freely. Stiff = bearing failure.

fixes.title: Replace condenser fan motor
fixes.description: Disconnect wiring, unscrew from bracket, install new motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow several hours for temperature recovery. Error clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If motor is clean and free but doesn't run with voltage applied, replace it. If new motor still shows error, control board relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The temperature sensor in the freezer compartment has failed. The control board cannot accurately read freezer temperature, which may cause over-cooling or under-cooling.

fixes.title: Locate the freezer thermistor
fixes.description: Usually clipped to the evaporator coil or mounted inside the freezer compartment.

fixes.title: Test resistance
fixes.description: Should read approximately 5k–16k ohms depending on temperature.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Clip off old sensor, install new one at same location.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 5 minutes then restart. Code clears after normal temperature cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor tests good and code persists, the control board sensor input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted keypad — stuck key on control panel
error_codes.detail: A key on the oven's control panel membrane has been stuck for 60 continuous seconds. This is the most common oven control error on Whirlpool ranges.

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe around all buttons with a barely-damp cloth. Dry completely.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Many F2E0 errors clear after power cycling.

fixes.title: Replace keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F2E0 returns after cleaning and cycling, replace the membrane keypad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Wipe panel. If F2E0 persists, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F2E0 persists, the Electronic Range Control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad/touchpad disconnected from control board
error_codes.detail: The ribbon cable or connector between the keypad and the Electronic Range Control board has lost continuity.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check the ribbon cable
fixes.description: The ribbon cable connects the touchpad to the ERC board, typically running behind the control panel. Reseat both connectors.

fixes.title: Replace keypad if cable is integral
fixes.description: On most models the keypad and cable are one assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Check ribbon cable connections.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new keypad and cable and F2E1 persists, ERC board connector port failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor (RTD probe) has an open circuit. The oven will not heat safely without temperature feedback.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature, Whirlpool oven sensors read approximately 1,080–1,090 ohms. Open circuit = failed sensor.

fixes.title: Check connector at sensor
fixes.description: Reseat the 2-pin connector.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Located in the upper-back of the oven cavity.
fixes.warning: Let oven cool completely.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, turn on and test with a 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool, KitchenAid, Maytag ovens sharing the same control platform
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and F3E0 persists, ERC board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor is reading near-zero resistance, indicating a short circuit. This can cause the oven to detect falsely high temperatures.

causes.description: Wiring pinched against chassis causing short

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Room temp should read ~1,080 ohms. Near 0 = shorted.

fixes.title: Inspect wiring
fixes.description: Look for any pinch points along the sensor wiring.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: A shorted sensor cannot be repaired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, test with 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor is shorted too, wiring harness has a short to ground.

error_codes.meaning: Oven over-temperature — temp exceeded safe limit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature exceeded the maximum safe limit during normal cooking (typically above 590°F/310°C).

causes.description: Temperature sensor failure causing runaway heating
Bake relay stuck on control board

fixes.title: Turn off circuit breaker immediately
fixes.description: Allow oven to cool completely — 1 full hour minimum.
fixes.warning: Do not open oven door. Stand clear.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor after cooling
fixes.description: Sensor should read ~1,080 ohms. Failed sensor can cause runaway heating.

fixes.title: Replace sensor or control board
fixes.description: If sensor is good, the heating relay on the ERC board is stuck closed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker immediately. Do not use oven until diagnosed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F3E2 is a safety event. Professional diagnosis required before further use.

error_codes.meaning: Door switch failure — latch not sensing door position
error_codes.detail: The door switch is not detecting the door position correctly during a self-clean cycle or normal operation.

fixes.title: Check door closes flush
fixes.description: The door must close completely and evenly.

fixes.title: Test door switch
fixes.description: The switch is typically near the door latch. Test continuity with door open and closed.

fixes.title: Replace door switch
fixes.description: If contacts have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. Check door alignment and switch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door switch replaced and F5E0 persists, the ERC board door switch input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Self-clean door latch failure — lock timeout
error_codes.detail: The door latch motor did not complete the lock or unlock operation within the required time during a self-clean cycle.

causes.description: Door latch mechanism stuck or jammed

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check latch for obstructions
fixes.description: Food debris can jam the latch mechanism.

fixes.title: Test and replace latch assembly
fixes.description: The latch motor is at the top-rear of the range.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing latch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F5E1 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new door latch and F5E1 persists, ERC board latch relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch during lock function — latch system fault
error_codes.detail: The control commanded the door latch to lock (usually for self-clean) but never received confirmation that the latch reached the locked position, so the cycle is locked out. A failed latch motor or a misaligned latch switch is the most common root cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check door alignment and latch
fixes.description: Ensure door closes completely.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F9E0 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new latch and F9E0 persists, ERC board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: EEPROM communication error — control board memory fault
error_codes.detail: The Electronic Range Control (ERC) board cannot communicate with its onboard EEPROM memory chip. The control's stored settings and calibration data are unreadable.

causes.description: EEPROM chip or control board failure

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power. A surge-induced F1E0 often clears.

fixes.title: Check incoming voltage
fixes.description: Verify a stable 240V supply. Brownouts can corrupt EEPROM comms.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F1E0 returns, the control board EEPROM has failed and the board must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1E0 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.models_affected: Whirlpool, KitchenAid, Maytag ovens sharing the same ERC platform
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If power cycling does not clear F1E0, the control board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: EEPROM checksum error — corrupted control memory
error_codes.detail: The data stored in the ERC board's EEPROM failed its checksum verification, meaning the stored calibration or configuration data has been corrupted.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. A one-time corruption can clear on reboot.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F1E1 returns, the EEPROM data cannot be restored in the field and the board must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1E1 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F1E1 means corrupted control memory — replace the ERC board.

error_codes.meaning: UL A/D error — microprocessor analog-to-digital fault
error_codes.detail: The control board's microprocessor is unable to perform analog-to-digital conversions correctly (the 'UL' or upper-limit watchdog circuit). The board cannot reliably read its sensor inputs.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: An A/D conversion fault is internal to the control board and not field-repairable.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1E2 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F1E2 is an internal control fault — replace the ERC board.

error_codes.meaning: Model ID error — control not configured for this model
error_codes.detail: The ERC board's stored model identification does not match the appliance, or the model ID resistor/jumper has been read incorrectly. The control cannot apply the correct operating parameters.

causes.description: Replacement board not configured for this model

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Verify correct replacement board
fixes.description: If F1E4 appeared after a board swap, confirm the part number matches your exact model.

fixes.title: Replace with correct ERC board
fixes.description: Install a board configured for your model number.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Verify the control board matches your model.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the correct board is installed and F1E4 persists, the model ID circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Calibration shifted — stored temperature calibration out of range
error_codes.detail: The oven's stored temperature calibration values have shifted outside the acceptable range stored in the control's EEPROM, so the control can no longer trust its temperature calibration.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Re-run oven temperature calibration
fixes.description: If your model supports user calibration offset, reset it to 0 and re-test.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F1E5 returns, the calibration data is unrecoverable.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Reset calibration offset if persistent.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F1E5 after power cycling indicates the control board must be replaced.

error_codes.meaning: Latch signal mismatch — door latch feedback disagrees with control
error_codes.detail: The door-latch position signal received by the control does not match the commanded latch state. The control sees conflicting information about whether the self-clean latch is locked.

causes.description: Door latch switch failure or misalignment
Wiring between latch and board damaged

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch assembly
fixes.description: Check that the latch moves freely and its position switch actuates correctly.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: If the latch switch feedback is faulty.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing latch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F1E6 returns, inspect and replace the door latch.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new latch and F1E6 persists, the control board latch input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Cancel key drive line open — control panel fault
error_codes.detail: The Cancel key drive line on the control panel is open, so the control cannot scan the Cancel/Off key. This typically points to a touchpad or ribbon-cable fault.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Reconnect the touchpad ribbon cable to the control board.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F2E5 persists, the membrane keypad has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. Reseat ribbon cable. If F2E5 persists, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new touchpad and F2E5 persists, the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Cancel key drive line open — control panel fault (variant)
error_codes.detail: Like F2E5, the Cancel key drive line is open and the control cannot read the Cancel/Off key. The fault is in the touchpad, ribbon cable, or control key-scan circuit.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Reconnect the touchpad ribbon cable to the control board.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F2E6 persists, replace the membrane keypad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. Reseat ribbon cable. If F2E6 persists, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new touchpad and F2E6 persists, the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Meat probe defect — temperature probe fault
error_codes.detail: The control detected a fault on the meat/temperature probe circuit — typically a shorted probe or probe jack. The probe reading is outside the valid range.

causes.description: Probe left plugged in or wiring fault

fixes.title: Remove the meat probe
fixes.description: Unplug the probe from the oven jack. If the error clears, the probe is faulty.

fixes.title: Inspect the probe jack
fixes.description: Check the receptacle inside the cavity for damage or food debris.

fixes.title: Replace the meat probe
fixes.description: Use a replacement probe matched to your model.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the meat probe. Power cycle 5 minutes. Replace probe if F4E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new probe and F4E1 persists, the probe jack or control input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Return line not connected — control wiring fault
error_codes.detail: The control board's keypad return (scan) line is not connected, so the control cannot complete its key-scan matrix. This usually indicates a disconnected ribbon cable or control wiring fault.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat all control connectors
fixes.description: Reconnect the keypad ribbon cable and verify control board harness connections.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If the wiring is sound and F6E0 persists, the control board has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Reseat ribbon cable and control connectors.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If all connections are secure and F6E0 persists, replace the control board.

error_codes.meaning: Common switch wire defective — function key fault
error_codes.detail: The common (shared) switch wire serving the function keys is defective, so the control reads a stuck or shorted function key. The fault is in the touchpad or its wiring.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat and inspect the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Look for cracks or damage in the touchpad ribbon cable.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F7E1 persists, the membrane keypad common wire has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F7E1 persists, replace the touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new touchpad and F7E1 persists, the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: A two-digit F0 indicates a general Electronic Range Control board fault detected during self-diagnostics. On older Whirlpool ERC controls this is a catch-all control failure code.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes, then restore power.

fixes.title: Reseat control connectors
fixes.description: Check the control board harness connections.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F0 persists after power cycling, replace the control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F0 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F0 indicates the control board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: A two-digit F1 indicates a general control failure detected by the Electronic Range Control. On controls that report sub-codes, this corresponds to the F1-E# family of EEPROM/microprocessor faults.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat control connectors
fixes.description: Check the control board harness connections.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F1 persists, the control board has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F1 indicates the control board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Control failure — door/latch circuit
error_codes.detail: A two-digit F5 indicates a control failure associated with the door switch and self-clean latch circuit. On controls that report sub-codes this maps to the F5-E# door/latch family.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check door and latch
fixes.description: Ensure the door closes fully and the latch moves freely.

fixes.title: Replace door latch or switch
fixes.description: If the latch or door switch has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing latch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. Check door and latch operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the latch and switch are good and F5 persists, the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Function key stuck or shorted
error_codes.detail: A two-digit F7 indicates a stuck or shorted function key on the control panel. The control detects a key held closed and cannot return to normal operation.

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe around all buttons with a barely-damp cloth and dry completely.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad/touchpad
fixes.description: If F7 returns, the membrane keypad has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean panel, power cycle 5 minutes. If F7 persists, replace touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new touchpad and F7 persists, the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: A two-digit F8 indicates a control board failure, often an EEPROM or analog reference fault detected during self-diagnostics. On sub-code controls this maps to internal control faults.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check incoming voltage
fixes.description: Verify a stable 240V supply.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC board
fixes.description: If F8 persists, the control board has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F8 persists, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F8 indicates the control board needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heater open circuit — heating element has failed
error_codes.detail: The wash water heater has an open circuit, meaning the heating element is burned through. The washer can still run cold water cycles, but hot and warm water settings will not work, and some sanitize cycles will be disabled.

causes.description: Heater wiring harness damaged or disconnected

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect the two wires from the heater. Test with a multimeter for continuity. A good heater reads approximately 20–30 ohms. Open circuit = failed heater.

fixes.title: Check wiring to heater
fixes.description: Inspect both wires leading to the heater for damage, burned insulation, or loose connections.

fixes.title: Replace the heating element
fixes.description: The element is typically accessible from under the tub. Follow service manual for your specific model.
fixes.warning: Unplug and fully drain the washer before accessing the heater.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the heating element, unplug for 5 minutes and restart. The code clears after a successful heated cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating element access requires significant disassembly. Professional service is recommended if you're not experienced.

error_codes.meaning: Water temperature too low
error_codes.detail: The water entering the washer is below the expected temperature for the selected cycle. This can indicate a water heater issue in the home, a cross-connected supply hose, or a failing water temperature sensor.

causes.description: Home water heater set too low or needs time to deliver hot water
Hot and cold supply hoses connected in reverse
Water inlet valve not opening hot side

fixes.title: Check hot water supply
fixes.description: Run the hot water faucet at a nearby sink and feel the temperature. If cold, the home water heater has an issue.

fixes.title: Verify hose connections
fixes.description: Make sure the hot hose (usually red coded) connects to the hot supply valve and hot port on the washer.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: The thermistor reads incoming water temperature. Test resistance and compare to spec.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing the supply issue or replacing the sensor, restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hot water is confirmed good at the tap but E61 persists, the NTC sensor or inlet valve hot side needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heater relay failure on main control board
error_codes.detail: The relay on the main control board that switches power to the heating element has failed. The element may be physically fine, but the board cannot send power to it.

causes.description: Heater relay stuck open on control board

fixes.title: Confirm heater element is good
fixes.description: Test element first (see E64) to confirm the element itself is not the issue before replacing the board.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: If element tests OK, the board relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 15 minutes and restart. If E65 returns with a known-good heater element, the board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement for GE washers is complex. Professional diagnosis is recommended to confirm the board and not the element is the cause.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor (water temperature sensor) has an open circuit and is not providing temperature readings to the control board. The washer's heating function is disabled.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect and test resistance with multimeter. Room temperature reading should be approximately 6k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat connector firmly at the thermistor mounting.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Install new thermistor and ensure it's properly clipped to the heater or drum housing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If thermistor is new and tests correctly but error persists, control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Spin speed error — motor not reaching target RPM
error_codes.detail: The washer motor is not spinning the drum at the commanded speed. Clothes will exit the cycle still wet.

causes.description: Drum overloaded preventing motor from reaching speed

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items and retry with a smaller load.

fixes.title: Check drive belt
fixes.description: On belt-driven models, inspect the belt for wear, stretching, or breakage.

fixes.title: Inspect motor brushes
fixes.description: On brushed DC motors, worn brushes cause speed loss. Replace if worn below 1/4 inch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run a Spin Only cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If belt and brushes are fine and error persists, motor control board testing is required.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board detected that the drive motor failed to run or drew excessive current when commanded. The drum will not tumble or spin and the cycle halts. Worn motor windings or a broken drive belt letting the motor race are the most common root causes.

fixes.title: Check drive belt
fixes.description: Inspect for breakage or slipping.

fixes.title: Test motor windings
fixes.description: Test continuity of motor coils.

fixes.title: Replace motor
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to reset the control, then plug back in and run a short spin cycle. If E43 returns as soon as the drum tries to turn, the motor or motor control board has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor replacement requires significant disassembly.

error_codes.meaning: Motor speed sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board stopped receiving speed feedback from the motor tachometer, so it cannot regulate drum speed. The washer may agitate erratically or refuse to spin. A failed tach sensor on the motor or a chafed sensor wire is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Test speed sensor
fixes.description: Check resistance per service manual.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes to clear the code, then restart on a spin cycle. If E44 reappears once the drum starts turning, test and replace the motor speed sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and error persists, control board input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The control board commanded the door lock to engage or release but never received confirmation from the lock switch. The cycle cannot start, or the door stays locked after it ends. A worn latch or failed lock solenoid is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Inspect door latch
fixes.description: Check for wear or cracks.

fixes.title: Test lock solenoid
fixes.description: Test continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but E47 persists, lock solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The GE dishwasher cannot drain water within the expected time. Standing water remains in the tub.

causes.description: Drain filter clogged with food particles
Drain hose kinked or improperly high-looped

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter
fixes.description: Remove the filter at the bottom of the tub (twist counterclockwise) and rinse clean.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Verify the hose runs up to the counter underside before dropping to the drain.

fixes.title: Check the disposal
fixes.description: Run the disposal and confirm the connection point is clear.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If all above clear, test pump motor for operation.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run a rinse cycle to confirm drain.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump is running but water doesn't drain, the pump impeller may be broken requiring pump replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Flood switch activated — water in base tray
error_codes.detail: The flood protection switch has detected water in the base of the dishwasher. The unit will not run until the water is cleared and the leak source is fixed.

fixes.title: Tilt unit to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt the dishwasher forward (with power off) to let base water drain.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at breaker before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check entire perimeter of door gasket for damage.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect supply line and drain hose connections inside the unit.

fixes.title: Check detergent amount
fixes.description: Use only dishwasher-specific detergent, 1 tablespoon max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base tray, fix leak source, and restart. Code clears once sensor is dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak is visible, the tub itself may be cracked, requiring professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Wash motor failure — circulation pump not running
error_codes.detail: The main circulation pump motor has failed or is drawing incorrect current. The dishwasher cannot perform a wash cycle.

causes.description: Foreign object jammed in pump impeller

fixes.title: Check for debris in pump
fixes.description: Remove filter and reach into sump to clear any debris from impeller.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — broken glass may be present.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace circulation pump
fixes.description: If motor is burned, full pump assembly replacement is needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing jams, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement requires significant disassembly. Professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — cycle interrupted by power outage
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher detected a power interruption during a cycle. This is not a fault — it is an informational code. The cycle needs to be restarted.

causes.description: Power outage or interruption during cycle

fixes.title: Restart the cycle
fixes.description: Press Start to resume or select a new cycle.

fixes.title: Check circuit breaker
fixes.description: If power interruptions are frequent, check the dishwasher's dedicated circuit.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start to begin a new cycle. PF clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: PF does not require service unless power interruptions are frequent — in that case, check household wiring.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — not draining properly
error_codes.detail: The control board did not see the water level drop during the drain period — the pump ran but water stayed in the tub, leaving standing water after the cycle. A clogged drain filter or a kinked or improperly looped drain hose is the most common root cause.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or improperly routed

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the filter in the tub bottom.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure high-loop installation and no kinks.

fixes.title: Check disposal
fixes.description: Run disposal before cycle.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose clear, test pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, cancel and restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor tested failed, replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: The main control board failed an internal self-check or stopped responding, so the dishwasher may go unresponsive or halt mid-cycle. Internal component failure on the board, often triggered by a power surge or moisture intrusion, is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If C3 returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Professional board replacement recommended to avoid wiring errors.

error_codes.meaning: OWI sensor failure — optical water indicator fault
error_codes.detail: The optical sensor that detects water clarity (how dirty the wash water is) has failed. The dishwasher may run shorter cycles than needed.

causes.description: OWI sensor lens dirty or coated

fixes.title: Clean the OWI sensor
fixes.description: Located on the sump assembly. Clean the lens with a soft cloth.

fixes.title: Test and replace OWI sensor
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning or replacing, run a normal wash cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: C7 rarely causes complete failure — dishwasher still washes, just uses default cycle length.

error_codes.meaning: Water not hot enough — incoming water temperature too low
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher detected that the incoming water temperature is below the minimum needed for effective washing.

causes.description: Home water heater temperature set too low (below 120°F/49°C)
Hot water run too long before cycle — hot water depleted

fixes.title: Run hot water at nearby sink
fixes.description: Run the kitchen hot water faucet for 30–60 seconds before starting the dishwasher. This clears the cold water in the supply line.

fixes.title: Check water heater temperature
fixes.description: Set to at least 120°F (49°C).

fixes.title: Test NTC sensor
fixes.description: If water is hot at tap but HO persists, test the sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run hot water at sink first, then restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water heater is hot and sensor replaced but HO persists, the inlet valve hot side is not opening.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure sensor fault — water level sensing issue
error_codes.detail: The control board is receiving implausible or no readings from the water level pressure sensor, so it cannot verify the fill level. Fills may be wrong or the cycle may halt. A failed sensor or a blocked pressure hose from the sump is the usual cause.

causes.description: Air trap or hose to sensor blocked

fixes.title: Check air trap and hose
fixes.description: The pressure hose from the sump to the sensor should be clear.

fixes.title: Test and replace sensor
fixes.description: If hose is clear, test sensor and replace if failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run a test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and clear hose don't resolve PrS, control board pressure input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost sensor issue — freezer not defrosting correctly
error_codes.detail: The defrost sensor (which monitors the evaporator temperature during defrost cycles) has failed or is reading incorrectly. This can lead to frost buildup on the evaporator coils and eventual cooling failure.

fixes.title: Defrost the refrigerator manually
fixes.description: Unplug and allow complete defrost (24–48 hours). Check if code clears after full defrost.

fixes.title: Locate and test the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Behind the freezer back panel, on or near the evaporator. Test resistance.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Unclip from evaporator and install replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, allow 24 hours for normal operation to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If defrost sensor is new and error continues, the defrost heater or defrost timer/board needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer compartment temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor measuring freezer temperature has failed. Without accurate freezer temperature data, the control board cannot regulate compressor operation correctly.

fixes.title: Test thermistor resistance
fixes.description: In a 32°F/0°C environment: approx 16k ohms.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: The connector is often the issue — unplug and firmly reseat.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Install new sensor in same clip location.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 5 minutes and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both sensors fail simultaneously, the control board or wiring harness has a common fault.

error_codes.meaning: Demo/showroom mode active — cooling is disabled
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has been placed in showroom display mode. All lights and controls work, but the compressor and cooling system are off. Food will spoil in this mode.

causes.description: Demo/display mode activated, often after a power interruption or self-test
Display board or main board communication fault keeping the mode stuck

fixes.title: Exit demo mode
fixes.description: On most GE models: press and hold 'Lock' and 'Ice Maker' simultaneously for 3 seconds. Or hold 'Freezer' and 'Refrigerator' temp buttons for 6 seconds.

fixes.title: Check your model's manual
fixes.description: If the standard combination doesn't work, consult the manual for your specific model number.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Follow the demo mode exit procedure for your model. The display will return to actual temperatures.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If demo mode cannot be exited with standard button combinations, the control board may have a software issue.

error_codes.meaning: Fresh food compartment temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor measuring refrigerator (fresh food) compartment temperature has failed.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~6k–8k ohms.

fixes.title: Reseat connector
fixes.description: Firmly reconnect.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located inside refrigerator compartment.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow 24 hours temperature stabilization.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board detected an open or out-of-range signal from the evaporator coil temperature sensor, so defrost timing can no longer be regulated accurately. Frost may build up and cooling suffer. A failed clip-on sensor or heavy frost encasing it is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Manually defrost
fixes.description: Unplug and let frost clear before diagnosing.

fixes.title: Test sensor
fixes.description: ~16k ohms at 32°F.

fixes.title: Replace sensor
fixes.description: Clip-on replacement to evaporator coil.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, restore power, and allow 24 hours for temperatures to stabilize. If 4E returns after the reset, test and replace the evaporator sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If frost builds up again quickly, defrost heater is failing.

error_codes.meaning: Humidity/ambient sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board is getting no valid signal from the humidity or ambient temperature sensor, so it cannot fine-tune defrost cycles and anti-condensation heaters. Cooling usually continues normally. A failed sensor or loose connector is the typical cause.

fixes.title: Test sensor
fixes.description: Low-priority repair. Check connector first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, then restore power. If 6E reappears within a day, reseat the sensor connector and replace the sensor — cooling is usually unaffected meanwhile.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: 6E rarely causes cooling failure.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor open — temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The dryer thermistor (temperature sensor) has an open circuit and is not providing temperature readings.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms expected.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat at thermistor.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct area.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, unplug 1 minute and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor shorted — exhaust temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The outlet thermistor is reading an abnormally low resistance, indicating a short circuit.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Should read 10k+ ohms at room temp. Near 0 = shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute to clear the code, then restart. If E3 returns immediately, the outlet thermistor is still shorted — replace it before running another load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor shows E3, control board thermistor circuit shorted.

error_codes.meaning: High-limit thermostat tripped — overheating detected
error_codes.detail: The high-limit safety thermostat has tripped due to excessive heat. A blocked vent is the most common cause.

causes.description: High-limit thermostat failed permanently after trip

fixes.title: Clean vent system
fixes.description: Full duct cleaning — filter, duct, exterior cap.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: After cleaning, test for continuity. Tripped = open = replace.

fixes.title: Run after vent cleaning
fixes.description: If thermostat tests good after vent cleaning, restart normally.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean vent, replace thermostat if failed, unplug 5 minutes, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If vent is clear and thermostat replaced but E4 returns, heating element relay is stuck closed.

error_codes.meaning: Inlet thermistor failure — intake temperature sensor
error_codes.detail: The air intake thermistor has failed. This sensor monitors incoming air temperature.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k ohms.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute, then restart a timed-dry cycle. If E8 returns, the inlet thermistor reading is still out of range — replace the sensor before further use.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Exhaust thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The exhaust air thermistor is returning an open or out-of-range signal, so the control cannot monitor exhaust temperature. The dryer may cut heat or stop mid-cycle to protect itself. A failed thermistor in the exhaust path is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust path.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 1 minute and restart. If E9 reappears when the cycle begins, replace the exhaust thermistor — don't keep running the dryer with a failed exhaust sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor relay failure on control board
error_codes.detail: The control board detected that the drum motor relay on the main board is stuck or no longer switching, so the motor cannot be reliably started or stopped. A burned relay contact on the board, often following a power surge, is the most common root cause.

causes.description: Motor relay burned on control board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: E10 almost always requires board replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If E10 returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement for GE dryers is a direct swap. Photograph all wiring.

error_codes.meaning: Lint screen reminder
error_codes.detail: This is a maintenance reminder to check and clean the lint screen. Not an error.

causes.description: Lint screen cleaning reminder interval reached
Lint screen or housing genuinely clogged

fixes.title: Clean lint screen
fixes.description: Remove the screen, clear all lint, and wash with warm soapy water to remove fabric-softener film. Dry fully before reinstalling.

fixes.title: Vacuum the screen housing
fixes.description: Use a vacuum crevice tool in the lint screen slot to pull out lint that has fallen past the screen into the housing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean lint screen. LS clears after screen is cleaned.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad stuck — a button is continuously pressed
error_codes.detail: A button on the oven's touch control pad has been continuously pressed for over 60 seconds. This is one of the most common GE oven errors.

fixes.title: Clean the keypad
fixes.description: Wipe around all control pad buttons with a barely-damp cloth. Dry completely before restoring power.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 5 minutes. F0 clears on many GE ovens after power cycling.

fixes.title: Replace the control panel/membrane
fixes.description: If F0 returns after cleaning and cycling, the membrane has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Wipe panel clean. If F0 returns, replace touchpad.
error_codes.models_affected: GE Profile, GE Café, and standard GE electric and gas ranges
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F0 persists, the ERC (Electronic Range Control) board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven over-temperature — exceeded 590°F in bake or 990°F in self-clean
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor detected a temperature above the safe maximum. The oven has locked out for safety.

causes.description: Temperature sensor failure (reporting falsely high temp)
Heating element relay stuck closed on ERC board

fixes.title: Turn off circuit breaker immediately
fixes.description: Allow oven to cool completely for 1 hour minimum.
fixes.warning: Do NOT open oven door. Stand clear. If you smell burning, evacuate and call the fire department.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor after cooling
fixes.description: GE oven sensors read ~1,080 ohms at room temp. Check for open circuit or short.

fixes.title: Replace sensor if failed
fixes.description: If sensor tests abnormal, replace it.

fixes.title: If sensor tests OK — replace ERC board
fixes.description: The heating relay is stuck closed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker immediately. Do not use oven until cause is identified.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F2 is a safety event. Professional diagnosis required. A stuck relay requires ERC board replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor has an open circuit and is not providing temperature readings to the control board.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temp, GE oven sensor should read ~1,080 ohms. Open circuit (OL/∞) = sensor failed.

fixes.title: Reseat connector
fixes.description: Check and firmly reseat the sensor connector at the sensor and at the control board.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Located in the upper-back of the oven cavity.
fixes.warning: Allow oven to cool completely before reaching inside.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, restore power and test with a 350°F bake cycle.
error_codes.models_affected: GE, GE Profile, Hotpoint, and RCA electric ranges sharing the GE platform
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and F3 persists, the ERC board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor is reading near-zero resistance, indicating a short circuit.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: At room temp, sensor should read ~1,080 ohms. Near 0 = shorted.

fixes.title: Inspect sensor wiring
fixes.description: Check for any kinks or pinch points.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Shorted sensors cannot be repaired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, restore power and test.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor and F4 persists, ERC board sensor input is shorted.

error_codes.meaning: Touchpad/membrane key button fault
error_codes.detail: A specific key on the control panel touchpad is stuck or has a fault. Similar to F0 but F7 may indicate the fault started from a key other than the first scanned one.

fixes.title: Clean the touchpad
fixes.description: Wipe carefully around all touch buttons.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace touchpad/membrane
fixes.description: If F7 returns after cleaning.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Clean and inspect touchpad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new touchpad and F7 persists, ERC board failed.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic Range Control internal fault
error_codes.detail: The ERC (Electronic Range Control) board has experienced an internal logic fault.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 15 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace ERC board
fixes.description: F8 almost always requires board replacement.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 15 minutes. If F8 returns, board must be replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: ERC board replacement involves high-voltage wiring. Professional installation recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch failure — self-clean lock not completing
error_codes.detail: The door latch motor did not complete the lock or unlock operation within the required time during a self-clean cycle.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check latch for food debris
fixes.description: Food can jam the latch mechanism. Clean around latch area.

fixes.title: Test and replace door latch assembly
fixes.description: Located at the top-rear of the oven.
fixes.warning: Unplug oven before accessing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle 5 minutes. If F9 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new door latch assembly and F9 persists, ERC board door lock relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — water not draining within expected time
error_codes.detail: The Bosch washing machine cannot drain water within the allowed time. This halts the cycle and leaves water in the drum.

fixes.title: Clean the drain pump filter
fixes.description: The filter is behind the lower-front service flap. Turn cap slowly to drain water, remove and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready — significant water will drain.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Inspect hose for kinks from back of machine to standpipe.

fixes.title: Check standpipe
fixes.description: Pour water directly into standpipe. If it backs up, clear the household drain.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter is clear and hose is good, pump motor may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, press the pause/cancel button to reset, then restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump is replaced and E18 persists, control board drain relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure — cannot lock door for cycle
error_codes.detail: The washer door lock mechanism cannot engage. The washer will not start without a confirmed locked door.

fixes.title: Check door seal for caught items
fixes.description: Pull back the rubber gasket seal and inspect for trapped clothing.

fixes.title: Inspect door latch
fixes.description: Look for cracked or worn plastic on door latch mechanism.

fixes.title: Test door lock solenoid
fixes.description: Disconnect and test solenoid coil for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is fully closed and locked. Restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes and clicks but E02 persists, door lock solenoid replacement is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake fault — fill time exceeded
error_codes.detail: The Bosch washer did not reach the required water level within the expected fill time. Water supply issues are almost always the cause.

causes.description: Inlet hose kinked or screen blocked

fixes.title: Open supply valves fully
fixes.description: Confirm both hot and cold valves are completely open.

fixes.title: Clean hose screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses at machine connection and clean mesh screens.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity — 200–500 ohms expected.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply issue and press Start to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists after inlet valve replacement, the pressure switch is likely faulty.

error_codes.meaning: Anti-flood device activated — water detected in base
error_codes.detail: The Bosch AquaStop system has detected water in the base tray of the washing machine. This is a safety shutdown.

fixes.title: Tilt machine to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt machine forward to drain water from base tray. Power off first.
fixes.warning: Turn off water and power before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect supply hose fittings
fixes.description: Check hose connections at wall and at machine for any weeping.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for tears or detachment.

fixes.title: Inspect internal hoses
fixes.description: Access back panel and check internal hose connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak, allow to dry. Code clears when float sensor resets.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no external leak is visible, internal tub or pump housing crack needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Leak protection activated — water detected in base tray
error_codes.detail: The AquaStop anti-flood system has detected water in the base tray of the Bosch dishwasher. This is one of the most searched Bosch dishwasher error codes. The dishwasher will pump water out continuously and lock itself until the issue is resolved.

causes.description: Overfilled with water — door seal worn or torn
Sump or pump housing crack allowing water into base
Condensation or steam accumulation triggering float sensor
Internal hose connection loose or cracked
Using too much detergent causing foam overflow

fixes.title: Tilt the dishwasher to drain the base
fixes.description: With power off at the breaker, carefully tilt the dishwasher forward about 45 degrees. Water will drain from the base pan. Place towels down. Let it sit tilted for 1–2 minutes.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before tilting. The unit is heavy — get assistance.

fixes.title: Allow the base to dry
fixes.description: After draining, leave the dishwasher upright with the door open for several hours to allow any remaining moisture to evaporate from the base.

fixes.title: Check the door seal
fixes.description: Run your fingers along the full perimeter of the rubber door gasket. Look for cracks, tears, or sections that have pulled away from the channel. Replace if damaged.

fixes.title: Check for oversudsing
fixes.description: If you used too much detergent, suds overflow into the base. Use only recommended dishwasher detergent — 1 tablespoon maximum.

fixes.title: Inspect internal hose connections
fixes.description: If the above don't apply, access the inside of the door panel and check the water inlet, circulation, and drain hoses for cracks or loose connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Tilt the unit to drain the base, allow it to dry completely, then restart. If the leak has been resolved, the float sensor will reset and the E15 code will clear.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If E15 recurs after drying and the door seal is intact, there is an internal water leak (cracked sump, hose, or pump) requiring professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Drain blocked — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The Bosch dishwasher cannot drain water. This is a very common error often caused by a blocked drain filter or a kinked drain hose. It's usually a quick DIY fix.

causes.description: Clogged drain filter and mesh screen
Garbage disposal connection blocked or knockout not removed
Drain pump impeller blocked by glass or debris

fixes.title: Clean the drain filter system
fixes.description: Remove the bottom rack. Unscrew the coarse filter (twist counterclockwise), then lift out the flat fine mesh filter. Rinse both thoroughly under running water using a brush.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Pull the dishwasher out slightly and trace the drain hose. It should loop up to the underside of the counter and not be kinked.

fixes.title: Check garbage disposal
fixes.description: Run the disposal before starting the dishwasher. If newly installed, check that the knockout plug was removed.

fixes.title: Check pump impeller
fixes.description: If filter is clean and hose is clear, reach into the sump and feel around the pump opening for broken glass or hard debris blocking the impeller.
fixes.warning: Use gloves — broken glass may be present.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, press the On/Off button for 3 seconds to reset, then run a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear but the pump still doesn't drain (you can hear it trying), the pump motor has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump or filter blocked — drain impeller obstructed
