error_codes.detail: The drain pump filter or pump impeller is physically blocked by debris. This is a more specific version of E24 pointing to the pump or filter area rather than the hose.

causes.description: Hard debris (bone, toothpick, glass) blocking pump impeller
Accumulated food debris in filter basket

fixes.title: Clean filter and remove impeller debris
fixes.description: Follow the same steps as E24. Pay particular attention to clearing the pump impeller area.
fixes.warning: Use gloves when reaching into the pump area.

fixes.title: Check pump impeller rotation
fixes.description: After clearing, reach in and try to spin the pump impeller. It should rotate freely.

fixes.title: Replace drain pump if impeller is damaged
fixes.description: If impeller is broken or pump motor is seized, replace the pump assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing debris, press On/Off for 3 seconds to reset then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If drain pump is replaced and E25 persists, there is a control board drain relay fault.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element or NTC sensor failure — water not heating
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher cannot heat water to the target temperature. The heating element or the temperature sensor monitoring it has failed. Dishes will not be properly sanitized.

causes.description: Heater relay on control board failed

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Access from under the dishwasher. Disconnect element terminals. Should read 15–25 ohms. Open circuit = element failure.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: At room temperature: approximately 6k–7k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install new element or sensor as needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run a test cycle on a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and sensor both test OK, the control board heater relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Heating circuit failure — heater not functioning
error_codes.detail: The heating circuit has an electrical fault. Similar to E01 but specifically indicates a relay or circuit board issue rather than the element itself.

causes.description: Heater relay failure on main PCB

fixes.title: Test heating element first
fixes.description: Rule out element failure before replacing the board.

fixes.title: Check wiring to heater
fixes.description: Inspect element wiring for burn marks or melted insulation.

fixes.title: Replace control board or relay
fixes.description: If element is good, the PCB heater control circuit has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, restart on a short test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement is complex. Professional diagnosis recommended to confirm it's the board and not a wiring issue.

error_codes.meaning: Water shortage — insufficient water entering dishwasher
error_codes.detail: The control did not detect the water level rising as expected during fill, so the cycle pauses or aborts to keep the circulation pump from running dry. A partially closed supply valve under the sink or a kinked inlet hose is the most common root cause.

causes.description: Water supply valve under sink closed or partially closed
Inlet mesh filter blocked with scale

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Inspect inlet hose
fixes.description: Check for kinks from the wall to the dishwasher.

fixes.title: Clean inlet mesh filter
fixes.description: Unscrew the hose from the dishwasher and clean the small mesh filter inside the inlet port.

fixes.title: Test and replace inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid for continuity. Should read 200–500 ohms.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore water supply and press Start to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water supply is confirmed good and error persists, inlet valve replacement is needed.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure — oven not heating
error_codes.detail: The bake or broil heating element has failed. The oven will not heat up, or heating will be uneven.

causes.description: Heating element burned through (visible break or blistering)
Element wiring harness connection burned or loose

fixes.title: Inspect heating element visually
fixes.description: Open the oven and look at the bake element (bottom) and broil element (top). A burned-through element will have a visible break, hole, or blistered area.

fixes.title: Test element continuity
fixes.description: After allowing oven to cool, disconnect element terminals and test with multimeter. Good element reads 15–30 ohms. Open circuit = failed.

fixes.title: Replace the element
fixes.description: Unscrew the mounting screws, pull the element forward, disconnect the wires, and connect the new element.
fixes.warning: Allow oven to cool completely and disconnect power at the breaker.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, turn power back on and test with a short bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the element looks intact and tests with correct resistance but the oven still won't heat, the control board relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor failure — NTC sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor (NTC probe) has failed. Without accurate temperature readings, the oven cannot regulate heat properly — it may overheat or not heat at all.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe burned out or open circuit
Probe connector loose at control board
Wiring from probe to board damaged

fixes.title: Test the temperature probe
fixes.description: The probe is usually a small pointed sensor inside the oven cavity. Test resistance at room temperature — should read approximately 1,000–1,100 ohms.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: The probe connects to the control board via a 2-wire connector. Ensure it is firmly seated.

fixes.title: Replace the probe
fixes.description: Unscrew from the oven back wall and replace with a new probe.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the probe, run the oven on a short bake cycle. The error should not return.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the probe is new and tests correctly but E111 persists, the control board's sensor input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Safety cutout tripped — oven overheated
error_codes.detail: The oven exceeded its maximum safe operating temperature and the thermal safety cutout has tripped. This is a critical safety code. Do not continue using the oven until the underlying cause is found.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe failure causing control board to keep heating
Cooling fan failure causing heat buildup

fixes.title: Do not use the oven
fixes.description: Stop all oven use until this is diagnosed.
fixes.warning: SAFETY CRITICAL — do not operate the oven until diagnosed.

fixes.title: Allow oven to cool completely
fixes.description: Wait at least 2 hours before any inspection.

fixes.title: Test the temperature probe
fixes.description: A failed probe can cause the control board to keep heating without limit. Test resistance (1,000–1,100 ohms at room temp).

fixes.title: Check the cooling fan
fixes.description: Ovens have a cooling fan. Listen for it running when the oven is on. No sound = failed fan.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Do not reset and continue using. This code requires diagnosing the underlying overheating cause before operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: E132 is a safety event requiring professional service before further oven use. The root cause must be identified to prevent recurrence.

error_codes.meaning: Low water supply — insufficient fill detected
error_codes.detail: KitchenAid washers share the Whirlpool platform. F8E1 indicates the washer is not detecting adequate water fill within the timeout period. Supply valve, inlet screens, and water pressure are the first things to check.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both valves behind washer are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoids for continuity (200–500 ohms).

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply issue, press and hold Start for 3 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is good and valve is new, flow meter needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The KitchenAid washer cannot drain within the expected time. The pump filter is almost always the culprit.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Behind lower access panel. Drain water, remove and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure hose is not kinked and has proper high loop.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear, test pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run Drain & Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pump replacement needed if motor tests failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The door lock cannot engage or disengage properly. The washer will not operate with an unlocked door.

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Look for laundry caught in gasket.

fixes.title: Inspect latch
fixes.description: Check for cracked plastic on latch mechanism.

fixes.title: Test lock solenoid
fixes.description: Test for continuity with multimeter.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Ensure door is firmly latched and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but error persists, lock assembly needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds detected
error_codes.detail: The washer has detected excessive foam in the drum and paused the cycle to let the suds break down — it may add rinse water to help. The cycle runs much longer than normal but resumes on its own. Using too much detergent, or non-HE detergent in an HE machine, is the cause in nearly every case.

fixes.title: Wait for suds to clear
fixes.description: Washer will resume automatically within 30 minutes.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: Use HE detergent only; max 1 tablespoon per load.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clears automatically. No reset needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a mechanical fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump failure — dishwasher not draining
error_codes.detail: The drain pump motor has failed or is blocked. Water remains standing in the tub.

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean filter assembly under the bottom rack.

fixes.title: Check drain hose routing
fixes.description: Confirm high-loop installation.

fixes.title: Clear pump impeller
fixes.description: Remove filter and check pump opening for debris.
fixes.warning: Use gloves.

fixes.title: Replace drain pump
fixes.description: If motor is failed, replace pump assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, cancel and restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor tested failed, replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Stuck relay on control board
error_codes.detail: A relay on the main control board is stuck in a closed or open position. This usually requires control board replacement.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If error persists, board relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If error returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement requires professional service if complex wiring is involved.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — water temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor measuring wash water temperature has failed. Heating function disabled.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Resistance at room temp: ~6k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Simple sensor replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off power at the breaker for 60 seconds (most KitchenAid dishwashers are hardwired), then restore power. After replacing the thermistor, run a normal cycle and verify the water heats without 1-1 returning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan in the freezer has stopped. Cold air cannot circulate to either compartment.

fixes.title: Defrost the freezer
fixes.description: Unplug and fully defrost before replacing motor.

fixes.title: Test fan motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC — if no spin, motor failed.

fixes.title: Replace motor
fixes.description: Access behind freezer back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug refrigerator first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow 24 hours temperature stabilization.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice recurs quickly, defrost heater is failing.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The condenser fan at the back/bottom of the refrigerator has failed. Compressor will overheat.

fixes.title: Clean condenser area
fixes.description: Vacuum out dust from fan and coils.

fixes.title: Test fan
fixes.description: Spin by hand — should be free. Test with 12VDC.

fixes.title: Replace motor
fixes.description: Remove access panel, swap motor assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow several hours for temperature recovery.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If clean and free but no spin, motor failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Frigidaire washer cannot drain water within the programmed time. The pump filter is the most likely cause.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access filter at front lower panel. Drain water, remove and rinse filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure hose has high loop and is not kinked.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: Test motor continuity if filter is clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails continuity test, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Fill time exceeded — water not filling
error_codes.detail: The pressure sensor did not register the target water level before the fill timer expired, so the control paused the cycle to avoid running dry. A closed or partially open supply valve, or sediment-clogged inlet screens, are the most common causes.

fixes.title: Open supply valves
fixes.description: Check both hot and cold valves are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh filters.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Door open — lid or door not properly closed
error_codes.detail: The washer cannot detect a closed and locked door. Cycle will not start or will pause.

fixes.title: Check for items in seal
fixes.description: Look for clothing caught in the door gasket.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push until you hear a definite click.

fixes.title: Inspect and replace latch
fixes.description: If latch is worn, replace assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door is closed but E41 persists, lock solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock control board failure
error_codes.detail: The door lock control board has failed. This requires replacing either the door lock assembly or the main control board.

causes.description: Main control board door lock relay

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Test door lock assembly
fixes.description: If lock tests failed, replace assembly.

fixes.title: Replace control board if needed
fixes.description: If door lock tests OK, main board door lock relay has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. If error returns, door lock or board replacement needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both door lock and board are replaced and error continues, wiring harness has a fault.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: The electronic control board has experienced an internal fault. This is the main PCB that controls all dryer operations.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If error returns, board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If E11 returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement is straightforward but requires photographing all wire positions.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure
error_codes.detail: The control detected an open circuit in the heating element, so the drum turns but no heat is produced and clothes stay wet. An element coil burned through from age or from restricted exhaust airflow is by far the most common root cause.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect wires, test continuity. Should read 10–15 ohms.

fixes.title: Replace element
fixes.description: Access element from back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

fixes.title: Check board relay
fixes.description: If element OK, board heater relay failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element replaced and no heat, control board relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Drain problem — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Frigidaire dishwasher cannot drain water. Standing water remains in the tub.

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the filter in the dishwasher bottom.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm high loop and no kinks.

fixes.title: Check disposal
fixes.description: Run disposal before cycle.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose clear, test pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, cancel and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Leak detected in base
error_codes.detail: Water has entered the base pan of the dishwasher, triggering the flood protection switch.

fixes.title: Drain base by tilting
fixes.description: Tilt dishwasher forward (power off) to drain base water.
fixes.warning: Power off before tilting.

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Inspect gasket for tears.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect all internal hose fittings.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: Use only dishwasher detergent, 1 tbsp max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak, dry, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no visible leak found, internal crack needs professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Water supply problem — insufficient fill
error_codes.detail: The control did not detect the expected water level within the fill window, so the cycle pauses rather than run the wash pump dry. Dishes come out dirty or the cycle stalls early. A closed supply valve or kinked inlet hose is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is open.

fixes.title: Check hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to unit.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply confirmed good.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Frigidaire dishwashers are often hardwired — switch off the breaker for 5 minutes to reset the control, restore power, then start a new cycle after confirming the supply valve is fully open.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply good and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — cycle interrupted
error_codes.detail: Power to the dishwasher was interrupted mid-cycle, and the control displays PF so you know the load may not have finished. PF is a notice, not a fault — a brief outage or a tripped breaker is the cause, and the dishes may need rewashing.

fixes.title: Clear the code and restart
fixes.description: Press Start/Cancel to clear PF, then start a new cycle to finish the interrupted load.

fixes.title: Check household power if recurring
fixes.description: If PF appears often without known outages, check the breaker and the dishwasher's junction-box connections for looseness.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start. PF clears automatically.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Not a fault. No service required.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature alarm — refrigerator or freezer above safe temperature
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has detected that compartment temperature is above the safe threshold. Food safety may be compromised if this has persisted for more than 2 hours.

causes.description: Door left open or not sealing properly

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Press paper between door and frame. If it pulls out easily, the seal is worn.

fixes.title: Clean condenser coils
fixes.description: Vacuum the condenser coils behind or under the unit.

fixes.title: Check fans
fixes.description: Listen for both the condenser fan (back/bottom) and evaporator fan (inside freezer) running.

fixes.title: Allow time to recover
fixes.description: If door was accidentally left open, close it and wait 4 hours.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly. H1 clears when temperature returns to normal range.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fans are running, coils are clean, and door seals are good but temperature stays high, the compressor or refrigerant needs professional inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan motor failure
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan has stopped functioning. Without it, cold air cannot circulate and temperatures will rise.

fixes.title: Check for ice blockage
fixes.description: Unplug and fully defrost the freezer before diagnosing the motor.

fixes.title: Test fan motor
fixes.description: Apply 12VDC — if no spin, motor has failed.

fixes.title: Replace fan motor
fixes.description: Access behind freezer back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Allow 24 hours after repair for temperature stabilization.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice accumulates again within weeks, defrost heater is failing.

error_codes.meaning: Runaway temperature — oven overheating
error_codes.detail: The oven has detected a runaway temperature condition, meaning it is heating beyond the safe range. This is a critical safety error. Stop using the oven immediately.

causes.description: Oven temperature probe failure causing uncontrolled heating

fixes.title: Stop oven use immediately
fixes.description: Do not use the oven until the fault is diagnosed.
fixes.warning: FIRE HAZARD — stop oven use immediately.

fixes.title: Test the oven temperature probe
fixes.description: The probe should read ~1,100 ohms at room temperature. Out of spec = replace.

fixes.title: Replace control board if probe is OK
fixes.description: If probe tests fine, the board's temperature control relay has stuck.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Do not reset and continue using. Diagnose the underlying cause first.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F10 is a safety event. Professional diagnosis required before returning the oven to service.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature probe failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor (probe) has failed with an open circuit. The oven cannot safely regulate temperature.

fixes.title: Test probe resistance
fixes.description: Should read ~1,100 ohms at room temperature.

fixes.title: Replace probe
fixes.description: Unscrew from oven back wall and install new probe.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing probe, run oven on short bake cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If probe is new and tests correctly, control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted key — button stuck or shorted
error_codes.detail: A key on the oven control panel is registering as continuously pressed. This prevents normal operation.

causes.description: Stuck button from grease or moisture

fixes.title: Clean control panel
fixes.description: Clean around all buttons with dry cloth. Check for sticky residue.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace keypad
fixes.description: If a button remains stuck, replace the membrane keypad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F11 returns on the same key, that key is shorted.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If keypad replacement doesn't resolve F11, the control board has a shorted keypad input.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature probe short circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature probe is reading near-zero resistance, indicating an internal short. This causes the oven to see a falsely high temperature and prevents safe heating operation.

causes.description: Wiring pinched causing short to chassis

fixes.title: Test the probe
fixes.description: Disconnect the probe from the control board. At room temperature, it should read approximately 1,100 ohms. Near 0 ohms = shorted probe.

fixes.title: Inspect probe wiring
fixes.description: Look for pinch points where the wiring passes through the oven cavity. Even slight pressure against the chassis can cause a short.

fixes.title: Replace the temperature probe
fixes.description: A shorted probe cannot be repaired. The probe threads into the upper-rear oven wall — typically 1–2 screws. Pull 2–4 inches of wire slack through and connect the new probe.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing probe, run a short 350°F bake cycle to confirm F31 is resolved.
error_codes.models_affected: Frigidaire FFEF/FFES/FFGF/FGGF series ranges
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If probe measures correctly and F31 persists after replacement, the control board sensor input has an internal short.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch failure — lock motor did not reach locked position
error_codes.detail: The door latch motor failed to reach the locked position within the required time. This error appears when starting a self-clean cycle or when the latch sensor does not confirm the lock.

causes.description: Door latch mechanism jammed by food debris

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the range for 10 minutes, then try again. A transient F90 from a timing glitch clears with a power cycle.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch mechanism
fixes.description: Open the oven door and look at the top of the oven cavity. The latch slides into a slot in the oven frame. Clear any debris or baked-on food from the latch slot.

fixes.title: Check door closes flush
fixes.description: If the oven door has slight warping or the hinges are worn, the door may not seat tightly enough for the latch to travel fully. Test: with the door closed, press it from all four edges — it should not flex.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: The latch assembly is mounted at the top-rear of the oven. Remove the back service panel (typically 4 screws) and disconnect the latch wiring harness. Install the new assembly.
fixes.warning: Unplug the range before accessing any internal components.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F90 returns, inspect latch for debris, then replace latch assembly.
error_codes.models_affected: Frigidaire gallery and professional series ranges with self-clean
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door latch assembly is new and F90 persists, the control board latch motor relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch failure — lock motor did not reach unlocked position
error_codes.detail: The door latch motor ran but failed to fully retract to the unlocked position after a self-clean cycle completed. The oven door may be stuck locked after self-clean.

causes.description: Latch mechanism binding after high self-clean temperature

fixes.title: Let oven cool completely
fixes.description: If self-clean just finished, the oven may still be above the latch-unlock threshold (typically 375°F / 190°C). Wait 1–2 hours and the door may unlock on its own.

fixes.title: Power cycle after cooling
fixes.description: With oven cool, turn off circuit breaker for 10 minutes. On some models this resets the latch state machine and unlocks the door.

fixes.title: Manual latch release
fixes.description: On most Frigidaire ranges, the latch has a manual release: remove the top rear panel and use a screwdriver to manually slide the latch to the unlocked position. Refer to the service manual for your model number.
fixes.warning: Range must be cool and unplugged before attempting manual latch release.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: If the latch motor failed mid-cycle, replace the complete latch assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Let oven cool fully. Unplug 10 minutes. If door remains locked, use manual latch release.
error_codes.models_affected: Frigidaire gallery and professional series ranges with self-clean
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door remains locked after cooling, power cycling, and manual release attempt, call a professional — do not attempt to force the door.

error_codes.meaning: Control board internal checksum error
error_codes.detail: The Electronic Range Control (ERC) board failed its internal memory self-check. The board's EEPROM or program memory has been corrupted, possibly by a power surge.

causes.description: Loose connection on board power supply

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 15 minutes. On rare occasions, a transient F13 from a brown-out clears with a full power cycle.

fixes.title: Check wiring connections to board
fixes.description: Disconnect all ribbon cables and wiring harnesses from the ERC board, then reseat them. Loose connections can cause erratic board behavior.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC control board
fixes.description: F13 is almost always a board failure. The ERC board sits behind the control panel — remove the back service panel or the front control panel depending on the model.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 15 minutes. If F13 returns, ERC board must be replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement involves handling multiple high-voltage connections. Professional installation recommended if you are not comfortable with appliance electronics.

error_codes.meaning: EEPROM error — control board memory fault
error_codes.detail: A variant of the board memory fault. The ERC board has detected an error in its non-volatile storage (EEPROM) that holds calibration and configuration data.

causes.description: Intermittent power causing incomplete write cycles

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 15 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC control board
fixes.description: F14 is a board-level EEPROM fault and cannot be repaired by resetting alone.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 15 minutes. If F14 returns, replace ERC board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Replace the ERC board. This is not a DIY repair unless you are comfortable with oven electronics.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — water not draining
error_codes.detail: Maytag washers share the Whirlpool platform. F9E1 means the machine cannot drain within the expected time. Pump filter clog is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Clean drain filter
fixes.description: Access via lower front panel. Drain water and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm no kinks and proper high loop.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear, test pump motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing blockage, run Drain & Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pump replacement needed if motor tests failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low water fill detected
error_codes.detail: The control opened the inlet valve but the pressure sensor never registered enough water within the allowed fill time, so the cycle pauses or aborts. A partly closed supply valve or clogged inlet hose screens are the most common causes — both fixable without parts.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both hot and cold fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoids for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix supply issue and hold Start for 3 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply good and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Main electronic control board failure
error_codes.detail: The primary control board failed its internal self-check, so the washer halts and won't run cycles reliably. A failed onboard component (often a relay or capacitor) is the usual root cause, frequently triggered by a power surge or moisture reaching the board.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If error returns, board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If error returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement requires photographing all wiring before disconnecting.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds — too much detergent
error_codes.detail: The washer detected excess foam — usually via drag on the drum or pressure-sensor readings — and paused the cycle to run a suds-reduction routine. Using too much detergent, or any non-HE detergent in this high-efficiency machine, is the cause in nearly every case.

fixes.title: Wait
fixes.description: Washer resumes automatically within 30 minutes.

fixes.title: Reduce detergent
fixes.description: HE detergent only, 1 tbsp max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Wait — the washer runs a suds-reduction rinse and resumes on its own, usually within 30 minutes. To clear immediately, press Cancel, run a Rinse & Spin with no detergent, then restart the load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD is almost never a service issue. Call a pro only if the code appears on every load with correct HE detergent dosing — that points to a failing pressure sensor or a drain restriction trapping suds.

error_codes.meaning: Lid will not lock — latch blocked or failed
error_codes.detail: On these Whirlpool VMW-platform washers, F5E2 means the lid lock could not engage, usually because something is blocking the latch or the lid lock assembly has failed. The washer will not spin until the lid locks.

fixes.title: Clear the latch area
fixes.description: Make sure nothing blocks the lid from closing fully and the strike enters the lock cleanly.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute, then restore power.

fixes.title: Replace the lid lock assembly
fixes.description: If the lock will not engage.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the latch, unplug 1 minute. Replace the lid lock if F5E2 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new lid lock and F5E2 persists, the control board lock output has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Lid lock fault — cannot lock or unlock
error_codes.detail: The washer cannot confirm the lid is locked or unlocked. Most often the lid lock, lid switch, or control panel is at fault, or the lid is not fully closed.

fixes.title: Check the lid closes fully
fixes.description: Ensure no laundry or object blocks the lid.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Replace the lid lock assembly
fixes.description: If the lock or switch has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the lid fully, unplug 1 minute. Replace the lid lock if F5E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new lid lock and F5E1 persists, the control board lock circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Lid will not unlock — latch or control fault
error_codes.detail: The washer cannot unlock the lid after a cycle, typically due to a stuck door latch or a control board fault. The lid stays locked and the machine cannot complete.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1–2 minutes to release the lock, then restore power.

fixes.title: Inspect the lock mechanism
fixes.description: Look for a jammed or broken latch.

fixes.title: Replace the lid lock assembly
fixes.description: If the lock will not release.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 1–2 minutes to release the lock. Replace the lid lock if F5E3 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new lid lock and F5E3 persists, the control board unlock circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor / basket speed sensor fault — drive system
error_codes.detail: The control cannot read the motor or basket speed correctly. On top-load VMW washers this often involves the shifter (mode shifter actuator) that engages wash versus spin, or the motor speed sensor.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Check the shifter / drive coupling
fixes.description: Listen for the shifter engaging; inspect the drive system for failure.

fixes.title: Replace the shifter or motor
fixes.description: If the shifter actuator or motor has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 1 minute. If F7E1 persists, inspect the shifter and drive motor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive system diagnosis is complex — professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Motor / drive system fault — basket not reaching speed
error_codes.detail: The motor control detected a drive-system problem — the basket could not be driven correctly, often due to the drive coupling, shifter, or a mechanical bind.

causes.description: Mechanical bind in the drive system

fixes.title: Check for a bind
fixes.description: Remove the load and spin the basket by hand to feel for binding.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Inspect and replace the drive components
fixes.description: Inspect the shifter, coupling, and motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 1 minute, check for a mechanical bind. Inspect the drive system if F7E3 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive system diagnosis is complex — professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Motor control fault — drive electronics error
error_codes.detail: The motor control unit reported an internal fault driving the motor. The basket cannot be driven reliably and the cycle stops.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Inspect motor wiring
fixes.description: Check the harness between the control and the motor for damage.

fixes.title: Replace the motor control or motor
fixes.description: If the drive electronics have failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 1 minute. If F7E6 persists, the motor control unit likely needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor control replacement is complex — professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Motor unable to reach target speed
error_codes.detail: The drive motor could not reach the commanded speed within the allowed time. An overloaded or unbalanced basket, a drive fault, or a motor control problem can cause this.

fixes.title: Redistribute the load
fixes.description: Remove some items and rebalance, then restart.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Inspect the drive system
fixes.description: Check the shifter, coupling, and motor if F7E7 persists.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Rebalance the load and unplug 1 minute. Inspect the drive system if F7E7 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the load is balanced and F7E7 persists, the drive system needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Dispenser fault — detergent dispenser system error
error_codes.detail: The control detected a problem with the dispenser system (the dispenser motor/valve that releases detergent, bleach, or softener). The wrong amount may be dispensed, or the dispenser cannot be verified.

fixes.title: Clean the dispenser
fixes.description: Remove and rinse the dispenser drawer and clear any detergent residue.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Replace the dispenser assembly
fixes.description: If the dispenser motor or actuator has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the dispenser and unplug 1 minute. Replace the dispenser if F8E2 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the dispenser is clean and F8E2 persists, the dispenser actuator or control has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Control / user interface fault — communication mismatch
error_codes.detail: F2E3 indicates an issue with the appliance control unit, the user interface, or the wiring between them — the two are not communicating consistently. Button presses may not register or cycles may not start.

causes.description: Wiring/connector between control and UI loose

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Reseat the UI connector
fixes.description: Check and firmly reseat the harness between the user interface and the main control.

fixes.title: Replace the user interface board
fixes.description: If the connection is sound but F2E3 persists.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 1 minute and reseat the UI connector. Replace the UI board if F2E3 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new UI board and F2E3 persists, the main control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Water level / pressure sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an out-of-range value from the water-level pressure sensor, so it cannot measure the water level accurately. Fill and drain decisions become unreliable.

causes.description: Air hose to pressure sensor kinked or clogged

fixes.title: Check the pressure (air) hose
fixes.description: Inspect the hose from the tub to the sensor for kinks, leaks, or clogs.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixes.description: If the sensor has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Check the air hose and unplug 1 minute. Replace the pressure sensor if F3E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the hose is clear and F3E1 persists, the pressure sensor or control has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — main control to motor control
error_codes.detail: The main control board and the motor control unit have lost reliable communication. The drive cannot be commanded correctly and the cycle stops.

causes.description: Wiring/connector between control boards loose or damaged

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Reseat the inter-board harness
fixes.description: Check and reseat the connectors between the main control and the motor control.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If the harness is good, replace the motor control or main control.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 1 minute and reseat the inter-board harness. Replace a board if F6E1 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Inter-board communication diagnosis is complex — professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: No water detected — fill failure
error_codes.detail: On these Whirlpool-platform front-load washers, F20 (FH on older models) means no water or insufficient water was detected entering the tub. A closed supply, clogged inlet screens, or a failed inlet valve are the usual causes.

causes.description: Supply valves closed or low pressure

fixes.title: Check the supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both hot and cold valves are fully open.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screens
fixes.description: Shut off water, disconnect the hoses, and clean the mesh inlet screens.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply first.

fixes.title: Test the inlet valve
fixes.description: Test the inlet valve solenoids for continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Confirm supply open and clean inlet screens. Hold Start to resume. Replace inlet valve if F20 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply and valve are good and F20 persists, the pressure sensor or control has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock fault — door will not lock
error_codes.detail: On these Whirlpool-platform front-load washers, F22 means the door lock did not engage. The washer will not start a cycle until the door is confirmed locked.

causes.description: Door not closing fully / strike misaligned

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Ensure the door clicks shut and nothing blocks the strike.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock assembly
fixes.description: If the lock will not engage.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly and unplug 1 minute. Replace the door lock if F22 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new door lock and F22 persists, the control board lock circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door switch error — door state not detected
error_codes.detail: On these Whirlpool-platform front-load washers, F26 indicates the door switch did not report the door opening/closing as expected, so the control cannot verify the door state.

fixes.title: Open and close the door
fixes.description: Cycle the door a few times so the switch registers; ensure it closes fully.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock/switch assembly
fixes.description: If the switch has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cycle the door and unplug 1 minute. Replace the door switch if F26 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new door switch and F26 persists, the control board door input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Serial communication error — control boards not communicating
error_codes.detail: On these Whirlpool-platform front-load washers, F28 means the central control unit and the motor control unit have lost serial communication. The drive cannot be commanded and the cycle stops.

causes.description: Wiring/connector between control boards loose or damaged

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 1 minute.

fixes.title: Reseat the inter-board harness
fixes.description: Check and reseat the serial connectors between the central control and the motor control.

fixes.title: Replace the failed control unit
fixes.description: If the harness is good, replace the motor control or central control.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 1 minute and reseat the serial harness. Replace a control unit if F28 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Serial communication diagnosis is complex — professional service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic control board failure
error_codes.detail: The main dryer control board has experienced an internal fault and needs replacement.

fixes.title: Check circuit breaker
fixes.description: Confirm dryer has full 240V power.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If F01 returns, board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If F01 returns immediately, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F01 persists on new board, there may be a wiring or power supply issue.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor (temperature sensor) failure
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an open circuit from the exhaust thermistor, so it cannot measure outgoing air temperature or regulate heat safely — the dryer stops or refuses to heat. A failed thermistor is the most common cause; it's an inexpensive part on the blower housing.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct inside cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor failure — drum not turning
error_codes.detail: The control commanded the drive motor but detected no rotation, so the drum will not turn and the cycle stops. Failed motor windings are the most common root cause, though a bad start capacitor or a tripped thermal overload on the motor can produce the same code.

fixes.title: Test drive motor
fixes.description: Test motor windings for continuity.

fixes.title: Check thermal overload
fixes.description: Press reset button on motor if present.

fixes.title: Replace drive motor
fixes.description: Remove drum to access motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After motor replacement, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive motor replacement requires significant disassembly.

error_codes.meaning: Restricted airflow — blocked vent (safety shutdown)
error_codes.detail: The dryer detected insufficient airflow through the exhaust. This is a safety alert — running with a blocked vent causes overheating and is a fire risk. A clogged lint screen or blocked exhaust duct is the usual cause.

causes.description: Long/kinked vent run or blocked outdoor flap

fixes.title: Clean the lint screen
fixes.description: Remove and clean the lint filter every load.

fixes.title: Clear the exhaust duct
fixes.description: Disconnect and clean the full vent run and the outdoor flapper.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before disconnecting the duct.

fixes.title: Check the vent length
fixes.description: Excessive duct length or too many bends restricts airflow.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the lint screen and exhaust duct, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If airflow is clear and AF persists, the airflow/moisture sensor or control has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor open circuit — temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an open circuit (very high resistance, typically over 50k ohms) from the thermistor, so it cannot measure air temperature and stops heating. A failed thermistor or a loose connector is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: At room temperature the thermistor should read roughly 10k ohms. Infinite resistance = open.

fixes.title: Check the connector
fixes.description: Reseat the thermistor connector.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Located on the blower housing or exhaust duct.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor and E1 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor short circuit — temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control is reading a shorted thermistor (very low resistance, typically under 500 ohms), so the temperature reading is invalid. A shorted thermistor or pinched wiring is the usual cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Room temperature should read ~10k ohms. Near 0 ohms = shorted.

fixes.title: Inspect the wiring
fixes.description: Look for pinched or melted wiring shorting the thermistor.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: A shorted thermistor cannot be repaired.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads shorted, the wiring harness has a short to ground.

error_codes.meaning: Inlet thermistor failure — temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an out-of-range resistance from the inlet thermistor, so it cannot measure incoming air temperature and regulate heat safely. A failed thermistor or a loose connector is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: At room temperature the thermistor should read roughly 10k ohms.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Check the inlet thermistor connector.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Install a new inlet thermistor.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor and F24 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drive motor failure — drum not turning
error_codes.detail: The control commanded the drive motor but detected no rotation, so the drum will not turn and the cycle stops. Failed motor windings are the most common cause, though a bad start switch or a tripped thermal overload can produce the same code.

fixes.title: Test the drive motor
fixes.description: Test the motor windings for continuity.

fixes.title: Check the thermal overload
fixes.description: Press the reset button on the motor if present.

fixes.title: Replace the drive motor
fixes.description: Remove the drum to access the motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After motor replacement, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive motor replacement requires significant disassembly.

error_codes.meaning: Moisture sensor failure — auto-dry not working
error_codes.detail: The control detected an implausible reading from the moisture sensor bars inside the drum, so auto-dry cycles cannot judge when clothes are dry — loads stop damp or run far too long. Fabric-softener residue coating the metal bars is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Clean the sensor bars
fixes.description: Wipe the metal moisture sensor strips inside the drum with a little rubbing alcohol.

fixes.title: Use timed dry as a workaround
fixes.description: Auto-dry depends on the sensor; timed dry bypasses it.

fixes.title: Check sensor wiring / replace sensor
fixes.description: If cleaning does not help, inspect the wiring or replace the sensor.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the moisture sensor bars and restart on auto-dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If cleaned and F29 persists, the moisture sensor or control board input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Restricted airflow / moisture sensor — stored airflow fault
error_codes.detail: The stored F30 fault indicates restricted airflow or an airflow/moisture sensing problem detected during a cycle. It commonly accompanies the AF alert when the exhaust is blocked.

causes.description: Clogged lint screen or exhaust duct

fixes.title: Clean the lint screen and duct
fixes.description: Clear the lint filter and the full exhaust run.
fixes.warning: Unplug before disconnecting the duct.

fixes.title: Check the vent length
fixes.description: Reduce excessive duct length or bends.

fixes.title: Inspect the airflow/moisture sensor
fixes.description: If airflow is clear, check the sensor and wiring.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the lint screen and exhaust duct, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If airflow is clear and F30 persists, the airflow/moisture sensor or control has failed.

error_codes.meaning: L2 line voltage / heating circuit fault
error_codes.detail: On Maytag dryers, F31 indicates an L2 (line 2) voltage supply problem reaching the control board — the heater cannot turn on. A tripped breaker leg, a failed high-limit thermostat, an open heating element, or wiring is the usual cause.

causes.description: One 240V breaker leg tripped / low line voltage
High-limit thermostat or thermal fuse open

fixes.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixes.description: Both legs of the 240V double breaker must be on. Reset fully if one tripped.

fixes.title: Test the high-limit thermostat and thermal fuse
fixes.description: Check both for continuity; an open device kills the heat circuit.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Check the element for continuity; replace if open.
fixes.warning: Unplug before servicing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reset both breaker legs and unplug 1 minute. Test the heat circuit if F31 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If line voltage is good and F31 persists, the heating circuit or control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low or no line voltage — heater cannot turn on
error_codes.detail: An L2 (Low or No Line Voltage) alert means the dryer is not receiving full power — typically one leg of the 240V supply is missing, so the drum may turn but the heater will not work. A tripped breaker leg or a loose connection at the breaker or outlet is the usual cause.

causes.description: Loose connection at breaker or outlet

fixes.title: Reset the circuit breaker
fixes.description: Fully switch the dryer's double breaker off then on — both legs must be live.

fixes.title: Check the outlet and cord connection
fixes.description: Confirm the power cord is fully seated and the terminal block is tight.
fixes.warning: Unplug before checking the terminal block.

fixes.title: Have the supply inspected
fixes.description: Persistent L2 after resetting points to a house wiring or breaker fault.
fixes.warning: Electrical supply faults should be inspected by an electrician.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reset both legs of the breaker and restart. Check the cord and terminal block if L2 persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the breaker and cord are good and L2 persists, have an electrician check the 240V supply.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — cycle interrupted by loss of power
error_codes.detail: PF (Power Failure) means the dryer lost power during a running cycle. It is an informational alert, not a fault — the cycle was interrupted and needs to be restarted.

fixes.title: Restart the cycle
fixes.description: Press Start to resume or restart the cycle.

fixes.title: Check the cord and outlet
fixes.description: If PF recurs, ensure the power cord is fully seated and the outlet is sound.
fixes.warning: Unplug before checking the terminal block.

fixes.title: Check the breaker
fixes.description: A breaker that trips repeatedly indicates an electrical fault to investigate.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start to restart the cycle. PF clears on its own.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: PF is not a fault. Call a pro only if it recurs with stable household power — that points to a loose connection or control fault.

error_codes.meaning: ERC board EEPROM checksum error — control board memory failure
error_codes.detail: The Electronic Range Control board failed its internal memory checksum. This is usually caused by a power surge or age-related failure of the board's non-volatile memory. The oven will not operate normally with this fault active.

causes.description: Brown-out during a write cycle corrupting memory

fixes.title: Power cycle the range
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes. A power cycle occasionally clears a transient F1-E0 caused by a brown-out.

fixes.title: Replace the ERC control board
fixes.description: F1-E0 is almost always a board failure. The ERC board is mounted behind the control panel. Remove the back panel or the control panel housing (2–6 screws depending on model) and disconnect all wiring harnesses.
fixes.warning: Photograph all wiring harness positions before disconnecting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 15 minutes. If F1-E0 returns, ERC board must be replaced.
error_codes.models_affected: Maytag MER and MGR series ranges
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control board replacement requires handling multiple high-voltage connections. Professional installation is recommended.

error_codes.meaning: ERC board keypad stuck key — shorted keypad detected during power-up
error_codes.detail: On power-up, the ERC board detected that a key on the control panel was already pressed (shorted). This prevents normal startup. Distinct from F2-E0 in that it is detected at power-on, not during normal operation.

causes.description: Keypad membrane failure (key stuck at power-on)
ERC board keypad scan circuit failure

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Wipe all keypad buttons with a barely-damp cloth. Pay attention to seams between buttons where grease can accumulate.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power. If a key was physically stuck, cleaning and power cycling often resolves F1-E1.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad membrane
fixes.description: If F1-E1 returns after cleaning, the keypad membrane has failed internally.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Wipe panel clean. Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F1-E1 persists, replace keypad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new keypad and F1-E1 persists, the ERC board keypad scan circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted keypad — stuck key during normal operation
error_codes.detail: A key on the oven's keypad registered as continuously pressed for more than 60 seconds during normal operation. This is the most common oven control panel error on Maytag ranges built on the Whirlpool platform.

causes.description: Key stuck from grease or food debris buildup
Keypad membrane failure — key shorted internally

fixes.title: Clean the control panel
fixes.description: Clean around every key with a barely-damp cloth, focusing on the seams. Allow the panel to dry completely before powering on.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes. Many F2-E0 errors on Maytag ranges clear after a power cycle and cleaning.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad
fixes.description: If F2-E0 returns consistently after cleaning, the membrane keypad has an internal short.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean panel. Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. If F2-E0 returns, replace keypad.
error_codes.models_affected: Maytag MER/MGR/MEW/MET ranges and wall ovens
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new keypad and F2-E0 persists, the ERC board has a failed keypad scan circuit.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad disconnected — ribbon cable failure
error_codes.detail: The ribbon cable or connection between the keypad and the ERC board has lost continuity. The ERC board can no longer communicate with the touchpad.

causes.description: Ribbon cable connector loose at ERC board
ERC board ribbon connector port failure

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Remove the control panel backing. Locate the flat ribbon cable connecting the keypad to the ERC board. Disconnect and firmly reseat both ends. Look for any cracking or creasing in the ribbon.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad assembly
fixes.description: On most Maytag models, the keypad and ribbon cable are a single assembly. If the ribbon is damaged, replace the complete keypad.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 5 minutes. Reseat ribbon cable. If F2-E1 persists, replace keypad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new keypad and cable and F2-E1 persists, the ERC board ribbon connector port has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor (RTD probe) has an open circuit. The oven will refuse to heat because it cannot safely regulate temperature without accurate sensor feedback. This is a very common Maytag range error.

causes.description: Temperature sensor failure — burned out or open
Sensor connector loose at ERC board

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Pull the range out from the wall and remove the back access panel (4–6 screws). Locate the temperature sensor — a thin metal probe extending 2 inches into the oven cavity from the rear. Disconnect the 2-wire connector and test resistance with a multimeter: at room temperature it should read 1,080–1,090 ohms. Open circuit (OL) = failed sensor.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Before replacing, push the 2-pin connector firmly onto the sensor terminals. Loose connections are common and can cause intermittent F3-E0.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: The sensor is mounted with 1–2 screws. Pull the sensor out from inside the oven cavity and feed the wires through the back wall. Install the new sensor and route the wires.
fixes.warning: Let the oven cool completely before working inside.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, run a 350°F bake cycle and verify F3-E0 is resolved.
error_codes.models_affected: Maytag MER/MGR series electric and gas ranges
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor tests correctly and F3-E0 persists, the ERC board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature sensor is reading near-zero resistance — a short circuit condition. The ERC board detects a falsely hot reading and shuts down heating to prevent runaway temperature.

causes.description: Wiring pinched against oven cavity wall causing short

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect sensor. Room-temperature reading should be ~1,080 ohms. Near 0 ohms = shorted sensor.

fixes.title: Inspect the sensor wiring
fixes.description: Look for any points where the wiring passes through the oven wall — metal burrs or improper routing can pinch the wire and cause a chassis short.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: A shorted sensor cannot be repaired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing sensor, run a 350°F bake cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new sensor still shows near-0 ohms with the connector disconnected, the wiring has a short to chassis.

error_codes.meaning: Oven over-temperature — exceeded safe operating limit
error_codes.detail: The oven temperature exceeded the maximum safe operating limit, typically above 575°F (302°C) during normal cooking. The ERC board shut off all heating and requires a diagnosis before reuse.

causes.description: Temperature sensor failure causing runaway heating
Bake relay on ERC board stuck in closed position
Wiring fault causing continuous power to heating element

fixes.title: Turn off circuit breaker immediately
fixes.description: Do not attempt to use the oven again until the fault is diagnosed. Allow the oven to cool fully — at least 1 hour.
fixes.warning: FIRE HAZARD — do not open the oven door or use the oven until diagnosed.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor after cooling
fixes.description: Disconnect and test the oven temperature sensor (should read ~1,080 ohms). A failed open or shorted sensor can cause the ERC board to drive the heating element continuously.

fixes.title: Replace sensor if out of spec
fixes.description: If sensor reads correctly, the bake relay on the ERC board has stuck closed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker. Do not use until fault is diagnosed by a professional.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F3-E2 is a safety event. Have a qualified technician diagnose the oven before returning it to service.

error_codes.meaning: Self-clean door latch failure — latch did not lock
error_codes.detail: The door latch motor ran but the latch switch did not confirm a locked position within the required time. Self-clean cycle cannot start or is interrupted.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes. A transient latch timeout can clear with a power cycle.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch mechanism
fixes.description: Open the oven door and look at the top of the oven opening. The latch hook should move freely with light finger pressure. Clear any food debris from the latch slot in the oven frame.

fixes.title: Check door closure
fixes.description: The door must close fully and evenly. Check that both hinges are engaged and the door seal is intact.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: The latch motor sits at the top rear of the range. Access via the back service panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug the range before accessing the latch assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. Check latch for debris. If F5-E1 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.models_affected: Maytag MER/MGR/MEW ranges with self-clean
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new door latch and F5-E1 persists, the ERC board latch relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch during lock — general latch circuit fault
error_codes.detail: A general fault with the door latch circuit occurred during a locking operation. Similar to F5-E1 but triggered from a different latch state.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 5 minutes.

fixes.title: Check door alignment and latch slot
fixes.description: Ensure the oven door closes completely and the latch hook travels freely.

fixes.title: Replace the door latch assembly
fixes.description: F9-E0 typically points to the latch motor or switch.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes. If F9-E0 returns, replace door latch assembly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If latch is replaced and F9-E0 persists, the ERC board latch relay or wiring has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Fan motor overload — motor drawing excess current
error_codes.detail: The fan motor has tripped its overload protection by drawing more current than the rated limit. This is usually caused by a worn motor bearing, a buildup of grease on the blower wheel, or a blocked exhaust duct restricting airflow.

causes.description: Grease buildup on blower wheel causing imbalance and drag
Blocked or kinked exhaust duct increasing static pressure
Fan motor bearing worn or seized

fixes.title: Clean the grease filters and blower wheel
fixes.description: Remove the grease filters (most Broan filters pull straight down or tilt out). Wash in hot soapy water or the dishwasher. If accessible, also clean the blower wheel — grease accumulation on the blades causes imbalance that makes the motor work harder.

fixes.title: Check the exhaust duct
fixes.description: Go to where the duct exits the house and confirm the damper flap opens freely. Check the full duct run for kinks, disconnected sections, or blockages — a bird or wasp nest at the termination cap is a common culprit. Duct should be as short and straight as possible with no 90-degree elbows close to the hood.

fixes.title: Power cycle the hood
fixes.description: Turn off the circuit breaker for 15 minutes to reset the motor overload. If the underlying cause is resolved (duct clear, blower clean), the motor will run normally.

fixes.title: Replace the blower motor
fixes.description: If the motor bearing has seized or the motor is drawing excess current even with clean blades and clear duct, replace the motor/blower assembly.
fixes.warning: Shut off circuit breaker before accessing the motor housing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean grease filters and blower wheel. Check duct for blockages. Turn off circuit breaker 15 minutes.
error_codes.models_affected: Broan Elite, BCDF, and BCSF series range hoods
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If motor is replaced and E01 returns immediately, the control board motor driver has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Fan motor failure — motor not running
error_codes.detail: The range hood control board is not detecting the fan motor running at all. The motor has failed electrically, or the connection between the board and motor has been lost.

causes.description: Wiring connector between board and motor disconnected
Control board motor output circuit failed

fixes.title: Check the motor connector
fixes.description: Disconnect power. Locate the motor connector on the control board (typically a 2–3 pin connector). Disconnect and firmly reseat. Check for any burned or corroded pins.

fixes.title: Test motor windings
fixes.description: Disconnect the motor connector. Test resistance across the motor leads — most Broan blower motors read 3–15 ohms when healthy. Open circuit (OL) = failed motor.

fixes.title: Check the motor thermal fuse
fixes.description: Some Broan motors have an inline thermal fuse in the motor lead. Test continuity — zero resistance is good, open circuit means the fuse blew and needs replacement.

fixes.title: Replace the blower motor assembly
fixes.description: If motor windings are open, replace the complete blower/motor assembly.
fixes.warning: Disconnect circuit breaker before any motor work.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Check motor connector. Disconnect circuit breaker 15 minutes. Test motor windings.
error_codes.models_affected: Broan Elite and connected series range hoods
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new motor and E02 persists, the control board motor output circuit has failed — replace the control board.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — Wi-Fi module or app connectivity failure
error_codes.detail: On Broan connected (Wi-Fi enabled) range hoods, E03 indicates the hood has lost communication with the app or the Wi-Fi module has failed. This error does not affect the hood's core cooking ventilation function.

causes.description: Wi-Fi router signal too weak at hood location
Router channel or band incompatibility (5GHz not supported)

fixes.title: Check Wi-Fi signal strength
fixes.description: Broan connected hoods require a 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi network. If your router only broadcasts 5 GHz, the hood will not connect. Confirm your router has a 2.4 GHz band enabled.

fixes.title: Reconnect via the Broan Home app
fixes.description: Open the Broan Home app and use 'Add Device' to re-pair the hood. A power cycle (circuit breaker off 30 seconds) before re-pairing often helps.

fixes.title: Move router or use Wi-Fi extender
fixes.description: If the hood is far from the router, the signal may be too weak for reliable connectivity.

fixes.title: Factory reset the Wi-Fi module
fixes.description: Press and hold the Wi-Fi button on the hood for 10 seconds until the indicator flashes rapidly. Re-pair from the app.

fixes.title: Replace the Wi-Fi control module
fixes.description: If the module fails to connect even with strong 2.4 GHz signal and after factory reset, the Wi-Fi hardware has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker 30 seconds. Ensure 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi available. Re-pair via Broan Home app.
error_codes.models_affected: Broan connected/smart range hoods (BCSF, BCSD series)
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: E03 does not affect ventilation function. The hood will still run manually. Only call a pro if physical module replacement is needed and you are not comfortable with electrical work.

error_codes.meaning: Heat sensor tripped — oven or cooktop overheating detected
error_codes.detail: The range hood's built-in heat sensor detected unusually high temperatures above the cooktop. Some connected Broan hoods automatically activate at high speed when this sensor trips. If the fan does not run after F01, the sensor circuit or board has failed.

causes.description: Cooktop fire or very high-heat cooking that triggered the sensor
Heat sensor calibration drift (sensor reading too high)

fixes.title: Check for actual overheating
fixes.description: If there was a flame-up or very high-heat event at the cooktop, turn off the burners, allow everything to cool, and clear the hood's grease filters. The F01 may clear on its own once the temperature drops.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off circuit breaker for 10 minutes after the cooktop and hood have cooled.

fixes.title: Test the heat sensor
fixes.description: The heat sensor is a small disc or rod sensor near the hood's intake. Test resistance — at room temperature it should show a predictable value per the service manual (typically 10–100 kΩ for NTC sensors). Significantly out of range = replace sensor.

fixes.title: Replace the heat sensor
fixes.description: The sensor is typically clipped or screwed into the underside of the hood canopy.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Allow to cool. Turn off circuit breaker 10 minutes. If F01 returns, test and replace heat sensor.
error_codes.models_affected: Broan Elite and connected series with heat sensing
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new heat sensor and F01 persists, the control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Range hood fan does not run on any speed
error_codes.detail: The fan blower does not respond to any speed button press. This is the most common Broan range hood complaint and can have several causes — from a simple tripped circuit breaker to a failed motor or control board.

causes.description: Thermal overload protection tripped from restricted airflow
Faulty control board or speed switch

fixes.title: Check the circuit breaker
fixes.description: Go to your electrical panel and check whether the breaker for the range hood has tripped. Reset if needed. Range hoods are on a dedicated circuit or shared kitchen circuit.

fixes.title: Check the grease filters
fixes.description: Clogged grease filters cause the motor to overheat and trip the thermal overload. Remove and clean all filters, then wait 15–30 minutes for the motor to cool before testing.

fixes.title: Test the rocker switches / speed buttons
fixes.description: The speed switches or touchpad can fail. With power off, test switch continuity.

fixes.title: Test motor windings and replace if failed
fixes.description: With the circuit breaker off, disconnect the motor leads and test resistance. Open circuit = motor has failed.
fixes.warning: Confirm power is off at breaker before touching any wiring.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Check circuit breaker. Clean grease filters. Let motor cool 30 minutes. Test again.
error_codes.models_affected: All Broan and Nutone range hood models
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the motor, switches, and wiring all check out normal, the control board has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Range hood lights do not come on
error_codes.detail: The cooktop lighting built into the range hood has stopped working. This can be a simple bulb failure, a blown fuse on the lighting circuit, or a failed lighting board on more advanced models.

causes.description: Blown lighting fuse on control board

fixes.title: Replace the bulb
fixes.description: Most Broan range hoods use standard appliance bulbs: 40W incandescent (A15 base), T8 halogen, or LED G8/G9 depending on the model. Check your model number at Broan.com for the exact bulb type. Turn off circuit breaker before accessing the bulb socket.
