parts: Appliance bulb for your Broan model ($5–$25)
fixes.warning: Circuit breaker off before changing bulbs.

fixes.title: Check the light socket
fixes.description: If the replacement bulb also does not light, test the socket with a multimeter (120V AC at the socket with power on). No voltage = wiring or board issue.

fixes.title: Check board fuse
fixes.description: Some Broan models have a small glass fuse (1–2A) on the control board for the lighting circuit. Inspect and replace if blown.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off circuit breaker. Replace bulb with correct type for your model.
error_codes.models_affected: All Broan and Nutone range hood models
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new bulb, socket has power, and lights still do not work, the lighting control board or switch has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Slow drain — pump is draining but too slowly
error_codes.detail: Kenmore washers are made by Whirlpool or LG depending on the model series. F21 on Whirlpool-platform models means a slow or failed drain. Check the pump filter first.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access panel at lower front. Drain water, remove and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper high loop installation.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear and hose free, pump may be weak or failing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run Drain & Spin cycle after clearing blockage.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump filter cleaning doesn't resolve F21, the pump may need replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Water supply error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool-built 110-series Kenmore washers, F20 means the control opened the inlet valve but detected no water entering the drum within the allowed fill time, so the cycle stops. A closed supply valve or clogged inlet hose screens cause most cases — no parts needed.

fixes.title: Open supply valves
fixes.description: Both hot and cold valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoids for continuity (200–500 ohms).

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully and straighten any kinked fill hoses, then press Pause/Cancel twice to clear the code. Unplug for 60 seconds if it persists, then restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is good and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: On LG-manufactured Kenmore models (typically 400+ series), OE indicates a drain failure. Pump filter and drain hose are the first checks.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Front lower panel filter. Drain water, clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; correct height (28–47 inches).

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter clear, listen for pump operation.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run Spin Only cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump filter and hose are clear but no draining, pump motor has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor locked (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: Motor stall or hall sensor fault on LG-manufactured Kenmore washers. The drum cannot rotate at the commanded speed.

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove half the items and retry.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace hall sensor
fixes.description: The hall sensor is a small sensor near the rotor — inexpensive and often the sole cause.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes, reduce load, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If hall sensor replacement doesn't resolve LE, stator or main board needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: On LG-built Kenmore washers, UE means the drum's vibration sensing detected an unevenly distributed load, so the spin cycle can't reach full speed safely and the washer keeps retrying. Items bunched to one side — a lone towel or bedspread — is the usual trigger.

fixes.title: Redistribute laundry
fixes.description: Open door, move items evenly around drum.

fixes.title: Adjust load size
fixes.description: Remove or add items to balance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Redistribute and press Start/Pause to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If UE occurs every load, suspension springs are worn.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error (LG-platform Kenmore models)
error_codes.detail: On LG-built 796-series Kenmore washers, IE means the control opened the inlet valve but the water level didn't rise within the expected fill window, so the cycle halts. A partly closed supply valve or clogged inlet hose screens are the most common root causes.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Fully open both hot and cold valves.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply confirmed good.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully, check the fill hoses for kinks, and clean the inlet screens. Press Power off, wait 60 seconds, then power on and restart the cycle — IE clears once a normal fill is detected.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The Electrolux washer has failed to drain within the expected time. Pump filter is the first place to check.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Behind lower access panel. Drain water, remove and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm no kinks and proper high loop.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: Test motor if filter is clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run Spin Only cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor tested failed, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: The washer is not detecting water entering during fill. Supply valve, inlet screens, and inlet valve are the common causes.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Remove hoses and clean mesh screens at washer ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully, then press Start/Pause to resume or cancel and restart the cycle. If E10 returns in the first minutes of fill, clean the inlet screens and retest.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply confirmed and error persists, inlet valve needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Door not closed or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The door lock switch never signaled the control that the door is fully closed and locked, so the cycle will not start. Most often the door just needs a firmer push or laundry is caught in the gasket; a failed lock solenoid is the next most likely cause.

fixes.title: Check for items in seal
fixes.description: Inspect gasket for caught clothing.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push until definite click.

fixes.title: Replace door lock assembly
fixes.description: If latch or solenoid is failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close door firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but E40 persists, lock solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor failure
error_codes.detail: The drive motor has failed or is not operating correctly. The drum will not rotate.

fixes.title: Check for belt (belt-drive models)
fixes.description: Inspect belt for breakage.

fixes.title: Test motor
fixes.description: Access motor, test winding continuity.

fixes.title: Replace motor or control board
fixes.description: Replace whichever component failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 10 minutes to reset the motor control, then restart on a spin-only cycle. If E50 returns as soon as the drum tries to turn, motor or board replacement is required.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor replacement requires significant disassembly.

error_codes.meaning: Wiring harness fault — communication error
error_codes.detail: The main wiring harness has a fault. This is typically a wiring or board communication issue.

fixes.title: Inspect wiring connections
fixes.description: Check all connector plugs for loose fit, especially at the control board and motor.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Replace wiring harness
fixes.description: If connectors are secure but error persists.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E90 returns, wiring harness needs inspection.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If harness is intact, control board communication port has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Insufficient heating — dryer not reaching temperature
error_codes.detail: The control detected the drum reaching temperature too slowly or not at all, so cycles run long and clothes come out damp. A burned-out heating element is the most common root cause, followed by a high-limit thermostat tripped by a blocked vent.

fixes.title: Check for vent blockage
fixes.description: A blocked vent causes high-limit to trip, cutting heat.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Test for continuity — should read 10–15 ohms.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: Test for continuity. Tripped thermostat reads open.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install new element or thermostat as needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes, then run a timed-dry test on high heat. If E61 returns or the dryer still runs cool, test the heating element and high-limit thermostat before further use.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element and thermostat are good, board heater relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure — open circuit
error_codes.detail: Current monitoring on the heater circuit shows an open circuit — the heating element has burned through, so the drum turns but no heat is produced. Age-related element burnout is the most common cause, often accelerated by restricted exhaust airflow.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test for continuity. Open circuit = failed.

fixes.title: Replace element
fixes.description: Access from back panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug dryer before accessing element.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, run test cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If element replaced and no heat, board heater relay has failed.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an open or out-of-range resistance from the NTC temperature sensor, so it cannot regulate drum heat. The dryer may run without heat or stop mid-cycle. A failed thermistor in the exhaust duct is the most common cause.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct inside cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Pump fault — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The control detected that water was not pumped out within the expected drain time — the drum still holds water when the pump should have emptied it, so the program stops with F11. The most common root cause is a pump filter clogged with lint or debris; a kinked drain hose or an object jamming the pump impeller follow close behind.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Behind lower service flap. Turn slowly and drain water before removing filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; correct standpipe height.

fixes.title: Remove impeller obstruction
fixes.description: Check pump housing for foreign objects.

fixes.title: Replace pump
fixes.description: Miele pumps are high-quality but can fail.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run a Drain/Short Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele service intervals are long, but pump filter should be cleaned every 6 months.

error_codes.meaning: Drain time exceeded — slow or failed drain
error_codes.detail: The drain cycle is taking longer than allowed. Water is not emptying from the drum.

fixes.title: Clean filter
fixes.description: Same as F11 procedure.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Trace hose for kinks.

fixes.title: Replace pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele authorized service recommended for pump replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Water protection system activated — flood protection triggered
error_codes.detail: The Miele waterproof system (Waterproof System/WPS) has detected water in the base of the machine. This is a safety lockout.

fixes.title: Tilt machine to drain base
fixes.description: Carefully tilt forward (power off) to drain base tray.
fixes.warning: Power off water and electricity first.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for tears or pulled sections.

fixes.title: Check supply hose fittings
fixes.description: Inspect both ends of the supply hose for weeping.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak source, dry thoroughly. Sensor resets when base is dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no leak source found, internal hose or sump connection needs Miele authorized inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Motor fault — drive motor failure
error_codes.detail: The Miele washing machine motor has experienced a fault. This is a serious error typically requiring professional service. Miele motors are brushless and exceptionally durable, but can fail after many years.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes. A transient motor fault may clear.

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items — F53 can sometimes be triggered by extreme overloading.

fixes.title: Contact Miele service
fixes.description: F53 motor faults on Miele machines almost always require authorized technician diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F53 persists, contact Miele authorized service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele motor replacement is complex and typically requires factory-trained technicians. DIY motor replacement risks voiding the warranty.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The water temperature sensor (NTC thermistor) has failed or is reading out of spec.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: At room temp: should read approximately 6k–10k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat the connector at the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Miele genuine parts recommended.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, restart. Code clears after successful heated cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele thermistors are reliable. If new part fails quickly, there may be a wiring issue.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor short-circuited
error_codes.detail: The control read the water/heating NTC temperature sensor (or its wiring) as short-circuited, so heating is disabled and the program stops with F1. At about 68°F the sensor should read roughly 14.5k ohms — a reading far below that points to a shorted thermistor, a pinched harness, or moisture in a connector.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, switch back on — a transient moisture short can clear.

fixes.title: Inspect sensor connector
fixes.description: Check for water ingress or corrosion at the NTC plug and dry it out.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: At room temp it should read ~14.5k ohms; near 0 ohms confirms a short.

fixes.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Fit a genuine Miele thermistor if the part is shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, restart. If F1 returns, the sensor or wiring needs attention.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the new sensor faults immediately, a wiring or board issue is likely — Miele authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: NTC temperature sensor open-circuited
error_codes.detail: The control read the water/heating NTC temperature sensor (or its leads) as open-circuited — the opposite of F1 — so it cannot confirm water temperature and stops with F2. An open circuit usually means a broken thermistor element, a disconnected plug, or a severed lead.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, switch back on.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Confirm the NTC plug is fully seated on both the sensor and the board.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: An infinite/open reading at room temp confirms the sensor is open.

fixes.title: Replace the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Fit a genuine Miele thermistor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, restart. If F2 persists, replace the sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F2 after a new sensor points to harness or board damage — Miele authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Hot water intake fault
error_codes.detail: On hot-and-cold fill models the machine did not detect correct flow or temperature on the hot-water feed within the fill window, so the program stops with F15. A closed or restricted hot supply, a kinked hot hose, or a faulty hot inlet valve are the usual causes.

causes.description: Hot supply valve closed or restricted

fixes.title: Open the hot tap fully
fixes.description: Confirm the hot-water stopcock under the sink is fully open.

fixes.title: Straighten/clean the hose
fixes.description: Trace the hot hose for kinks and clean the mesh screen at the inlet.

fixes.title: Replace the hot inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply is good but the fault stays, the valve has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the hot supply, straighten the hose, switch off ~60 seconds, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the hot supply is confirmed good, Miele authorized service recommended for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heating fault — water not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The control expected the wash water to reach its target temperature within a set time but the NTC never confirmed it, so the program halts with F20 and washes run cold. A failed heating element is the leading cause, followed by the NTC sensor or the heater relay on the board.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and check continuity — a typical element reads ~20–30 ohms; open means failed.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Confirm the thermistor reads in range (see F1/F2).

fixes.title: Replace the failed component
fixes.description: Fit a genuine element or sensor as diagnosed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run a heated cycle to confirm the fault clears.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heater replacement on Miele washers requires care with the seal and harness — authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Door lock fault — door will not lock
error_codes.detail: The machine could not confirm the door lock engaged, so for safety it will not start and shows F34. A door not fully latched is the most common trigger, followed by a failed interlock or a wiring fault to the lock.

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Press the door until it clicks; clear any laundry trapped in the seal.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch and striker
fixes.description: Check the striker hook and lock cavity for damage or obstruction.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock
fixes.description: If the door is shut but F34 stays, the interlock has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly, switch off ~60 seconds, restart. If F34 returns, the lock needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele door interlock replacement is best handled by authorized service to keep the safety circuit intact.

error_codes.meaning: Door unlock fault — door will not release
error_codes.detail: At the end of the cycle the control could not confirm the door lock released, so it holds F35 and keeps the door shut. A failed lock mechanism or its wiring is the usual cause; the door stays locked until the fault is cleared or the lock is replaced.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall, wait 5 minutes, switch on — the lock may release on reset.

fixes.title: Use the emergency release
fixes.description: If fitted, use the manual lock release behind the lower flap to free the door.
fixes.warning: Only after the drum has stopped and water is drained.

fixes.title: Replace the door lock
fixes.description: A lock that will not release needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off 5 minutes; if the door frees but F35 returns, the lock needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door stays locked with water inside, contact Miele authorized service rather than forcing it.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic unit fault — main control failure
error_codes.detail: The control electronics reported an internal fault and the program stops with F39. This points at the main electronic unit (control board) rather than a single sensor or motor, and usually needs board-level diagnosis.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall for 5–10 minutes to clear a transient board fault.

fixes.title: Check supply quality
fixes.description: Confirm the machine is on a sound circuit, not a weak extension or unstable supply.

fixes.title: Board diagnosis/replacement
fixes.description: Persistent F39 indicates the electronic unit needs Miele diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off 5–10 minutes and restart. If F39 persists, the control unit needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Control-board faults on Miele washers require authorized service — board coding and calibration are model-specific.

error_codes.meaning: Faulty electronic memory — incorrect data
error_codes.detail: The control read invalid data from its electronic memory (EEPROM), so it cannot run reliably and stops with F41. This is a board-level fault — corrupted or failing memory on the main electronic unit.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall for several minutes; an occasional glitch may clear.

fixes.title: Board re-coding/replacement
fixes.description: Persistent F41 means the electronic unit needs reprogramming or replacement by Miele.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off several minutes and restart. If F41 persists, the control unit needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: EEPROM faults require Miele service — the board must be coded to the specific model.

error_codes.meaning: Appliance model not programmed
error_codes.detail: The control electronics are not coded to a specific model, so the machine does not know its own configuration and stops with F43. This typically appears after a board replacement that was not programmed, or after a memory fault wiped the model code.

causes.description: Replacement board not coded to model

fixes.title: Confirm recent board work
fixes.description: F43 almost always follows a control-board swap that skipped the model coding step.

fixes.title: Program the control
fixes.description: The electronic unit must be coded to the exact model by Miele service tooling.

error_codes.reset_instructions: F43 is not user-resettable — the control must be programmed to the model by Miele service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Model coding requires Miele service tooling — always authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Defective Flash RAM — incorrect data supplied
error_codes.detail: The control detected a fault in its Flash RAM and is being supplied incorrect data, so it halts with F45. Like F41/F43 this is a fault inside the main electronic unit rather than a mechanical part.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall for several minutes to attempt a clear.

fixes.title: Board diagnosis/replacement
fixes.description: Persistent F45 means the electronic unit needs reflashing or replacement by Miele.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off several minutes and restart. If F45 persists, the control unit needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Flash RAM faults require Miele service — reflashing/coding is model-specific.

error_codes.meaning: Drive fault — motor or drive electronics
error_codes.detail: The control detected a fault in the drive system and stops with F50. Miele motors are brushless and durable, so the fault often sits in the motor control electronics or wiring rather than the motor windings themselves; severe overloading can also trigger it.

fixes.title: Power cycle and reduce load
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes and remove some laundry — a transient drive fault may clear.

fixes.title: Check the drum turns freely
fixes.description: With power off, confirm nothing is jamming the drum or belt.

fixes.title: Drive diagnosis
fixes.description: Persistent F50 needs board/motor diagnosis by Miele.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes and restart with a lighter load. If F50 persists, the drive needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Drive electronics and motor work on Miele washers require authorized technicians.

error_codes.meaning: Pressure/level sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control received an implausible reading from the water-level pressure sensor, so it cannot judge fill or drain accurately and stops with F51. A blocked or split pressure hose, debris in the chamber, or a failed sensor are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixes.description: Check the thin hose between the tub chamber and the sensor for kinks, splits, or trapped water.

fixes.title: Clear the pressure chamber
fixes.description: Clean any soap/debris build-up that can block the air column.

fixes.title: Replace the level sensor
fixes.description: If the hose and chamber are clear, the sensor has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the pressure hose/chamber, switch off ~60 seconds, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fault persists after cleaning and a new sensor, Miele authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Spin fault — final spin speed below 400 rpm
error_codes.detail: The drum never reached the required final spin speed (under ~400 rpm), so the load comes out wetter than expected and the machine flags F56. Severe imbalance, an overload, or a drive/sensor issue keeps the spin from ramping up.

fixes.title: Redistribute the load
fixes.description: Open the door, rebalance bulky items, and avoid overloading.

fixes.title: Run a spin-only cycle
fixes.description: Test whether a balanced load spins up correctly.

fixes.title: Drive/sensor diagnosis
fixes.description: If a balanced load still fails, the drive or speed sensor needs checking.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Rebalance the load and run a spin-only cycle. If F56 persists with a balanced load, service is needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent low-spin faults with a balanced load point to drive or bearing issues — Miele service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Water path control unit fault
error_codes.detail: The control could not correctly operate or confirm the water-path control unit (the valve/diverter assembly that routes water through the detergent and rinse paths), so it stops with F63. A jammed diverter, a wiring fault, or a failed control unit are the usual causes.

causes.description: Scale or debris jamming the diverter

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the wall for several minutes to clear a transient fault.

fixes.title: Check for limescale
fixes.description: In hard-water areas, scale can jam the diverter — descaling may help.

fixes.title: Replace the water path unit
fixes.description: If the diverter is jammed or the unit failed, it needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off several minutes and restart. If F63 persists, the water-path unit needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: The water-path control unit is a sealed assembly best replaced by Miele authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Drum lighting cap missing
error_codes.detail: The control detected that the drum interior lighting cap/cover is missing or not correctly fitted and flags F65. This is a minor, mostly cosmetic fault around the drum light — it does not affect the wash, but the machine reports it for completeness.

fixes.title: Refit the lighting cap
fixes.description: Check the drum interior light cover is present and clicked into place.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: If the cap is present, reseat the light module connector.

fixes.title: Replace the light module
fixes.description: If the lamp/module has failed, fit a genuine replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Refit the lighting cap, switch off ~60 seconds, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: F65 is minor — only call a pro if the light module itself has failed and you are not comfortable replacing it.

error_codes.meaning: Drainage fault — water not pumped out
error_codes.detail: The control commanded a drain but the water level in the sump didn't drop within the expected time, so the program halts with F11 and standing water remains in the tub. A clogged filter combination in the sump is by far the most common root cause, followed by a kinked drain hose or a failing drain pump.

fixes.title: Clean the filters
fixes.description: Miele dishwashers have a fine filter and coarse filter. Remove and clean both.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Ensure proper high-loop installation and no kinks.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear, pump may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the filters and check the drain hose, then switch the dishwasher off at the unit, wait about 60 seconds, switch it back on, and restart on a short program — the fault clears once the sump drains normally.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele drain pump replacement is best handled by authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake fault — dishwasher not filling
error_codes.detail: The Miele dishwasher did not take in the required amount of water within the fill time limit, so the program stops with F13. The control monitors fill volume and pressure at the circulation system — a closed or partially open supply tap is the most common cause, followed by a blocked inlet screen or kinked hose.

causes.description: Supply valve closed or partially open

fixes.title: Check supply valve
fixes.description: Confirm under-sink valve is fully open.

fixes.title: Check inlet hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to unit.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screen
fixes.description: Unscrew supply hose and clean the screen at the inlet port.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve
fixes.description: Miele inlet valves are reliable but do fail after many years.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the under-sink supply valve fully and straighten the inlet hose. Switch the dishwasher off at the unit, wait about 60 seconds, switch it back on, and start a fresh program — the fault clears once water flows normally.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good, Miele authorized service recommended for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heating issue — water not heating to target temperature
error_codes.detail: The control expected the wash water to reach its target temperature within a set time, but the temperature sensor never confirmed it, so the program halts with F15. Dishes come out poorly cleaned and unsanitized. A failed heating element is the leading cause; a faulty NTC sensor or heater relay are the alternatives.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test continuity — should read 15–30 ohms.

fixes.title: Test NTC sensor
fixes.description: At room temp: ~6k–7k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install appropriate replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run full program to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating component replacement on Miele requires care to maintain waterproof sealing.

error_codes.meaning: Flow meter fault — water quantity sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The flow meter measuring water entering the dishwasher has failed. Water quantity cannot be accurately controlled.

fixes.title: Clean flow meter
fixes.description: The flow meter can be removed and cleaned to clear scale deposits.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat the flow meter connector.

fixes.title: Replace flow meter
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't resolve it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, restart on short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele flow meters are repairable. Authorized Miele service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake fault at the start of a step
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher failed to take in the required water at the start of a program step, so it stops with F12. It is closely related to F13 (which faults later in the step) and almost always traces to the supply side — a closed or restricted tap, a kinked hose, or a blocked inlet screen.

causes.description: Supply valve closed or partially open

fixes.title: Open the supply tap
fixes.description: Confirm the under-sink valve is fully open and water pressure is adequate.

fixes.title: Straighten the inlet hose
fixes.description: Trace the hose from wall to unit for kinks.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixes.description: Unscrew the supply hose and clean the mesh screen at the inlet port.

fixes.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply is good but F12 stays, the valve has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the tap fully and straighten the hose, then switch off ~60 seconds, switch back on, and restart on a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the supply is confirmed good, Miele authorized service recommended for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Heater pressure switch fault
error_codes.detail: The control could not get a valid signal from the heater pressure switch — the safety switch that confirms water is circulating before the heater is energized — so it stops with F14 to protect the heating circuit. A blocked or split pressure hose, debris in the circulation path, or a failed switch are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Clean the filters and sump
fixes.description: Remove and rinse the filter combination so the circulation path is clear.

fixes.title: Inspect the pressure hose
fixes.description: Check the hose to the pressure switch for kinks, splits, or trapped water.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure switch
fixes.description: If hose and path are clear, the switch has likely failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the filters, check the pressure hose, switch off ~60 seconds, then restart on a short program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pressure-switch and heating-circuit work is best handled by Miele authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Heating circuit fault — heater relay or temperature sensor
error_codes.detail: The control expected the heater to bring the water to temperature but the heating circuit did not respond as expected, so it stops with F24. The fault sits in the heating circuit — most often the heater relay on the board, the NTC temperature sensor, or the heating element itself.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Confirm the temperature sensor reads in range (~14.5k ohms cold).

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Check element continuity — open means failed.

fixes.title: Board/relay diagnosis
fixes.description: A failed heater relay on the control needs board-level diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off ~60 seconds and restart on a full program to confirm heating.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heater relay and element work require care with the waterproof sealing — Miele authorized service recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Circulation pump / frequency converter fault
error_codes.detail: The control detected a fault driving the circulation pump (the pump that sprays water through the arms during the wash), often reported by its frequency converter, so the program stops with F78 and dishes are left unwashed. A blocked or jammed pump impeller, a wiring fault, or a failed converter/pump are the usual causes.

causes.description: Circulation pump impeller jammed by debris

fixes.title: Clear debris from the sump
fixes.description: Remove the filters and check the sump and spray-arm feed for glass, seeds, or labels.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the unit for several minutes to clear a transient drive fault.

fixes.title: Replace the circulation pump
fixes.description: If the impeller is jammed/failed or the converter is faulty, the pump needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the sump and switch off several minutes. If F78 persists, the circulation pump needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Circulation pump replacement on Miele dishwashers is a sealed-system job for authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Slide-shutter positioning fault during water intake
error_codes.detail: The control could not confirm the correct position of the slide shutter / diverter that directs water during intake, so it stops with F84. It is closely related to the F63 water-path fault — the diverter valve, its position sensing, or the control electronics are the usual causes; excess detergent foam can also confuse the positioning.

fixes.title: Use the correct detergent dose
fixes.description: Excess or wrong detergent can foam and disrupt the shutter — run a rinse and dose correctly.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the unit for several minutes to clear a transient fault.

fixes.title: Diverter/board diagnosis
fixes.description: Persistent F84 needs the diverter valve and control electronics checked by Miele.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run a rinse with the correct detergent dose and power off several minutes. If F84 persists, the diverter/control needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Diverter and control-board faults on Miele dishwashers require authorized service.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet valve fault — water supply issue
error_codes.detail: Speed Queen washers are extremely reliable commercial-grade machines for home use. E3 indicates the water inlet valve is not functioning correctly or water pressure is insufficient.

causes.description: Supply valve closed or partially closed

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both hot and cold valves are fully open.

fixes.title: Test water pressure
fixes.description: Minimum 20 PSI required.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity (200–500 ohms).

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve if failed
fixes.description: Speed Queen parts are available from authorized dealers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After fixing supply, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen machines are built for longevity. If valve fails, genuine SQ parts are recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump fault — drainage problem
error_codes.detail: The control ran the drain pump but the water level didn't drop within the allowed time, so the cycle stops with E8 and the tub stays full. On these commercial-grade machines the most common root cause is lint, coins, or small items lodged in the pump filter or impeller rather than an actual pump motor failure.

fixes.title: Access pump filter
fixes.description: Speed Queen front-loaders have a pump filter. Drain water and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper installation.

fixes.title: Replace drain pump
fixes.description: Speed Queen pumps are commercial-grade but can fail.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Authorized Speed Queen service is available through their dealer network.

error_codes.meaning: Motor feedback error — motor control fault
error_codes.detail: The motor control system has detected incorrect feedback from the drive motor. This is a serious error typically requiring technical diagnosis.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check motor belt (if applicable)
fixes.description: Some SQ models use a belt drive.

fixes.title: Contact Speed Queen service
fixes.description: Speed Queen machines are built to last 25 years — motor faults typically warrant authorized service.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E2 returns, Speed Queen authorized service is recommended.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen's commercial heritage means complex motor faults are best handled by their dealer service network.

error_codes.meaning: Lid switch fault (top-load models)
error_codes.detail: The lid switch is not detecting the lid as closed. The washer will not spin with an open or faulty lid switch.

causes.description: Lid switch actuator bent or broken

fixes.title: Inspect lid switch actuator
fixes.description: The plastic tab that depresses the switch when the lid closes. Check for cracks.

fixes.title: Test lid switch
fixes.description: Depress switch manually and listen for click. Test with multimeter for continuity.

fixes.title: Replace lid switch
fixes.description: Unscrew switch from lid frame and install replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, close lid firmly and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new lid switch doesn't resolve E5, the wiring harness to the switch needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — control board fault
error_codes.detail: The main controller cannot communicate with a sub-module or the user interface. This typically indicates a control board issue.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check wiring harness connectors
fixes.description: Reseat all connectors on the control board.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If power cycle and connections don't resolve it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E1 returns, contact Speed Queen authorized service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Speed Queen machines are repairable for life — authorized dealers stock parts for decades.

error_codes.meaning: Heating error — dryer not heating correctly
error_codes.detail: The Speed Queen dryer's heating system is not functioning correctly. The drum may turn but produces no or insufficient heat.

causes.description: Tripped high-limit thermostat from blocked vent

fixes.title: Check and clean vent
fixes.description: A blocked vent causes high-limit thermostat to trip permanently.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test continuity — 10–20 ohms expected.

fixes.title: Test high-limit thermostat
fixes.description: Test for continuity — failed thermostat reads open.

fixes.title: Replace failed part
fixes.description: Install genuine Speed Queen replacement.
fixes.warning: Unplug before accessing heating components.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer (or switch off its breaker) for 60 seconds, clear the lint screen and vent, then restore power and run a timed-dry test cycle to confirm the dryer heats normally before loading laundry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If vent, element, and thermostat all check out, the control board heater relay needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor failure — temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control board is reading an open or out-of-range resistance from the exhaust thermistor, so it can no longer regulate drum temperature and shuts the cycle down to prevent overheating. An open-circuit thermistor is the cause in most cases; a loose connector or chafed wiring accounts for the rest.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacing the thermistor or reseating its connector, unplug the dryer (or flip the breaker) for 60 seconds, restore power, and run a short heated cycle to confirm the code stays clear.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Drain failure — cycle cannot complete
error_codes.detail: Amana washers are built on the Whirlpool platform. F9E1 means the washer failed to drain within the expected time. Pump filter cleaning resolves this in most cases.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access lower front panel. Drain water and clean filter.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm high loop and no kinks.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: Test motor if filter is clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run Drain & Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pump replacement needed if motor tests failed.

error_codes.meaning: Low water supply
error_codes.detail: The control opened the fill valve but the pressure sensor never saw enough water arrive within the allowed fill time, so the cycle pauses or aborts. A partly closed supply valve or clogged inlet hose screens cause most cases and can be fixed without any parts.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screens
fixes.description: Clean mesh screens at washer inlet ports.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully and check the hoses for kinks, then unplug the washer for 60 seconds. Plug back in and press Start — F8E1 clears once the washer detects a normal fill.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply good and error persists, inlet valve replacement needed.

error_codes.meaning: Excessive suds
error_codes.detail: The washer detected excess foam in the tub and paused the cycle to run a suds-reduction routine before continuing. Using too much detergent, or any non-HE detergent in this high-efficiency machine, is the cause in nearly every case — it is not a mechanical fault.

fixes.title: Wait
fixes.description: Washer resumes automatically.

fixes.title: Use less detergent
fixes.description: HE detergent only, 1 tablespoon max.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Wait — the washer runs a suds-reduction rinse and resumes by itself, usually within 30 minutes. To clear it faster, press Cancel, run a Rinse & Spin with no detergent, then restart the load.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: SUD rarely needs service. Call a pro only if it appears on every load despite correct HE detergent dosing — repeated false SUD codes point to a failing pressure sensor or a partial drain restriction trapping foam.

error_codes.meaning: Control board failure
error_codes.detail: The main electronic control board failed its internal self-check, so the dryer halts and won't respond normally. A failed onboard component (relay or capacitor) is the usual root cause, often triggered by a power surge — sometimes a long power cycle clears a transient fault.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 15 minutes.

fixes.title: Replace board
fixes.description: If F01 returns.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 15 minutes. If F01 returns, board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board replacement requires photographing all wiring first.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure
error_codes.detail: The control is reading an open or out-of-range circuit from the exhaust thermistor, so it can't measure outgoing air temperature or regulate heat safely — the dryer stops or won't heat. A failed thermistor is the most common cause; it's an inexpensive part in the exhaust duct.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: Room temp: ~10k–12k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Located in exhaust duct.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, unplug 1 minute then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature alarm — temperatures above safe range
error_codes.detail: The refrigerator has detected temperatures above the safe threshold for food storage. Check the door seals and fans.

causes.description: Door left open or seal worn

fixes.title: Check door seal
fixes.description: Check all door gaskets for proper sealing.

fixes.title: Clean condenser coils
fixes.description: Vacuum condenser coils at back/bottom of unit.

fixes.title: Check fans
fixes.description: Confirm both evaporator and condenser fans are running.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close doors and wait 4 hours. H1 clears when temperature normalizes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fans run, coils are clean, and door seals are good but temperature stays high, compressor or refrigerant needs inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet fault — insufficient fill
error_codes.detail: Fisher & Paykel washers are known for their innovative top-load designs. E4 indicates a water supply issue preventing proper filling.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Confirm both valves fully open.

fixes.title: Clean inlet screen
fixes.description: Remove hose and clean mesh filter at washer port.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully and clean the inlet screens. Unplug the washer for 60 seconds to clear the fault, plug back in, and restart the cycle — the code clears once the machine fills at a normal rate.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel authorized service available for valve replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain pump fault — drainage problem
error_codes.detail: The drain pump is not functioning correctly. Water is not draining from the drum.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Locate the filter access point on your model. Drain water and clean.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Verify routing and no kinks.

fixes.title: Replace pump
fixes.description: FP pumps are quality but do fail.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, run spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel has an extensive authorized service network.

error_codes.meaning: Lid switch fault — lid not detected as closed (top-load models)
error_codes.detail: The lid switch is not detecting the lid as closed. The washer will not agitate or spin for safety.

fixes.title: Inspect switch actuator
fixes.description: Check the plastic tab that triggers the lid switch.

fixes.title: Test lid switch
fixes.description: Multimeter test for continuity when depressed.

fixes.title: Replace lid switch
fixes.description: Standard repair on top-load models.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, close lid and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If switch replacement doesn't resolve E6, wiring harness to the switch needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Motor controller fault
error_codes.detail: The motor controller has detected a fault in motor operation. Fisher & Paykel uses a brushless motor system that is generally very reliable.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes.

fixes.title: Check for overloading
fixes.description: Remove half the load and retry.

fixes.title: Contact F&P service
fixes.description: Motor controller faults typically need authorized service diagnosis.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If E2 returns, authorized service required.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel motor systems are proprietary — authorized service is recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Not draining — drain fault
error_codes.detail: Fisher & Paykel's DishDrawer dishwashers use a unique drawer design. U4 indicates the unit cannot drain water within the expected time.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or improperly routed

fixes.title: Clean the filter
fixes.description: Remove and clean the filter in the bottom of the drawer.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: Confirm proper routing with high loop.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose clear, pump may have failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing, restart cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Fisher & Paykel DishDrawer repair requires model-specific parts.

error_codes.meaning: Flood protection activated — water in base
error_codes.detail: The flood protection system has detected water in the base tray of the dishwasher.

fixes.title: Tilt to drain base
fixes.description: Tilt unit forward to drain base tray (power off).
fixes.warning: Turn off power before tilting.

fixes.title: Inspect door seal
fixes.description: Check gasket for damage.

fixes.title: Check hose connections
fixes.description: Inspect all internal connections.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Drain base, fix leak, dry. Code clears when sensor resets.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If no visible leak, internal crack needs F&P authorized inspection.

error_codes.meaning: Heating failure — water not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The DishDrawer's heating system has failed. Water is not heating to the programmed temperature.

fixes.title: Test heating element
fixes.description: Disconnect and test continuity.

fixes.title: Test temperature sensor
fixes.description: At room temp: ~6k–7k ohms.

fixes.title: Replace failed component
fixes.description: Install appropriate replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, run full program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heating component replacement requires careful waterproof resealing.

error_codes.meaning: Water inlet error — no water detected
error_codes.detail: The Haier washer is not detecting water entering the drum during the fill cycle.

fixes.title: Check supply valves
fixes.description: Both hot and cold valves must be fully open.

fixes.title: Check inlet hose
fixes.description: No kinks from wall to machine.

fixes.title: Test inlet valve
fixes.description: Test solenoid coils for continuity.

fixes.title: Replace valve if failed
fixes.description: Install new valve.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open both supply valves fully and straighten the inlet hose. Unplug the washer for 60 seconds to clear the code, plug back in, and restart the cycle from the beginning.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply is confirmed good, inlet valve replacement resolves this.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — water not draining
error_codes.detail: The control board started the drain pump but the pressure sensor still detected water in the tub after the allowed drain time, so the cycle pauses with E2. In most cases lint, coins, or debris clogging the pump filter is the root cause, followed by a kinked drain hose.

fixes.title: Clean pump filter
fixes.description: Access via lower front panel. Drain and clean.
fixes.warning: Have towels ready.

fixes.title: Check drain hose
fixes.description: No kinks; proper high-loop installation.

fixes.title: Test drain pump
fixes.description: If filter and hose are clear.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the drain filter and hose, unplug the washer for 60 seconds, then run a Spin-only cycle to confirm the tub empties before starting a full wash.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pump motor fails, replace it.

error_codes.meaning: Door not closed or door lock failure
error_codes.detail: The door lock circuit never reported a closed-and-locked state, so the control halts the cycle before filling — the washer will not run with an unverified door. Usually the door simply isn't latched fully or laundry is trapped in the seal; a failed lock solenoid is the common hardware cause.

fixes.title: Check for items in seal
fixes.description: Look for clothing caught in the gasket.

fixes.title: Close door firmly
fixes.description: Push firmly until definite click.

fixes.title: Replace door lock
fixes.description: If latch or solenoid is worn.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door, clear any laundry from the gasket, then push the door closed firmly until it clicks. Unplug the washer for 60 seconds, restore power, and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If door closes but E4 persists, lock solenoid has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Motor error — drive motor fault
error_codes.detail: The control board lost proper feedback from the drive motor — it commanded the drum to turn but tachometer or current readings came back wrong, so the cycle stops to protect the motor. Most often the motor control board has failed; worn motor windings or a loose wiring harness are the other usual culprits.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Reduce load
fixes.description: Remove items — overloading can trigger motor errors.

fixes.title: Test motor
fixes.description: Access motor and test winding continuity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes, reduce load, restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor control board or motor replacement requires professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Water level sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The water level pressure sensor is sending readings outside the expected range, so the control can't tell how much water is in the tub and stops the cycle to avoid overfilling or running the heater dry. A kinked or disconnected pressure hose is common, but a failed sensor is the leading cause.

fixes.title: Check pressure hose
fixes.description: Locate the small hose connecting the tub to the pressure sensor. Ensure it's not kinked, cracked, or disconnected.

fixes.title: Test pressure sensor
fixes.description: Disconnect hose from sensor and gently blow — should hear a click from the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace pressure sensor
fixes.description: If sensor doesn't respond to air pressure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After reseating the pressure hose or replacing the sensor, unplug the washer for 60 seconds to clear the fault from memory, plug back in, and start a new cycle to verify the level reading.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If pressure hose and sensor are fine, control board sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor failure — freezer compartment
error_codes.detail: The NTC thermistor in the freezer has failed. Temperature regulation may be compromised.

fixes.title: Test thermistor
fixes.description: At 32°F/0°C: approximately 16k ohms. At room temp: approximately 6k–8k ohms.

fixes.title: Check connector
fixes.description: Reseat thermistor connector.

fixes.title: Replace thermistor
fixes.description: Clip old sensor off evaporator coil and install new one.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, allow 24 hours temperature stabilization.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If new thermistor and error persists, control board sensor input failed.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost sensor failure
error_codes.detail: The defrost sensor has failed. Defrost cycles may not complete correctly, leading to frost buildup.

fixes.title: Manually defrost
fixes.description: Unplug and allow complete defrost before diagnosing.

fixes.title: Test sensor
fixes.description: Should read 5k–16k ohms depending on temperature.

fixes.title: Replace sensor
fixes.description: Unclip from evaporator and install new sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, allow 24 hours for normal operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If defrost sensor is new and correct, defrost heater or control board needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — control board fault
error_codes.detail: The main control board has lost communication with a sub-module or display board.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug 10 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check all connectors
fixes.description: Inspect and reseat all wiring connectors at the control board.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If power cycle and connections don't resolve it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 10 minutes. If F6 returns, control board replacement needed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If board replacement doesn't resolve F6, wiring harness between boards has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Door cannot close — door detected open or unlatched during operation
error_codes.detail: The washer senses the door is not fully closed or the latch released mid-cycle. Unlike dE, dC usually points at a physical closing problem rather than the lock electronics. Common after the boot seal shifts or laundry is pinched in the door.

causes.description: Laundry pinched in the door seal
Boot gasket protruding into the door path

fixes.title: Clear the door seal
fixes.description: Open the door and pull any fabric out of the gasket fold, then close firmly.

fixes.title: Check hinge and striker alignment
fixes.description: Look for door sag; tighten hinge screws and confirm the striker enters the latch slot squarely.

fixes.title: Replace the latch assembly
fixes.description: If the door closes but won't register, replace the latch/lock unit.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door fully, reclose it firmly, and press Start/Pause to resume the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door visibly closes flush but dC persists, the latch microswitch or board input needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalanced load — drum cannot reach spin speed safely
error_codes.detail: Ub is Samsung's unbalance variant (the display renders it like 'Ub'). The washer attempts redistribution tumbles, and if the load remains lopsided it aborts the spin to protect the suspension and bearings. Frequent Ub on normal loads indicates worn suspension.

causes.description: Items bunched to one side of the drum
Single heavy item like a comforter or rug
Washer not level on the floor

fixes.title: Redistribute the load
fixes.description: Pause the cycle, open the door, and spread items evenly around the drum.

fixes.title: Level the washer
fixes.description: Use a bubble level on the top panel and adjust the leveling feet until solid on all four corners.

fixes.title: Inspect suspension dampers
fixes.description: If Ub appears on every load, the shock absorbers/dampers are likely worn and need replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Redistribute the laundry, close the door, and press Start/Pause to resume.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If even small balanced loads trigger Ub, have a technician check the suspension and drum bearing.

error_codes.meaning: Unbalance error variant — repeated failed redistribution attempts
error_codes.detail: U6 appears on some Samsung display panels in place of UE/Ub when the unbalance correction routine fails several times in a row. The machine drains and stops rather than spinning a lopsided load at high RPM.

fixes.title: Untangle and redistribute
fixes.description: Separate tangled sheets or straps and arrange items evenly around the drum.

fixes.title: Split bulky loads
fixes.description: Wash comforters alone with two or three towels to balance, or split very large loads in half.

fixes.title: Check suspension and leveling
fixes.description: Rock the cabinet — any wobble means feet need adjustment; constant U6 means dampers are tired.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Open the door, rebalance the load, and restart the spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent U6 with balanced loads points to suspension or bearing wear that needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Water temperature sensor (thermistor) error — heater thermistor out of range
error_codes.detail: tE1 means the thermistor on the heating element circuit is reading open, shorted, or wildly out of range. The board can't verify water temperature, so heated cycles abort. Resistance should read roughly 10k–40k ohms depending on water temperature.

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to clear a transient sensor glitch, then retry a warm cycle.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect power, access the heater at the rear lower tub, and measure thermistor resistance — an open or near-zero reading confirms failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before probing the heater circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor/heater assembly
fixes.description: On many Samsung models the thermistor is part of the heating element assembly; replace as a unit if integrated.
fixes.warning: Mains-voltage heater circuit — confirm power is disconnected before touching terminals.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes and restart. If tE1 returns on heated cycles, the sensor needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a pro if you're not comfortable testing resistance on the mains-powered heater circuit.

error_codes.meaning: Duct/outlet temperature sensor error (washer-dryer combo models)
error_codes.detail: tE2 flags the secondary temperature sensor — on combo and steam models this is the duct or condensing-path thermistor rather than the wash heater sensor. The board sees an open, short, or implausible reading from it.

causes.description: Lint or moisture on the sensor (combo models)

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes; a one-off reading glitch will clear.

fixes.title: Inspect the sensor harness
fixes.description: Trace the wiring to the duct/secondary sensor and reseat the connector; look for green corrosion or pinched wires.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Replace the secondary thermistor
fixes.description: Measure resistance at room temperature; replace if open or shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the unit for 5 minutes and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor tests good and tE2 persists, the main board needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser/steam temperature sensor error
error_codes.detail: tE3 is the third thermistor fault in Samsung's tE family, tied to the condensing duct or steam-generator sensor on steam-equipped and combo models. An out-of-range reading stops steam and drying functions to prevent overheating.

causes.description: Scale buildup on the steam generator sensor

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and rerun the cycle to rule out a transient fault.

fixes.title: Reseat the sensor connector
fixes.description: Access the steam generator/condenser area and reseat the thermistor plug; clean any scale or lint off the sensor body.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Test resistance and replace if open/shorted; sensors are inexpensive and usually two screws plus a plug.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart. Avoid steam cycles until the sensor is fixed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If steam cycles keep faulting after sensor replacement, the steam generator itself needs professional service.

error_codes.meaning: Heating phase — estimated time over 2 hours (display status, often mistaken for an error)
error_codes.detail: 2H is usually not a fault: Samsung displays '2H' (or 3H/4H) when the estimated remaining time exceeds 99 minutes, typically during sanitize or heavy heated cycles. It becomes a problem only if the washer hangs at 2H far longer than the cycle should take, which indicates slow heating.

causes.description: Normal display for a long heated cycle
Scaled or weak heating element extending heat-up time
Thermistor reading low so the heater runs long

fixes.title: Let the cycle run
fixes.description: If the time eventually counts down below 99 minutes, the display is behaving normally — no repair needed.

fixes.title: Compare cycle duration
fixes.description: Time a sanitize cycle against the manual's stated length; more than ~30 minutes over suggests a weak heater.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Measure element resistance (roughly 10–30 ohms); replace if open or badly scaled.
fixes.warning: Mains-voltage heater — unplug the washer before testing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: No reset needed for normal operation; if stuck, unplug for 5 minutes and rerun a shorter cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If heated cycles never finish or water stays cold, have the heater and board tested professionally.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error (older Samsung models) — water not draining in time
error_codes.detail: On older Samsung washers with two-character displays, E2 is the legacy drain fault, equivalent to 5C/5E on newer models. The pressure sensor still detects water after the drain phase timed out.

fixes.title: Clean the debris filter
fixes.description: Open the lower front access panel, drain residual water via the small hose, and clean the filter thoroughly.
fixes.warning: Several liters of water may come out — have towels and a shallow pan ready.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Straighten any kinks and confirm the hose isn't pushed more than 6 inches into the standpipe.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: Run a Spin/Drain cycle and listen for the pump hum; a silent or grinding pump needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before replacing the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the blockage, run a Rinse+Spin cycle to confirm draining.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear but water still stands in the drum, the pump or pressure switch needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Overflow / water level error (older Samsung models)
error_codes.detail: E3 on legacy Samsung washers indicates the water level rose above the safe limit — the overflow equivalent of OC/OE on newer displays. The washer drains automatically as a safety measure. The usual culprits are a leaking inlet valve or a faulted pressure sensor.

causes.description: Water inlet valve not closing fully
Pressure sensor or hose fault giving a false low reading
Blocked pressure chamber causing late level detection

fixes.title: Watch for fill with power off
fixes.description: Turn the washer off at the wall with supply valves open; if the drum still fills, the inlet valve is mechanically stuck and must be replaced.

fixes.title: Check the pressure hose
fixes.description: Inspect the thin air hose from the tub to the pressure sensor for kinks, splits, or detergent sludge.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the top panel.

fixes.title: Replace the pressure sensor
fixes.description: If the hose is clear and the valve closes, the level sensor is misreporting and should be swapped.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the water supply, unplug for 5 minutes, then restart. Keep supply valves closed until the cause is found.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Overfilling risks flooding — if you can't identify the cause quickly, shut off the supply valves and call a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Air circulation error — insufficient airflow detected through the drum
error_codes.detail: The dryer's sensors detect that air is not moving through the system at the expected rate. Samsung dryers monitor airflow indirectly via temperature rise; restricted exhaust makes the duct heat too quickly and trips 1 AC before a thermal cutoff blows.

causes.description: Clogged exhaust vent or duct run
Lint screen or screen housing packed with lint
Crushed or kinked flexible duct behind the dryer

fixes.title: Clean the lint screen and housing
fixes.description: Remove the screen, wash it with warm soapy water, and vacuum the slot it sits in.

fixes.title: Clean the full vent run
fixes.description: Disconnect the duct from the dryer and brush/vacuum the entire run to the outside hood; confirm the exterior flap opens freely.

fixes.title: Inspect the blower wheel
fixes.description: With power disconnected, remove the front panel and check the blower for lint wads, socks, or a wheel slipping on its shaft.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer (or shut off gas and unplug) before opening the cabinet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the airflow restriction, then unplug for 5 minutes and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the vent run is long, routed through walls, or 1 AC returns after cleaning, hire a vent cleaning service or technician.

error_codes.meaning: Clog warning — exhaust duct restriction detected (early D80/D90 indicator)
error_codes.detail: Clg is Samsung's clog-level warning shown on models that later report D80/D90/D95 percentages. It means the Vent Sensor test measured significantly restricted exhaust airflow. The dryer may still run, but drying times stretch and the fire risk rises with lint accumulation.

causes.description: Lint buildup in the exhaust duct
Exterior vent hood blocked or flap stuck
Duct run too long or with too many elbows
Crushed transition duct behind the dryer

fixes.title: Clean the exhaust duct
fixes.description: Disconnect the duct and clean the full run with a rotary brush kit; vacuum from both ends.

fixes.title: Check the exterior hood
fixes.description: Go outside while the dryer runs — strong, warm airflow should exit. Clear the flap of lint, nests, or screening.

fixes.title: Rerun the Vent Blockage test
fixes.description: Use the dryer's vent check mode (empty drum, Time Dry per the manual) to confirm the restriction is cleared.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, run the vent blockage test cycle; the warning clears when airflow is restored.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If Clg persists after a thorough cleaning, the in-wall duct run may need professional vent service.

error_codes.meaning: No gas — burner failed to ignite (gas models)
error_codes.detail: The dryer's igniter glowed but the flame sensor never detected ignition, or gas flow is absent. Samsung gas dryers retry ignition a few times, then lock out with nG. The most common causes are a closed gas valve, a failed igniter, or a worn flame sensor.

causes.description: Gas supply valve closed or supply interrupted

fixes.title: Confirm gas supply
fixes.description: Verify the shutoff valve behind the dryer is open and other gas appliances in the home work.

fixes.title: Observe the igniter
fixes.description: With the front panel removed, start a heat cycle and watch the burner: no glow means a failed igniter; glow without flame points to gas valve coils.
fixes.warning: Gas appliance — shut off the gas valve and unplug before any disassembly; if you smell gas, stop and ventilate.

fixes.title: Replace flame sensor or valve coils
fixes.description: If the igniter glows and shuts off without flame, replace the valve coils; if the flame lights then drops, replace the radiant flame sensor.
fixes.warning: Do not bypass safety sensors. Leak-test fittings with soapy water after reassembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore gas supply, unplug the dryer for 5 minutes, and restart a heated cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Any persistent gas smell, or uncertainty handling gas fittings, means stop and call a qualified gas appliance technician.

error_codes.meaning: Duct sensor blockage — vent/moisture sensor reading abnormal due to restricted exhaust
error_codes.detail: PS indicates the duct-mounted sensor detects a blockage condition or its own circuit is faulted. In practice it almost always traces to lint loading on the sensor or in the exhaust path, with sensor failure a distant second.

fixes.title: Clean the sensor and duct
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer, locate the sensor in the internal exhaust duct, and gently clean it along with the surrounding duct.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Clean the full vent run
fixes.description: Brush out the duct from the dryer to the exterior hood to eliminate the restriction triggering the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace the duct sensor
fixes.description: If cleaning doesn't clear PS, test continuity and replace the sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning, unplug for 5 minutes and restart the dryer.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If PS returns after sensor cleaning and a full vent cleanout, have the sensor circuit and board tested.

error_codes.meaning: Noise sensor error — abnormal sound/vibration signal from the wash system
error_codes.detail: Samsung dishwashers with a noise (knock) sensor monitor pump and motor sound signatures. nE means the sensor detected abnormal noise — often cavitation from low water, debris in the pump, or a faulty sensor circuit itself.

causes.description: Debris (glass, bone, pit) in the circulation pump
Low water level causing pump cavitation

fixes.title: Inspect the sump and filters
fixes.description: Remove the lower rack, filters, and check the sump for hard debris; clear anything around the impeller area.
fixes.warning: Glass shards may be present — wear gloves.

fixes.title: Verify the fill level
fixes.description: Start a cycle and check water reaches the base of the heating area; weak fill means inlet valve or supply problems causing cavitation noise.

fixes.title: Check or replace the noise sensor
fixes.description: With power off, reseat the sensor connector; replace the sensor if the error persists with a clean, properly filling machine.
fixes.warning: Unplug or switch off the breaker before removing panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off at the breaker for 60 seconds, then rerun a cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Grinding or screeching from the pump along with nE means pump replacement — a job best left to a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Pump/zone wash fault — distribution motor or pump circuit error
error_codes.detail: PC indicates a fault in the zone-wash distribution system or pump circuit — the board detects the distribution motor isn't reaching its commanded position, or current draw consistent with corrosion or binding. Long-term water exposure corroding the pump/motor connector is a known failure pattern.

causes.description: Corroded connector or harness at the pump assembly

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch off at the breaker for 5 minutes — a position-sense glitch can clear.

fixes.title: Inspect connectors under the tub
fixes.description: Tip the unit (water supply off, drained) and inspect the pump and distribution motor connectors for green corrosion; clean or repair the harness.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at the breaker before working under the dishwasher.

fixes.title: Replace the distribution motor
fixes.description: If the valve doesn't rotate during a cycle, replace the distribution (zone wash) motor assembly.
