error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off power at the breaker for 5 minutes, then restart a normal cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Corroded wiring under the tub or a suspected board fault should be handled by a professional.

error_codes.meaning: Drain-related sensor fault — drainage not confirmed by the water level sensor
error_codes.detail: C5 on Samsung dishwashers (notably Waterwall and some DW80 series) flags a drainage/level-sensing inconsistency: the level sensor still sees water after the drain phase. It overlaps heavily with 5C in cause — check drainage first, sensor second.

causes.description: Drain hose kinked or air-gap/disposal port blocked

fixes.title: Clean filters and sump
fixes.description: Remove and rinse the filter stack and clear the sump of food debris.

fixes.title: Check the drain path
fixes.description: Confirm the hose has a high loop, isn't kinked, and the garbage disposal knockout plug was removed if newly installed.

fixes.title: Test the drain pump
fixes.description: Listen for the pump at the end of cycle; a silent pump with standing water needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at the breaker before replacing the pump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off at the breaker for 60 seconds after clearing the drain path, then rerun a cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water still stands after the filter, hose, and pump check out, the level sensor and board need professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Fan speed error — a cooling fan is not running at the commanded RPM
error_codes.detail: The control board reads feedback pulses from each fan motor. 26E means a fan (commonly the freezer or condenser fan on affected models) is reporting no rotation or wrong speed. Ice buildup seizing the evaporator fan is the classic cause.

causes.description: Fan blade obstructed by debris or shifted shroud

fixes.title: Force defrost or full thaw
fixes.description: Unplug the unit (food relocated) and let it thaw 8–24 hours, or run forced defrost from the diagnostic menu to melt ice off the fan.

fixes.title: Check the fan for obstruction
fixes.description: Remove the relevant panel and confirm the blade spins freely by hand; clear debris and reseat the shroud.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing interior panels.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: If the blade is free but doesn't run when powered, swap the fan motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the fault, unplug for 2 minutes to reset the board and let the unit restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If fans repeatedly ice over, the defrost system needs professional diagnosis — fan replacement alone won't fix it.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker fill tube heater error
error_codes.detail: 33E indicates the heater that keeps the ice maker's water fill tube from freezing has failed open or its circuit is faulted. Without it, the fill tube ices shut and the ice maker stops producing even though everything else works.

causes.description: Harness or connector fault to the heater
Ice blockage stressing the heater circuit

fixes.title: Thaw the fill tube
fixes.description: Power off the ice maker, and gently warm the fill tube area (hair dryer on low) to clear any ice plug.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before using any heat source near plastic parts; keep the dryer moving.

fixes.title: Test the heater circuit
fixes.description: With the unit unplugged, measure continuity across the fill tube heater; an open reading confirms failure.

fixes.title: Replace the heater/fill tube assembly
fixes.description: On most models the heater comes with the fill tube assembly.
parts: Samsung ice maker fill tube heater ($20–$50)

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press and hold Power Freeze + Power Cool (or per your model's manual) for 8–10 seconds to reset, or unplug for 2 minutes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the heater tests fine but 33E persists, the control board output should be checked professionally.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker temperature sensor error
error_codes.detail: The thermistor inside the ice maker assembly is reading open, shorted, or out of range. The board uses this sensor to time harvest cycles, so ice production halts. Samsung's known ice maker icing issues often kill this sensor or its connector.

fixes.title: Defrost the ice maker compartment
fixes.description: Use forced defrost mode (Fridge + a model-specific button) or thaw manually to clear frost around the sensor.

fixes.title: Inspect the connector
fixes.description: Unplug the unit, remove the ice maker, and check the harness plug for corrosion; clean and reseat.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the ice maker.

fixes.title: Replace the ice maker assembly
fixes.description: Samsung sells the sensor only as part of the ice maker assembly on most models.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 2 minutes after the repair; the code clears once the sensor reads in range.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Recurring icing that keeps killing the sensor indicates a sealing/defrost defect best handled by a technician familiar with Samsung ice maker service bulletins.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker (ice room) fan error — ice compartment fan not rotating
error_codes.detail: 41E means the fan that circulates cold air through the door-mounted ice room isn't spinning or returns no feedback. Frost jamming the fan blade is by far the most common cause on French-door Samsung models.

causes.description: Ice room fan frozen in frost
Air duct gasket leak letting moist air in and frosting the fan

fixes.title: Force defrost
fixes.description: Enter forced defrost (Fd) from the diagnostic panel to melt frost in the ice room, or thaw the compartment manually.

fixes.title: Check the fan
fixes.description: With power off, remove the ice maker and confirm the fan blade turns freely; clear ice and debris.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the ice maker assembly.

fixes.title: Replace the ice room fan
fixes.description: If free but not running, replace the fan motor; also reseat the duct gaskets to slow re-frosting.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After defrosting, unplug for 2 minutes; the code clears when fan feedback returns.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If frost rebuilds within weeks, the ice room sealing needs professional repair — repeated fan swaps won't hold.

error_codes.meaning: Ice room damper/sensor error — ice compartment not maintaining temperature
error_codes.detail: 42E reports a fault in the ice room circuit — typically the damper that meters cold air into the door ice compartment, or its associated sensor, isn't responding. Ice melts and refreezes into clumps, and production drops off.

causes.description: Ice room damper frozen or failed
Ice room sensor out of range
Frost accumulation in the air duct

fixes.title: Defrost the ice room
fixes.description: Run forced defrost or thaw manually to free the damper and clear duct frost.

fixes.title: Test damper operation
fixes.description: In diagnostic mode, command the damper and listen for movement; a silent damper after thawing has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing door/ice room panels.

fixes.title: Replace the ice room sensor
fixes.description: If the damper works, test the sensor's resistance and replace if open or shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 2 minutes after repair to reset the control board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Door-mounted ice room disassembly is fiddly and parts are model-specific — call a pro if you're unsure of your exact model's layout.

error_codes.meaning: Compressor lock error — compressor failed to start or stalled
error_codes.detail: 84E means the inverter board detected the compressor drawing locked-rotor current or failing to reach speed. This is one of the more serious Samsung refrigerator codes: causes range from a failing inverter board (cheap) to a seized compressor (expensive).

causes.description: Loose or burnt connector between inverter and compressor
Low line voltage or power quality issue

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to reset the inverter; if the compressor restarts and runs, monitor for recurrence.

fixes.title: Inspect inverter connections
fixes.description: With the unit unplugged, check the inverter board (near the compressor) for burnt connectors or bulged components; reseat all plugs.
fixes.warning: Capacitors on the inverter board can hold charge — wait several minutes after unplugging.

fixes.title: Replace the inverter board
fixes.description: An inverter swap is the cost-effective first repair; if the new board still throws 84E, the compressor itself has failed.
fixes.warning: Mains voltage present at the inverter — unplug before any work.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes and restore power. If 84E returns, do not keep power-cycling — diagnose the inverter.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new inverter board doesn't fix it, compressor replacement requires an EPA-certified sealed-system technician — get a quote before committing, as it can approach replacement cost.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor error — water temperature sensor reading out of range
error_codes.detail: The washer's heating thermistor is returning an open, shorted, or implausible reading, so the control board cannot regulate water temperature. Heated and steam cycles abort. The thermistor is usually integrated into or mounted beside the heating element at the bottom rear of the tub.

causes.description: Loose or corroded connector at the heater

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes to clear a transient sensor fault, then rerun a warm cycle.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, access the heater through the rear panel, and measure thermistor resistance — roughly 30k–50k ohms at room temperature on most LG models; open or zero confirms failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before probing the mains-powered heater circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Disconnect the sensor from the heater assembly and swap it; if integrated, replace the heating element assembly.
fixes.warning: Confirm power is disconnected before touching heater terminals.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes and restart. If tE returns on heated cycles, the sensor needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor still throws tE, the main control board's sensing circuit needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Overcurrent error — excessive current detected in the motor drive circuit
error_codes.detail: The main board's drive circuit detected current beyond safe limits while powering the direct-drive motor. This protects the board's IPM (inverter power module) from burning out. Causes range from a shorted stator winding to a failing board.

causes.description: Hall sensor fault confusing the drive timing
Wiring harness short between board and motor

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes; a single overcurrent trip from a momentary jam can clear.

fixes.title: Test the stator windings
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, remove the rear cover and rotor, and measure resistance between stator terminals — readings should be equal across all three pairs (typically 5–15 ohms); a shorted pair condemns the stator.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before removing the rotor; the rotor bolt is torqued tight.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: If the motor tests balanced and the harness is intact, the board's drive section has failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug and wait several minutes before handling the board — capacitors hold charge.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes and retry. Repeated F6 trips mean stop and diagnose — continued retries can destroy the board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Distinguishing a bad stator from a bad board requires meter work on live components — most owners should call a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Child lock active — controls are locked, not an error
error_codes.detail: CL is not a fault. The Child Lock feature is engaged, which disables the control panel so kids can't change settings mid-cycle. Many owners hit the button combination accidentally and assume the washer is broken because buttons stop responding.

causes.description: Stuck control panel button keeping the combo pressed
Control board glitch latching the lock

fixes.title: Deactivate Child Lock
fixes.description: Press and hold the Child Lock button (or the two buttons marked with the lock symbol, often Temp + Options) for 3–5 seconds until CL disappears.

fixes.title: Power cycle if unresponsive
fixes.description: Unplug for 60 seconds and try the unlock combination again after the panel reboots.

fixes.title: Check for a stuck button
fixes.description: Press each panel button — any that feel sticky or don't click may be holding the lock combo; clean around the button or replace the panel if jammed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the Child Lock button combination for 3–5 seconds; refer to the lock-symbol label on your control panel.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the panel stays locked after a power cycle and the correct unlock combo, the control panel or board needs service.

error_codes.meaning: Dry heater error — drying heater fault (washer-dryer combo models)
error_codes.detail: On LG washer-dryer combo units, dHE indicates the drying heater circuit failed to raise air temperature as expected, or its thermistor reads out of range. Wash functions usually still work; only the dry phase aborts. Lint restricting the condensing duct is a frequent contributor.

fixes.title: Clean the drying duct
fixes.description: Unplug the unit and clean lint from the air intake, condensing duct, and any accessible filter — restricted airflow overheats and trips the circuit.

fixes.title: Test the dry heater and thermal cutoff
fixes.description: With power disconnected, measure continuity across the drying heater and its thermal fuse; an open reading on either confirms the failure.
fixes.warning: Mains-voltage heating circuit — unplug the washer before testing.

fixes.title: Replace the duct thermistor
fixes.description: If the heater and fuse test good, measure and replace the duct temperature sensor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes and restart a dry cycle. If a thermal fuse blew, find the airflow cause before replacing it.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fuse keeps blowing after a thorough lint cleanout, a technician should check the blower and heater control relay.

error_codes.meaning: Leak detected (Aqua error) — water sensed in the base pan
error_codes.detail: LG washers with leak detection have a sensor in the base pan; AE means water reached it. The washer stops and may drain as a safeguard. The leak can be anywhere — hose clamps, the boot gasket, the dispenser, or a cracked tub — and gravity carries it to the pan.

causes.description: Loose internal hose clamp or split hose
Door boot gasket torn or folded
Drain pump housing or tub seal leaking

fixes.title: Dry the base pan
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, tip it back carefully or remove the lower panel, and dry the base pan so the sensor resets — then find the source before running it again.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before reaching into the base.

fixes.title: Run a test cycle and watch
fixes.description: With panels off and power restored, run a short cycle and watch with a flashlight for the drip source during fill, wash, and drain phases.
fixes.warning: Observe only — keep hands clear of moving and live parts.

fixes.title: Repair the source
fixes.description: Reseat hose clamps, replace a torn boot gasket, or replace the leaking pump housing as found.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Dry the base pan completely, fix the leak source, then unplug for 5 minutes before restarting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you can't locate the leak source after observing a full cycle, call a technician — running a leaking washer risks floor damage and electrical faults.

error_codes.meaning: No heat / airflow fault — dryer runs but cannot maintain heating
error_codes.detail: nF flags that the dryer detected inadequate heat rise, which on LG dryers is almost always an airflow problem rather than the heater itself. Restricted exhaust causes thermal cutoffs to cycle the heat off, and the board reports the no-heat condition.

causes.description: Lint filter or internal duct packed with lint
Thermal cutoff or high-limit thermostat tripped/failed
Heating element or gas ignition fault

fixes.title: Clean the lint filter and vent run
fixes.description: Wash the lint screen, then disconnect and brush out the full exhaust duct to the exterior hood.

fixes.title: Test thermostats and thermal cutoff
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer (and shut off gas on gas models), then check continuity on the high-limit thermostat and thermal cutoff near the heater; replace any open one along with fixing the airflow cause.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the cabinet; thermal cutoffs blow because of airflow problems — fix the cause too.

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: On electric models, measure element continuity (about 10–20 ohms); replace if open.
fixes.warning: 240V circuit — confirm the dryer is unplugged before touching heater terminals.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the vent and any failed cutoff, unplug for 5 minutes and run a timed dry cycle to verify heat.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Gas-model ignition faults (igniter, coils, flame sensor) are best handled by a technician comfortable with gas appliances.

error_codes.meaning: Control board error — internal board fault detected on power-up or mid-cycle
error_codes.detail: E2 on LG dryers indicates the main control board failed an internal self-check, often a relay feedback or memory fault. It can be triggered by a power surge and sometimes clears with a hard reset; a persistent E2 means the board itself is failing.

causes.description: Power surge or brownout corrupting board state
Loose harness connector at the board

fixes.title: Hard reset
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes (not just off at the panel) to fully discharge and reboot the board.

fixes.title: Reseat board connectors
fixes.description: With power disconnected, open the control housing and reseat every harness plug on the main board; look for heat discoloration.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the control housing.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: If E2 returns after a reset and connector check, the board needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Handle the new board by its edges and unplug the dryer before swapping.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes and restore power. If E2 reappears immediately, stop power-cycling and diagnose the board.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the error returns after a board swap, there's a deeper harness or component fault that needs professional tracing.

error_codes.meaning: Motor or board sensing error — drum motor not detected running
error_codes.detail: E3 indicates the control board isn't seeing expected feedback from the drive motor circuit shortly after starting. The drum may hum and stall or not move at all. A seized motor, a failed start capacitor (on belt-drive models), or board relay failure are the usual suspects.

causes.description: Belt broken so the motor trips its overload
Harness fault between board and motor

fixes.title: Check the belt and drum
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and turn the drum by hand — it should rotate with even resistance; a free-spinning drum means a broken belt.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening the cabinet.

fixes.title: Test the motor
fixes.description: With the belt off, spin the motor pulley by hand (it should turn freely) and check winding continuity; a seized or open motor needs replacement.

fixes.title: Replace the main board
fixes.description: If the motor and belt are good, the board's motor relay has likely failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before swapping the board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, then restart. A humming-then-stop pattern means stop retrying — you'll overheat the motor windings.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor replacement requires significant disassembly of the bulkhead and drum — call a pro if you haven't done dryer teardowns before.

error_codes.meaning: Tub clean reminder — time to clean the drum and condenser (not a fault)
error_codes.detail: tCL is a maintenance reminder, not an error. After a set number of cycles, LG dryers (especially heat-pump and condenser models) display tCL to prompt a drum/condenser cleaning routine. The dryer continues to work normally while it's shown.

fixes.title: Run the cleaning routine
fixes.description: Clean the lint filter, wipe the drum, and on condenser/heat-pump models rinse the condenser or clean its filter per the manual.

fixes.title: Clear the reminder
fixes.description: Press and hold the button indicated in your manual (commonly the Tub Clean or a labeled combo) for 3 seconds to acknowledge and reset the counter.

fixes.title: Power cycle if it persists
fixes.description: Unplug for 60 seconds if the display doesn't clear after acknowledging.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the indicated button for about 3 seconds after cleaning to reset the reminder counter.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: No service needed unless the reminder refuses to clear after cleaning and a power cycle — then the board's counter logic needs attention.

error_codes.meaning: Door close / child lock indicator — door not latched or controls locked
error_codes.detail: On LG dishwashers CL most often means the door isn't fully latched when you press Start; on some models the same display indicates the Control Lock is active. Either way, it's an operational state rather than a component failure in most cases.

causes.description: Rack or item protruding and blocking the door

fixes.title: Close the door firmly
fixes.description: Check that racks are fully pushed in and no tall item blocks the door, then press it closed until the latch clicks.

fixes.title: Release the Control Lock
fixes.description: Press and hold the button marked with the lock symbol (often Rinse or Dual Zone) for 3 seconds until CL clears.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch
fixes.description: If the door clicks but CL persists, the latch microswitch may be worn — test continuity and replace the latch assembly.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at the breaker before removing the inner door panel.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Hold the lock-symbol button 3 seconds to release the control lock, or relatch the door and press Start.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door physically latches and the lock is released but CL remains, the latch switch or board input needs service.

error_codes.meaning: Vario motor error — wash arm diverter motor not reaching position
error_codes.detail: The vario (diverter) motor alternates water between the upper and lower spray arms. nE means the board commanded a position change but never saw the position-switch confirmation. Food debris jamming the diverter disc is the usual cause; the motor itself fails less often.

fixes.title: Clean the sump and diverter
fixes.description: Remove the lower rack, spray arm, and filter stack, then clear debris from the sump and around the diverter opening.

fixes.title: Test the vario motor
fixes.description: Disconnect power, access the motor under the tub, and check that its cam rotates in diagnostic mode or with bench power; replace if seized.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at the breaker before tipping the unit or working underneath.

fixes.title: Check the position switch
fixes.description: If the motor turns but nE persists, the position microswitch on the assembly is misreporting — it usually comes with the motor assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power off at the breaker for 60 seconds after cleaning, then run a short cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Under-tub access requires pulling and tipping the dishwasher — call a pro if the unit is hard-plumbed or tightly installed.

error_codes.meaning: Bubble/leak error — excessive suds or air detected in the wash system
error_codes.detail: bE indicates the dishwasher detected excessive foam or air bubbles in the circulation system — usually from the wrong detergent or far too much of it. Suds make the pump cavitate and can trick the leak sensor. On some models bE escalates to or accompanies the leak error if foam reaches the base pan.

causes.description: Wrong detergent used (hand soap or laundry detergent)
Detergent overdose or rinse-aid leak inside the tub
Suds residue from a previous overdose
Leak sensor tripped by foam in the base pan

fixes.title: Clear the suds
fixes.description: Cancel the cycle and run one or two rinse-only cycles with no detergent; a tablespoon of vegetable oil in the tub helps knock down stubborn foam.

fixes.title: Use only dishwasher detergent
fixes.description: Verify everyone in the household uses automatic dishwasher detergent and the correct dose for your water hardness.

fixes.title: Dry the base pan
fixes.description: If bE persists with no suds, disconnect power and check the base pan under the tub for foam or water on the leak sensor; dry it out and find any drip source.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power at the breaker before removing the toe-kick and reaching into the base.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Run a rinse-only cycle to flush suds, then power off at the breaker for 60 seconds and retry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If bE appears with proper detergent and a dry base pan, the leak/bubble sensor or board needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Refrigerator fan error — fresh-food compartment fan not running
error_codes.detail: The fan that circulates air through the refrigerator (fresh food) compartment isn't returning rotation feedback. Without it, the fridge section warms while the freezer may stay cold. Ice from a defrost problem seizing the fan is the most common cause.

causes.description: Fan iced over from defrost system failure
Fan blade obstructed or shroud shifted

fixes.title: Thaw the fan
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator and let the compartment thaw 8–24 hours (or use forced defrost mode) to free an iced fan.

fixes.title: Check the fan blade
fixes.description: Remove the interior rear panel and confirm the blade spins freely; clear obstructions and reseat the shroud.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing interior panels.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: If the blade is free but the motor doesn't run when powered, replace it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After thawing or repair, plug back in; the code clears when fan feedback returns.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan ices over again within weeks, the defrost heater, sensor, or board needs professional diagnosis — the fan is the symptom, not the cause.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer sensor error — freezer compartment thermistor out of range
error_codes.detail: Er SS reports that the freezer temperature sensor is reading open, shorted, or implausible. The board falls back to timed cooling, so temperatures drift — food may partially thaw or freeze too hard. The sensor itself is cheap; corroded connectors are equally common.

causes.description: Frost intrusion at the sensor mount

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 2 minutes; a transient reading fault will clear on reboot.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the unit, locate the sensor clipped to the freezer evaporator area, and measure resistance — it should change smoothly as the sensor warms in your hand; open or fixed readings confirm failure.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the freezer rear panel.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Swap the thermistor and seal the harness splice well against moisture.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 2 minutes after the repair; the error clears once the sensor reads in range.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor still throws Er SS, the main board input circuit needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer sensor fault — freezer thermistor circuit open or shorted
error_codes.detail: FS is the short-display variant of the freezer sensor error shown on models with two-character panels. The board cannot read freezer temperature, so defrost timing and compressor control degrade and freezer temperature swings.

causes.description: Connector corrosion in the cold/moist environment
Harness pinched or broken behind panels

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 2 minutes and watch the display after restart.

fixes.title: Inspect and reseat the connector
fixes.description: Unplug the unit, remove the freezer back panel, and check the sensor plug for green corrosion; clean and reseat.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing interior panels.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Test resistance and replace if open or shorted; heat-shrink the splice to keep moisture out.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 2 minutes; FS clears automatically once the sensor circuit reads normally.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Repeat FS errors after sensor replacement point to a harness break inside the cabinet — professional tracing recommended.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error — main board and display board not talking
error_codes.detail: CO indicates the dispenser/display board and the main control board lost serial communication. Cooling usually continues on last-known settings, but the panel and dispenser stop responding. A loose door-hinge harness connector is the classic LG cause, since the display wiring flexes every time the door swings.

causes.description: Loose or damaged harness in the door hinge

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 2 minutes — communication re-handshakes on boot and a transient glitch clears.

fixes.title: Inspect the hinge harness
fixes.description: Unplug the unit, remove the top hinge cover, and check the door wiring loom for broken conductors or unseated plugs; reseat firmly.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before opening hinge covers and connectors.

fixes.title: Replace the display board
fixes.description: If the harness is intact, the display board is the more common failure of the two boards.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 2 minutes and restore power. If CO returns immediately, the fault is hardware, not state.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Distinguishing a bad display board from a bad main board requires swap testing — a technician can do this in one visit.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer (ice room) fan fault — icing fan circuit failure
error_codes.detail: 1F flags the ice-room/freezer fan circuit on LG models with door-mounted ice makers: the fan that pulls cold air into the icing compartment isn't reporting rotation. Ice production slows or stops and cubes may clump. Frost seizing the small fan is by far the most common cause.

causes.description: Ice room fan frozen in frost
Duct gasket leak letting moist air frost the compartment

fixes.title: Defrost the ice compartment
fixes.description: Unplug the unit and thaw the ice room (door open, ice bin out) or run forced defrost from the test mode.

fixes.title: Check the fan
fixes.description: With power off, remove the ice maker and confirm the small fan blade turns freely; clear ice and reseat the duct gaskets.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the ice maker assembly.

fixes.title: Replace the ice room fan
fixes.description: If thawed and free but not running, replace the fan motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After thawing or repair, plug back in; the code clears when the fan reports rotation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Recurring frost in the ice room means an air-leak or defrost defect that fan swaps won't fix — call a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Cycle fault — control detects a mismatch or stuck cycle state
error_codes.detail: On VMW (vertical modular washer) platforms F2E3 indicates the control board has detected an invalid cycle state, often caused by a control/UI mismatch or corrupted programming. It can also appear after a board replacement with the wrong part installed.

causes.description: Loose harness between UI and main control

fixes.title: Power cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to allow the control to reboot and clear a transient state fault.

fixes.title: Check UI-to-control harness
fixes.description: Remove the console and verify the ribbon/harness connectors are fully seated.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the console.

fixes.title: Replace main control board
fixes.description: If the fault returns after a reboot and the harness is good, the control board is at fault.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes, then restart a cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the code returns immediately after a power cycle, have a tech verify board part numbers and replace the control.

error_codes.meaning: Shifter fault — the drive shifter cannot move between agitate and spin
error_codes.detail: VMW top-load washers use a shifter assembly to switch the transmission between agitate and spin modes. F7E5 means the control commanded a shift but the shifter position sensor never confirmed it.

fixes.title: Power cycle and retry
fixes.description: Unplug 5 minutes; a transient sensor glitch sometimes clears.

fixes.title: Inspect shifter harness
fixes.description: Tilt the washer back and check the connector at the shifter under the tub for corrosion or damage.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before tilting it back.

fixes.title: Replace shifter assembly
fixes.description: The shifter (actuator) is mounted on the gearcase bottom — a common, inexpensive failure on VMW washers.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes. If the fault persists, the shifter needs service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you're not comfortable tilting the machine and working underneath it, call a tech — shifter replacement is a 30-minute job for a pro.

error_codes.meaning: Motor fault — the drive motor is not responding correctly
error_codes.detail: The control board has detected a problem with the drive motor circuit: either the motor failed to start, drew abnormal current, or the wrong motor is detected. On VMW machines this often traces back to the motor windings or its harness.

fixes.title: Check motor harness
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, tilt it back, and verify the motor connector is fully seated with no burnt pins.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before working underneath.

fixes.title: Test motor windings
fixes.description: Measure resistance across motor windings per the tech sheet; open or shorted windings mean motor replacement.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: If motor and harness test good, the motor drive circuit on the board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes and restart. A persistent F7E6 requires motor circuit diagnosis.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor and board diagnosis requires a multimeter and the tech sheet — call a pro if you don't have both.

error_codes.meaning: Motor unable to reach target speed
error_codes.detail: The motor is running but cannot reach the RPM the control commanded, usually during spin. Mechanical drag, an overloaded drum, or a worn drive component prevents the basket from accelerating.

causes.description: Worn splutch cam kit or drive belt
Item jammed between basket and tub

fixes.title: Reduce the load
fixes.description: Remove half the items — heavy waterlogged loads commonly trigger this — and retry a drain & spin.

fixes.title: Check for obstructions
fixes.description: Rotate the empty basket by hand; grinding or heavy resistance suggests a jammed item or worn bearing.

fixes.title: Replace splutch/belt
fixes.description: On belt-drive VMW models a glazed belt or worn splutch cam kit slips under load.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug 5 minutes, reduce the load, and run a Drain & Spin cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the empty basket won't spin up either, internal drive components need professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure (short circuit)
error_codes.detail: The exhaust thermistor reads as a short — the control sees an impossibly high temperature and shuts the dryer down to prevent overheating.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and measure resistance — a reading near 0Ω confirms a shorted sensor.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening panels.

fixes.title: Check for pinched wiring
fixes.description: Trace the sensor wires for spots where insulation has rubbed through against the cabinet.

fixes.title: Replace control board
fixes.description: Only if the sensor and wiring both test good.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes and retry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F23 with a good sensor means board or harness work — call a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Water level fault — float switch tripped or level reading invalid
error_codes.detail: The overfill float in the tub base has lifted, or the control is getting an invalid water-level signal. The dishwasher drains and refuses to fill to protect against flooding.

causes.description: Water in the base pan from a leak

fixes.title: Check the float
fixes.description: Remove the lower rack and verify the overfill float (front-left tub floor on most models) moves freely up and down.

fixes.title: Inspect the base for water
fixes.description: Remove the toe panel and look for standing water in the base pan — a leak there holds the float up.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before removing the toe panel.

fixes.title: Replace inlet valve or float switch
fixes.description: An overfilling valve or a switch that won't reset must be replaced.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clear the float, dry the base pan, and restore power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water keeps appearing in the base pan, there's an internal leak that needs professional repair.

error_codes.meaning: Power outage indicator — power was interrupted
error_codes.detail: PO is not a fault: the refrigerator lost power long enough that food temperatures may have risen. The display shows PO (sometimes with the warmest temperature reached) until you acknowledge it.

causes.description: Loose power connection at the outlet

fixes.title: Acknowledge the alert
fixes.description: Press Confirm/OK (or the Measured Fill / Options button per your model) to clear the PO display.

fixes.title: Check food temperatures
fixes.description: If the displayed max temperature exceeded 40°F for over 2 hours, inspect perishables before keeping them.

fixes.title: Verify the power source
fixes.description: If no outage occurred, check the breaker and outlet — repeated PO with no outage suggests a supply problem.
fixes.warning: Don't use an extension cord for a refrigerator.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press the confirm button on the display to acknowledge and clear PO.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If PO appears repeatedly with no household outages, have an electrician check the circuit.

error_codes.meaning: Communication failure between control boards
error_codes.detail: The main control board and the user interface (or a secondary board) have stopped talking to each other. Displays may freeze, and cooling may continue on defaults while the error stands.

causes.description: Wiring damage in the door hinge loom

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator (or flip the breaker) for 5 minutes to force both boards to re-handshake.

fixes.title: Reseat board connectors
fixes.description: Check the harness connectors at the main board (behind the rear lower panel) and the UI board.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before opening panels.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If the code returns, a tech can identify which board dropped off the communication bus.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes; if CF returns, board diagnosis is required.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board-level diagnosis on a CF code is best left to a tech — the fridge keeps cooling in the meantime on most models.

error_codes.meaning: Ice maker fault — ice maker not cycling correctly
error_codes.detail: The control has detected that the ice maker failed to complete its harvest cycle or its sensor reads out of range. Ice production stops or becomes intermittent while the rest of the refrigerator works normally.

causes.description: Ice jammed in the mold or ejector
Water supply to ice maker restricted

fixes.title: Clear jammed ice
fixes.description: Remove the ice bin and check the mold/ejector for fused cubes; let any blockage melt or remove it gently.

fixes.title: Power cycle the ice maker
fixes.description: Switch the ice maker off for a minute (or unplug the fridge 5 minutes) and let it attempt a fresh harvest cycle.

fixes.title: Replace the ice maker assembly
fixes.description: Modern ice makers are replaced as a unit when the motor module or thermistor fails.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn the ice maker off and back on, or unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new ice maker still throws 18 C, the harness or control board needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Main electronic control board failure
error_codes.detail: Kenmore dryers on the Whirlpool platform use F01 to flag an internal fault on the main control board. The board has detected corrupted memory or a failed internal check. A power cycle occasionally clears a one-off glitch, but a repeating F01 means the board itself has failed.

causes.description: Loose wiring harness connection at board

fixes.title: Power cycle the dryer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to reset the control board, then retry a cycle.

fixes.title: Inspect board connections
fixes.description: Remove the rear or top panel and reseat all harness connectors on the main control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening any panel — the board carries 240V.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: If F01 returns after a reset, replace the board.
fixes.warning: Discharge static before handling the new board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 5 minutes, plug back in, and start a cycle. If F01 returns, the board needs replacement.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you're not comfortable working around the 240V terminal block and control electronics, have a technician swap the board.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad / user interface stuck button fault
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dryers, F02 indicates a stuck key or a failed user interface (touchpad) assembly. The console is sending a continuous button press to the control. It can also surface after moisture gets behind the console.

causes.description: Loose ribbon cable between console and control

fixes.title: Press each button
fixes.description: Cycle through every console button a few times to free a stuck key, then power cycle.

fixes.title: Reseat the console ribbon cable
fixes.description: Open the console and reseat the ribbon connector between the touchpad and control board.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer first.

fixes.title: Replace the user interface
fixes.description: If the fault persists with cables seated, replace the touchpad/console assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes and press each console button several times before restarting.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a pro if replacing the user interface doesn't clear F02 — the main board may be misreading the keypad.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure — reading open
error_codes.detail: F22 on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dryers means the exhaust (outlet) thermistor is reading an open circuit. The control can't measure exhaust temperature, so the dryer won't heat properly or will stop. The thermistor is a cheap part mounted on the blower housing.

causes.description: Broken wire or loose connector to thermistor

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer, remove the lower access panel, and measure the thermistor — about 10k ohms at room temperature.
fixes.warning: Unplug before testing.

fixes.title: Check the wiring
fixes.description: Inspect the harness from the thermistor to the control board for chafed or broken wires.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: If it reads open or far off spec, replace it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes after replacing the thermistor; the code clears on the next successful cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor still produces F22, the main control board input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Outlet thermistor failure — reading shorted
error_codes.detail: F23 is the companion code to F22 on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dryers: the exhaust thermistor is reading a short circuit (resistance too low). The control sees an impossibly high temperature and shuts down heating. Same part, same location as F22.

causes.description: Pinched harness wire shorting to chassis

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Measure resistance — near 0 ohms means shorted; should be ~10k ohms at room temperature.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before testing.

fixes.title: Inspect the harness
fixes.description: Look for pinched or melted wires between the blower housing and the control board.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Swap the sensor if shorted.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes after the repair and run a timed-dry cycle to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the thermistor tests fine and wiring is intact, have a tech evaluate the control board.

error_codes.meaning: Moisture sensor fault
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dryers, F28 indicates the moisture sensor circuit has failed or lost communication. Auto-dry cycles can't sense dampness, so clothes come out wet or the cycle ends early. The sensor bars are inside the drum on the lint screen housing.

fixes.title: Clean the sensor bars
fixes.description: Wipe the two metal strips inside the drum with rubbing alcohol — dryer sheet residue insulates them.

fixes.title: Check sensor wiring
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and verify the harness from the sensor to the control is intact and seated.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

fixes.title: Replace the moisture sensor
fixes.description: If cleaning and wiring checks don't help, replace the sensor assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the sensor bars, unplug for 5 minutes, and run an auto-dry cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor still throws F28, the main control board needs diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Communication failure between control boards
error_codes.detail: F70 on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dryers means the main control board and the user interface board have stopped communicating. It often appears after a power event. Either board, or the harness between them, can be at fault.

causes.description: Loose harness between UI and main board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 10 minutes — a surge glitch often clears.

fixes.title: Reseat the communication harness
fixes.description: Open the console and rear panel and reseat the connectors at both boards.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening panels.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: If reseating doesn't help, the UI board is the more common failure; replace it, then the main board if needed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes. If F70 returns immediately at power-up, a board has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board-vs-board diagnosis is easiest with a tech's diagnostic mode access — call a pro if the reset doesn't stick.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure during the cycle
error_codes.detail: PF on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dryers simply means power was interrupted mid-cycle — a blackout, tripped breaker, or loose cord connection. It's informational, but repeated PF codes with no outage point to a supply problem.

causes.description: Loose power cord at terminal block

fixes.title: Restart the cycle
fixes.description: Press Start to resume or restart the cycle — the code is just a notification.

fixes.title: Check the breaker
fixes.description: A 240V dryer uses a double-pole breaker; if one leg trips repeatedly, replace the breaker.
fixes.warning: Breaker panel work involves mains voltage — turn off the main and proceed only if qualified.

fixes.title: Inspect the cord and terminal block
fixes.description: Unplug and check that cord terminals are tight and free of scorching.
fixes.warning: Never inspect the terminal block with the dryer plugged in.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Start to clear PF and resume the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If PF repeats with no household outages, have an electrician check the circuit and receptacle.

error_codes.meaning: Restricted airflow detected
error_codes.detail: AF on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dryers means the dryer is sensing restricted exhaust airflow — long dry times and high internal temperatures follow. A clogged vent duct is the cause in the overwhelming majority of cases, and it's also a fire hazard.

causes.description: Lint-blocked lint screen or screen housing

fixes.title: Clean the lint screen and housing
fixes.description: Remove the screen, wash it, and vacuum down into the screen housing.

fixes.title: Clean the vent duct
fixes.description: Disconnect the dryer and brush/vacuum the full duct run to the exterior hood.

fixes.title: Replace crushed duct
fixes.description: Swap any crushed foil flex hose for rigid or semi-rigid metal duct.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the vent, unplug for 5 minutes and run a timed-dry cycle to confirm strong exhaust flow.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Call a vent-cleaning service for long or inaccessible duct runs — a fully blocked vent is a fire risk.

error_codes.meaning: User interface cannot communicate with control board
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool-platform Kenmore and Kenmore Elite dishwashers, F2E2 (shown as 2-2 on dial displays) means the touchpad/UI is not talking to the main control board. The console may be unresponsive or lights flash erratically.

causes.description: Loose ribbon/harness between UI and control

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Kill power at the breaker for 5 minutes and retest.

fixes.title: Reseat the door console connectors
fixes.description: Open the inner door panel and reseat the UI-to-control harness.
fixes.warning: Turn off the breaker before opening the door panel.

fixes.title: Replace the UI board
fixes.description: If communication doesn't restore, the UI assembly is the more common failure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off the breaker for 5 minutes; if F2E2 returns at power-up, a board or harness has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If both boards seem suspect, a tech with diagnostic mode access can isolate which side failed.

error_codes.meaning: Float switch tripped — overfill detected
error_codes.detail: F8E4 (8-4) on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dishwashers means the overfill float switch has activated. Either there genuinely is too much water in the tub, or the float is stuck up on debris. The dishwasher will run the drain pump to protect against flooding.

fixes.title: Free the float
fixes.description: Remove the lower rack, lift and drop the float dome (front left of tub floor) and clean debris under it.

fixes.title: Check the inlet valve
fixes.description: If the tub fills while the dishwasher is off, the valve is leaking through — replace it.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply before replacing the valve.

fixes.title: Use correct detergent dose
fixes.description: Excess or wrong detergent makes suds that lift the float — use dishwasher-specific detergent only.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Free/clean the float, cancel the cycle to drain, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the tub overfills with a clean float, have a pro replace the inlet valve and check the board.

error_codes.meaning: Float switch open — water level too low
error_codes.detail: F6E4 (6-4) on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dishwashers flags a float switch problem in the opposite direction: the control expects water but the float circuit reads as if the tub is empty or the switch is open. Wash performance drops or the cycle halts.

causes.description: Low household water pressure / slow fill

fixes.title: Verify the water supply
fixes.description: Confirm the supply valve under the sink is fully open and the line isn't kinked.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet valve screen
fixes.description: Shut off water, disconnect the fill line, and clean the mesh screen at the valve.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply first.

fixes.title: Test/replace the float switch
fixes.description: Check the switch for continuity as the float moves; replace if intermittent.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Cancel the cycle, confirm water supply, and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If supply and float check out, the inlet valve or control board needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Control board memory error — stuck relay or corrupted firmware
error_codes.detail: F1E1 (1-1) on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dishwashers indicates the main control board detected corrupted memory or a stuck relay output. A hard reset sometimes recovers it, but a recurring 1-1 usually means a failing board, often from moisture or a power surge.

fixes.title: Hard reset
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes, restore power, and run a short cycle.

fixes.title: Inspect the board
fixes.description: Remove the toe-kick panel and check the control board for burn marks or corrosion.
fixes.warning: Breaker off before removing panels — line voltage is present at the board.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If 1-1 returns after a hard reset, replace the board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Breaker off for 10 minutes; if F1E1 returns on the next cycle, the board has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board replacement involves line-voltage connections — call a pro if you're not comfortable in the toe-kick area.

error_codes.meaning: Wash pump communication / motor fault
error_codes.detail: Code 4-3 on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dishwashers means the control board has lost communication with the wash (circulation) pump motor, or the motor failed to start. Dishes come out dirty because nothing is spraying.

causes.description: Loose harness at pump or board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Breaker off 5 minutes — a one-off communication glitch may clear.

fixes.title: Check the pump harness
fixes.description: Tip out the dishwasher access and reseat the connector at the circulation pump and at the board.
fixes.warning: Breaker off before reaching under the tub.

fixes.title: Replace the circulation pump
fixes.description: If the motor hums or is silent with good wiring, replace the pump assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle at the breaker for 5 minutes and rerun a cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Pump replacement requires pulling the unit out from under the counter — most owners hand this to a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Door switch fault — door not detected as latched
error_codes.detail: Code 5-1 on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dishwashers means the control doesn't see the door latch switch close, or it sees it open mid-cycle. The cycle won't start, or stops when the latch reads open.

causes.description: Misaligned or worn door latch assembly

fixes.title: Firmly close and latch
fixes.description: Press the door firmly closed and restart — verify nothing in the racks blocks the door.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch assembly
fixes.description: Remove the inner door panel top screws and check the latch for cracks and switch click.
fixes.warning: Breaker off before opening the door panel.

fixes.title: Replace the latch
fixes.description: Swap the latch/switch assembly if the switch doesn't actuate cleanly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly and press Start; 5-1 clears when the latch reads closed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new latch doesn't fix it, the board's door-switch input needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: No water detected entering the tub
error_codes.detail: Code 6-1 on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dishwashers means the control opened the fill valve but didn't detect water entering. Usually a supply or inlet valve issue rather than a board problem.

fixes.title: Open the supply valve
fixes.description: Check the valve under the sink is fully open and the line uncrimped.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixes.description: Shut off water, disconnect the fill line at the valve, clean the mesh screen.
fixes.warning: Shut off the water supply first.

fixes.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixes.description: If the valve gets power (120V at fill) but no water passes, replace it.
fixes.warning: Live-voltage testing — only if qualified; otherwise replace on symptom.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore the water supply, cancel/drain, and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the supply is confirmed and the valve is new, have a pro check the flow meter and board.

error_codes.meaning: Heating element failure — water not reaching temperature
error_codes.detail: Code 7-1 on Whirlpool-platform Kenmore dishwashers means the heater circuit failed to raise water temperature within the expected time. Dishes come out wet and poorly sanitized. The heating element or its relay is the usual culprit.

causes.description: Failed heater relay on control board

fixes.title: Test the heating element
fixes.description: Breaker off; measure across the element terminals under the tub — roughly 10–30 ohms is normal, open means failed.
fixes.warning: Breaker off before touching element terminals.

fixes.title: Replace the element
fixes.description: If open, replace the loop element in the tub floor.

fixes.title: Check the board relay
fixes.description: If the element tests good, the heater relay on the control board has likely failed — replace the board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After repair, switch the breaker off for 5 minutes and run a normal cycle with heated dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you can't isolate element vs. relay with a multimeter, call a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer evaporator fan motor fault
error_codes.detail: On LG-platform Kenmore and Kenmore Elite refrigerators, ER FF means the freezer evaporator fan isn't spinning at the commanded speed. Without that fan, cold air doesn't circulate and both compartments warm up. Ice buildup around the fan blade is a very common trigger.

causes.description: Ice buildup blocking the fan blade

fixes.title: Force defrost / manual thaw
fixes.description: Unplug for several hours (or use the forced-defrost mode) to melt ice around the fan.

fixes.title: Inspect the fan
fixes.description: Remove the freezer back panel and check the blade spins freely; look for frost patterns indicating a defrost problem.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the evaporator panel.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: If the blade is free but the motor won't run, replace it.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes (or hours, if iced) and restore power; the code clears if the fan runs normally.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If ice keeps returning around the fan, the defrost system needs professional diagnosis.

error_codes.meaning: Icemaker fan motor fault
error_codes.detail: ER IF on LG-platform Kenmore refrigerators means the fan that cools the icemaker compartment (in-door ice models) isn't running correctly. Ice production slows or stops. Ice blockage and motor failure are the leading causes.

causes.description: Loose connector in the door hinge harness

fixes.title: Thaw the ice compartment
fixes.description: Empty the bin and let the ice room thaw with the door open (towels down), or unplug for a few hours.

fixes.title: Check the hinge harness
fixes.description: Inspect the wiring loom through the upper door hinge for broken conductors — a common LG-platform failure point.
fixes.warning: Unplug before probing harness connectors.

fixes.title: Replace the icemaker fan
fixes.description: If the fan won't spin when free of ice, replace the motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle for 5 minutes after thawing; ER IF clears once the fan reaches speed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Broken door-hinge harness wires require careful splicing or a harness kit — a good job for a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Ice room fan feedback error
error_codes.detail: ER 1F on LG-platform Kenmore Elite refrigerators reports missing feedback from the ice room fan circuit — the board commands the fan but sees no tachometer signal. Symptoms and fixes overlap heavily with ER IF; frost in the ice room duct is the most frequent cause.

causes.description: Frost blocking the ice room fan

fixes.title: Defrost the ice room
fixes.description: Unplug and let the ice compartment thaw fully; vacuum out meltwater and debris.

fixes.title: Verify fan operation
fixes.description: With panels off, confirm the fan spins freely and the connector is seated.
fixes.warning: Unplug the unit before removing ice-room panels.

fixes.title: Replace the fan assembly
fixes.description: Replace if the motor runs but reports no feedback, or doesn't run at all.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes after thawing and restoring connections.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the error returns after a fan replacement, the main board's feedback circuit needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Refrigerator compartment fan motor fault
error_codes.detail: ER RF on LG-platform Kenmore refrigerators flags the fresh-food compartment circulation fan. When it stalls, the fridge section warms while the freezer may stay cold. Like the other fan codes, icing and motor failure dominate.

fixes.title: Thaw and inspect
fixes.description: Unplug for several hours if icing is suspected, then check the duct and blade.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Remove the interior rear panel of the fridge section and reseat the fan plug.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator first.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: Swap the motor if it won't spin when unobstructed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle for 5 minutes; the code clears when fan feedback returns.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent icing behind the fridge panel points at a sealed-system or defrost issue — call a pro.

error_codes.meaning: Condenser fan motor fault
error_codes.detail: CF (or Er CF) on LG-platform Kenmore refrigerators means the condenser fan — the fan near the compressor at the bottom rear — isn't reporting correct speed. Without it the compressor overheats and cooling degrades quickly. Dust-clogged blades and failed motors are the usual causes.

causes.description: Loose connector at the fan or main board

fixes.title: Clean the condenser area
fixes.description: Unplug, remove the rear lower panel, and vacuum/brush the fan blade and condenser coil.
fixes.warning: Unplug before working near the compressor — components are hot and the start relay carries mains voltage.

fixes.title: Spin-test the fan
fixes.description: The blade should rotate freely by hand with no grinding.

fixes.title: Replace the condenser fan motor
fixes.description: If clean and free but not running, replace the motor.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes after cleaning; CF clears when the fan reports normal speed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan runs but CF persists, the main board's feedback circuit needs professional testing — and an overheating compressor shouldn't wait.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost heater fault — defrost did not complete
error_codes.detail: dH on LG-platform Kenmore refrigerators means the defrost cycle failed to terminate normally — the evaporator didn't reach the cutoff temperature within the allowed time. A failed defrost heater or defrost sensor lets frost choke the evaporator, eventually killing all cooling.

fixes.title: Test the defrost heater
fixes.description: Unplug, pull the freezer back panel, and measure the heater — open circuit means failed.
fixes.warning: Unplug before testing; the heater runs on mains voltage.

fixes.title: Test the defrost sensor
fixes.description: Check the sensor/thermal-fuse assembly clipped to the evaporator for continuity when cold.

fixes.title: Replace the heater
fixes.description: Replace if open; clear all frost before reassembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After the repair, unplug for 5 minutes; run a forced defrost to confirm termination.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Mains-voltage heater testing and full evaporator de-icing are tech territory if you've not done it before.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: ER FS on LG-platform Kenmore refrigerators means the freezer compartment thermistor reads open or shorted. The board can't regulate freezer temperature, so it falls back to a fixed duty cycle — temperatures drift. The sensor is cheap and accessible.

fixes.title: Reseat the sensor connector
fixes.description: Unplug and reseat the thermistor plug behind the freezer panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator first.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Measure resistance — it should change smoothly with temperature; open or 0 ohms means failed.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Swap the thermistor if out of spec.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle for 5 minutes after replacement; ER FS clears when the sensor reads in range.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor still errors, the main board input has failed — call a pro.

error_codes.meaning: Refrigerator temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: ER RS on LG-platform Kenmore refrigerators is the fresh-food companion to ER FS: the refrigerator compartment thermistor is open or shorted. The fridge section may run too warm or too cold while the board free-runs without sensor input.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Unplug the unit and reseat the sensor plug behind the fridge interior panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug first.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Verify resistance changes with temperature; open or shorted readings mean replacement.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Cheap part, simple clip-in replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Power cycle for 5 minutes after the swap.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent ER RS with a known-good sensor means board-level repair — hand it to a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic oven control (EOC) fault — runaway or internal failure
error_codes.detail: F1 on Kenmore ranges (built on both Frigidaire and Whirlpool platforms) is the classic electronic oven control failure code. The control has detected an internal fault or a runaway-temperature condition. On older units a flaky keypad can also trigger it.

causes.description: Oven temperature sensor drifting out of range

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Switch the breaker off for 5 minutes; an isolated glitch may clear.

fixes.title: Test the oven temperature sensor
fixes.description: Unplug/breaker off and measure the sensor — about 1080–1100 ohms at room temperature on most platforms.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before testing — the control area carries mains voltage.

fixes.title: Replace the EOC
fixes.description: If the sensor is in spec and F1 returns, replace the electronic oven control.
fixes.warning: Mains-voltage connections at the board — breaker off and verify dead before touching.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Breaker off for 5 minutes. If F1 returns at idle, the EOC has failed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F1 appears during a cycle and the oven keeps heating, shut off the breaker and call a pro — runaway heat is a safety issue.

error_codes.meaning: Oven over-temperature detected
error_codes.detail: F2 on Kenmore ranges means the control sees the oven temperature above the allowed limit — during bake, broil, or even at idle. A drifted temperature sensor is the most common cause; a welded-closed heating relay is the dangerous one.

causes.description: Stuck heating element relay on the control

fixes.title: Cut power if actively overheating
fixes.description: If the oven heats with the controls off, switch off the breaker immediately.
fixes.warning: A welded relay keeps mains power on the element — breaker off first.

fixes.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixes.description: Measure resistance (~1080–1100 ohms at room temp); replace if out of spec.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If the sensor is good, the relay/control has failed.
fixes.warning: Breaker off and verify dead before working at the board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Breaker off for 5 minutes after the repair; verify temperatures with an oven thermometer.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Any oven that heats on its own is a fire hazard — keep the breaker off and call a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: F3 on Kenmore ranges (Frigidaire and Whirlpool platforms alike) means the oven temperature sensor circuit reads open. The control can't measure cavity temperature and won't run bake or broil. The sensor probe at the upper rear of the cavity is the usual failure.

causes.description: Loose/burned connector behind the rear panel

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Breaker off; unplug the sensor and measure — about 1080–1100 ohms at room temperature, open means failed.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before pulling the rear panel.

fixes.title: Inspect the connector
fixes.description: Heat-damaged plugs behind the oven are common — repair or replace the harness plug if scorched.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Two screws inside the cavity, one plug behind — an easy swap.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore power after the swap; F3 clears when the sensor reads in range.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor still shows F3, the control board input circuit has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor shorted
error_codes.detail: F4 is the mirror of F3 on Kenmore ranges: the temperature sensor circuit reads shorted (resistance far too low). The control interprets this as an impossible reading and locks out heating. Same sensor, same easy replacement.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Breaker off; near-zero ohms at the sensor means it's shorted — replace it.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before testing.

fixes.title: Check the harness
fixes.description: Look for the sensor lead pinched against the cavity or rear panel sheet metal.

fixes.title: Replace the control if needed
fixes.description: With a good sensor and clean harness, replace the EOC.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore power after replacement; verify with a bake cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If sensor and harness both check good, board diagnosis is a job for a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Runaway temperature detected
error_codes.detail: F10 on Frigidaire-platform Kenmore ranges means the control detected runaway temperature — the oven heating beyond setpoint, sometimes even with the oven off. This is the most safety-critical range code: a shorted sensor fools the control, or a welded relay keeps the element powered.

causes.description: Welded relay on the control board

fixes.title: Kill power immediately
fixes.description: If the oven is heating uncommanded, switch off the breaker now and leave it off.
fixes.warning: Do not use the oven until repaired — uncommanded heating is a fire hazard.

fixes.title: Test the temperature sensor
fixes.description: Breaker off; measure the sensor (~1080–1100 ohms at room temp) and replace if out of spec.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If the sensor is in spec, the relay board/EOC has failed and must be replaced before the oven is used again.
fixes.warning: Mains voltage at the board — breaker off, verify dead.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Do not simply reset and resume — repair first, then restore the breaker and verify with an oven thermometer.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Treat F10 as a call-a-pro code unless you can confidently test the sensor and verify the relay — the failure mode is dangerous.

error_codes.meaning: Oven temperature sensor shorted (Frigidaire-platform models)
error_codes.detail: F31 appears on newer Frigidaire-platform Kenmore ranges and indicates a shorted oven temperature probe circuit — the modern equivalent of the legacy F4. The control blocks heating until the sensor circuit reads sane.

causes.description: Damaged harness between probe and control

fixes.title: Test the probe
fixes.description: Breaker off; unplug the probe connector and measure — ~1080–1100 ohms at room temp is healthy, near zero is shorted.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before pulling the rear panel.

fixes.title: Inspect the harness
fixes.description: Check for melted insulation where the lead passes near the cavity.

fixes.title: Replace the probe
fixes.description: Simple two-screw replacement inside the oven cavity.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore the breaker after replacement; F31 clears when the circuit reads normal resistance.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the probe and harness test fine, the control board needs professional replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Drain fault — clogged filter or blocked drain path
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher could not empty the tub within the expected time. On Electrolux dishwashers i20 (and the related i40/iF0 variants) almost always traces to a dirty filter assembly, a blocked drain hose, or a tired drain pump.

causes.description: Food debris in the drain pump

fixes.title: Clean the filters
fixes.description: Remove the lower rack, twist out the cylindrical fine filter and lift the coarse filter; rinse both under running water.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Verify the hose isn't kinked behind the unit and that the sink connection or air gap is clear.

fixes.title: Inspect/replace the drain pump
fixes.description: Power off, remove the filter, and check the pump impeller for glass or debris; replace the pump if the impeller is damaged or seized.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before reaching into the sump.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the filters, then press and hold Cancel to drain and restart the cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the tub still won't drain after cleaning filters and hose, the drain pump needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Leak detected — water in the base pan (anti-flood triggered)
error_codes.detail: Water has reached the base pan beneath the tub and tripped the anti-flood float switch. The dishwasher runs the drain pump continuously and blocks all other functions until the base is dry and the leak is found.

causes.description: Loose hose clamp or split internal hose

fixes.title: Cut power and dry the base
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker, remove the toe panel, and soak up the water in the base pan with towels.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before removing the toe panel — water and live wiring may be present.

fixes.title: Find the leak source
fixes.description: Restore power, run a short cycle with the toe panel off, and watch with a flashlight for drips from hoses, sump seal, or door corners.

fixes.title: Replace the leaking part
fixes.description: Tighten clamps or replace the failed hose or sump gasket.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Dry the base pan completely, then restore power — i30 clears once the float drops.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you can't find the leak source quickly, call a pro — running with an active leak risks floor damage.

error_codes.meaning: Wash pump electronics fault — circulation pump relay/triac error
error_codes.detail: The control board has detected a fault in the circuit that drives the circulation (wash) pump — typically a shorted triac or relay on the board, or a shorted pump motor pulling the circuit down.

causes.description: Failed triac/relay on the main control board
Water intrusion into the pump connector

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes; if i50 returns immediately, there is a genuine hardware fault.

fixes.title: Test the circulation pump
fixes.description: Power off and measure the wash motor windings for a short; a shorted motor will kill replacement boards too.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker before disconnecting the pump.

fixes.title: Replace the main control board
fixes.description: If the pump tests good, the board's drive circuit has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off power at the breaker for 5 minutes, then retry a cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: i50 requires meter work on the pump and board — most owners should call a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Water heating fault — water not reaching target temperature
error_codes.detail: The water did not heat to the target temperature within the allowed time, or the temperature sensor reports an implausible value. Dishes come out poorly washed and cycles run long before the code appears.

causes.description: Heater relay failure on the control board
Low fill preventing the heater from covering

fixes.title: Test the heater
fixes.description: Power off and measure the flow-through heater element — roughly 10–30Ω is normal; open circuit means replacement.
fixes.warning: Turn off power at the breaker — the heater runs on mains voltage.

fixes.title: Test the NTC sensor
fixes.description: Measure the temperature sensor and compare against the resistance chart in the service sheet.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If heater and sensor test good, the heater relay on the board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off power for 5 minutes and run a hot cycle to confirm the fault.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Heater circuit diagnosis involves mains voltage — call a pro unless you're comfortable with a multimeter and breaker safety.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator fan circuit failure
error_codes.detail: The evaporator fan in the freezer compartment is not running or not reporting its speed back to the control. Without that fan, cold air never circulates — the freezer may stay cold near the coil while the fresh-food section warms up.

causes.description: Fan blade iced up and stalled

fixes.title: Listen for the fan
fixes.description: With doors closed (press the door switch by hand), the evaporator fan should be audible from the freezer; silence points to the fan or ice.

fixes.title: Defrost if iced
