fixes.description: Unplug for 8–24 hours with doors open and towels down — ice locking the fan blade is common after a defrost system failure.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: Remove the freezer back panel and replace the evaporator fan motor.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the evaporator cover.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes; if SY EF returns, fan diagnosis is required.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan was iced over, the underlying defrost fault also needs fixing — a tech should check the full defrost circuit.

error_codes.meaning: Communication error between control boards
error_codes.detail: The main power board and the user interface/display board have lost communication. The display may go blank or freeze while the sealed system keeps running on its last known settings.

causes.description: Damaged wiring in the cabinet loom

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to force the boards to re-establish communication.

fixes.title: Reseat connectors
fixes.description: Unplug and reseat the communication harness at both the main board (rear, lower) and the display board.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before opening board covers.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: A tech can scope the comms line to determine which board is dead.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes and check whether the display recovers.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If SY CE returns after a power cycle and reseated connectors, board replacement is a job for a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: A compartment temperature sensor (thermistor) reads as an open circuit. The control can't see the real temperature in that zone and falls back to a default duty cycle, so temperatures drift.

fixes.title: Identify the sensor
fixes.description: The display usually pairs OP with the affected compartment; press the compartment selector to see which zone reports it.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge, access the sensor, and measure resistance — a healthy NTC reads a few kΩ to tens of kΩ depending on temperature; open means replace.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before accessing sensors.

fixes.title: Check the harness
fixes.description: Inspect the connector for corrosion from condensation before condemning the board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes; the code returns immediately if the circuit is still open.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor doesn't clear OP, the board input circuit needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature sensor short circuit
error_codes.detail: A compartment thermistor reads as a short circuit — the control sees an impossibly cold value. As with OP, the affected zone runs on default timing and temperatures become unreliable.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge and measure the suspect sensor — a near-0Ω reading confirms the short.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before accessing sensors.

fixes.title: Dry the connector
fixes.description: Condensation inside the connector can mimic a shorted sensor; dry and re-test.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Thermistors are inexpensive clip-in parts on most Electrolux models.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes after the repair to clear the code.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent SH with a good sensor means board-level diagnosis — call a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Defrost circuit fault
error_codes.detail: The automatic defrost system did not complete correctly — the defrost heater, bi-metal thermostat, or defrost sensor is faulty. Left alone, frost builds on the evaporator coil until airflow stops and the fresh-food section warms.

fixes.title: Check for frost buildup
fixes.description: Heavy frost on the freezer back panel confirms the defrost system isn't running.

fixes.title: Test heater and thermostat
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge, remove the evaporator cover, and check continuity on the heater (typically 20–60Ω) and the bi-metal (closed when cold).
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator first — the defrost heater is a mains-voltage component.

fixes.title: Replace the failed part
fixes.description: Replace the open heater, bi-metal, or out-of-spec sensor; manually defrost fully before reassembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 8–24 hours to fully defrost, then restore power and monitor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If frost returns within a couple of weeks of a manual defrost, have a tech test the complete defrost circuit.

error_codes.meaning: Runaway temperature detected
error_codes.detail: The electronic oven control sees the cavity temperature climbing past safe limits — a genuine runaway or a faulty temperature probe (RTD) lying to the control. The oven shuts the elements off and may latch the door on self-clean models.

causes.description: Welded/stuck relay on the oven control board

fixes.title: Cut power if the oven is heating uncontrolled
fixes.description: If the oven heats while set to off, turn off the breaker immediately.
fixes.warning: A genuine runaway is a fire risk — do not leave the oven powered unattended.

fixes.title: Test the RTD probe
fixes.description: Unplug the range and measure the probe — about 1080Ω at room temperature is normal; far off means replacement.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before testing — range circuits are 240V.

fixes.title: Replace the oven control board
fixes.description: If the probe is in spec, a stuck heating relay on the EOC is the cause.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes. If F10 returns or the oven self-heats, leave power off and repair before use.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Any oven that heats on its own must be repaired by a pro before further use.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted keypad key detected
error_codes.detail: The control senses one or more keypad keys held or shorted for too long. Often appears after cleaning the panel (moisture behind the membrane) or as the touchpad membrane ages.

causes.description: Loose keypad ribbon cable at the EOC

fixes.title: Power cycle and dry
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes; if the panel was recently cleaned, give it a few hours to dry out.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Pull the range forward, remove the rear control cover, and reseat the keypad ribbon at the EOC.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before removing the control cover.

fixes.title: Replace the touchpad/overlay
fixes.description: If a specific key stays shorted, replace the membrane keypad (on many models it comes with the EOC as an assembly).

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes, then test the keypad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F11 keeps returning after drying and reseating cables, the keypad or EOC needs replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Control board internal checksum/EEPROM error
error_codes.detail: The electronic oven control failed its internal memory self-check — stored calibration or program data is corrupted. Sometimes a hard reset rewrites defaults; otherwise the board is failing.

causes.description: Corrupted EEPROM data after a power surge

fixes.title: Hard reset
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes so the control fully discharges, then restore power and watch the display.

fixes.title: Check power quality
fixes.description: If F13 followed a storm or brownout, consider a surge protector rated for the range circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the EOC
fixes.description: A board that re-throws F13 after a hard reset has failing memory and must be replaced.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before replacing the board — 240V circuit.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes, then restore power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the code returns after a full power-down reset, board replacement is the only fix.

error_codes.meaning: Display/touch panel not communicating with the control
error_codes.detail: The EOC cannot detect or communicate with the display/touch panel assembly. The display may be blank, garbled, or unresponsive while the fault stands.

causes.description: Loose ribbon/harness between display and EOC

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes and let the boards re-handshake on power-up.

fixes.title: Reseat display connections
fixes.description: Remove the rear control cover and reseat every connector between the display assembly and the EOC.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the control housing.

fixes.title: Replace display or EOC
fixes.description: If reseating doesn't help, the display assembly is the more common failure; the EOC is next.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes and check the display on power-up.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Distinguishing a dead display from a dead EOC requires service-sheet testing — call a tech if a power cycle doesn't fix it.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch fault — latch motor did not reach position
error_codes.detail: On Electrolux ranges and wall ovens with motorized door locks, E11 indicates the latch motor ran but the lock switches never confirmed the commanded position. Self-clean cannot start, or the door stays locked after a clean cycle.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes — on power-up the control re-homes the latch motor, which often frees a stuck lock.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch mechanism
fixes.description: Remove the rear or top access cover and check the latch cam and switches for jams or burnt connectors.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before reaching into the latch area.

fixes.title: Replace the latch assembly
fixes.description: The motor, cam, and switches usually come as one assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes; the latch motor re-homes on power-up.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door remains locked after a power cycle, don't force it — call a tech to release the latch without breaking the door glass.

error_codes.meaning: Oven control board internal fault (EEPROM/communication error)
error_codes.detail: The electronic oven control failed its internal self-check — corrupted memory or a board-level communication fault. Amana ranges share the Whirlpool control platform, so the diagnosis mirrors Whirlpool F1-E0.

causes.description: Memory corruption after a power surge

fixes.title: Hard reset
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes so the control fully discharges, then restore power.

fixes.title: Check connectors
fixes.description: Pull the range forward and reseat the harness connectors on the control board.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the control housing — 240V circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If F1E0 returns after a hard reset, the board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes, then restore power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the code returns after a full power-down, board replacement by a tech is the fix.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted or stuck keypad detected
error_codes.detail: The control senses a keypad key held or shorted continuously. Moisture behind the membrane after cleaning is a common trigger; an aging touchpad is the other.

causes.description: Loose keypad ribbon at the control

fixes.title: Power cycle and dry
fixes.description: Kill power at the breaker for 5 minutes; if the panel was recently wiped down, let it dry a few hours.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Reseat the keypad ribbon connector at the oven control.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the console.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad
fixes.description: If a key remains stuck, replace the touchpad (often sold combined with the control board).

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes and retest the keypad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F2E0 after drying and reseating means keypad or board replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch fault — lock motor did not reach commanded position
error_codes.detail: The motorized door lock ran but the lock switches never confirmed the locked or unlocked position. Self-clean can't start, or the door stays locked after cleaning.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes — the latch re-homes at power-up and often frees itself.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch
fixes.description: Access the latch (top rear of the oven cavity on most ranges) and check for jammed cams or burnt switch connectors.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before reaching into the latch area.

fixes.title: Replace the latch assembly
fixes.description: Motor, cam, and switches are replaced as one unit.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes; the latch motor re-homes on power-up.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door stays locked after a power cycle, don't force it — call a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Touchpad/membrane shorted
error_codes.detail: The control detects a shorted key matrix on the touch panel. Buttons become unresponsive or the microwave beeps and shows F2 on its own, frequently after steam or cleaning moisture gets behind the panel.

causes.description: Loose ribbon cable at the control board

fixes.title: Unplug and dry
fixes.description: Unplug the microwave for a few hours so trapped moisture can evaporate.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon
fixes.description: With the unit unplugged, open the control housing and reseat the touchpad ribbon.
fixes.warning: Microwave capacitors hold a lethal charge even unplugged — do not probe near the high-voltage section.

fixes.title: Replace the touchpad
fixes.description: If keys stay dead, replace the membrane/control panel assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes (longer if moisture is suspected), then retest.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Microwaves contain a high-voltage capacitor that can kill even unplugged — most owners should hand F2 to a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Microwave relay failure detected
error_codes.detail: The control board has detected a fault in the relay circuit that switches the magnetron power — the relay is stuck or its feedback reads wrong. The oven may not heat, or may attempt to energize when it shouldn't.

causes.description: Failed power relay on the control board
Harness fault in the relay sensing circuit

fixes.title: Unplug immediately if it heats unbidden
fixes.description: A stuck relay that energizes the magnetron unexpectedly is a safety issue — unplug the unit.
fixes.warning: Unplug the microwave and do not use it until repaired.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes; if F6 returns, the relay fault is genuine.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: The relay is board-mounted; the whole control board is replaced.
fixes.warning: High-voltage capacitor hazard — board replacement is a technician job.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes. If F6 returns, stop using the microwave.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Any relay fault in a microwave should go straight to a tech — the high-voltage section is lethal.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted keypad detected
error_codes.detail: The oven control senses a key held or shorted continuously on the touch panel. KitchenAid ranges use the Whirlpool control platform; moisture behind the membrane or a worn touchpad are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Power cycle and dry
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes; allow several hours of drying if the panel was recently cleaned.

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Reseat the keypad ribbon at the oven control board.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the console — 240V circuit.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad
fixes.description: If specific keys remain stuck, replace the touchpad/console overlay.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes and retest.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F2E0 keeps returning after drying and reseating, replace the keypad or control.

error_codes.meaning: Keypad cable unplugged or not detected
error_codes.detail: The control board cannot see the touchpad at all — typically the ribbon cable has worked loose or its contacts have oxidized. The display may work while buttons do nothing.

causes.description: Keypad ribbon cable loose or unplugged

fixes.title: Reseat the ribbon cable
fixes.description: Disconnect power, open the console, unplug and firmly reseat the keypad ribbon; clean contacts gently with isopropyl alcohol.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the console.

fixes.title: Inspect the cable
fixes.description: Look for cracks or torn traces in the flat cable — a damaged cable means a new keypad assembly.

fixes.title: Replace the keypad
fixes.description: If reseating doesn't restore detection, replace the touchpad assembly.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reseat the cable, then restore power at the breaker.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a known-good keypad still isn't detected, the control board connector has failed — tech territory.

error_codes.meaning: Loss of communication between control boards
error_codes.detail: The appliance manager and the user interface board have stopped communicating. The display may freeze or go blank; oven functions are blocked until the link is restored.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes to force a re-handshake.

fixes.title: Reseat the harnesses
fixes.description: Reseat the communication harness connectors at both boards behind the console.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the control housing.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: Service-sheet testing identifies which side of the link is dead.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes and observe power-up.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board-vs-board diagnosis needs the tech sheet and a meter — call a pro if the power cycle doesn't fix it.

error_codes.meaning: Door latch fault — lock motor position not confirmed
error_codes.detail: The motorized door lock ran but never reached the commanded position according to its switches. Self-clean won't start, or the door remains locked after the cycle.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes — the latch re-homes on power-up.

fixes.title: Inspect the latch
fixes.description: Check the latch assembly for jams and burnt connectors.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before reaching into the latch area.

fixes.title: Replace the latch assembly
fixes.description: Motor, cam, and switches replace as one unit.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes; the latch motor re-homes on power-up.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the door stays locked after a power cycle, call a tech rather than forcing it.

error_codes.meaning: Main control board EEPROM error
error_codes.detail: The dryer's main control failed its internal memory check. Sometimes a hard reset clears corrupted data after a power event; a board that re-throws F01 is failing.

causes.description: Memory corruption after a surge or outage

fixes.title: Hard reset
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer for 10 minutes, then restore power and start a timed-dry cycle.

fixes.title: Check the power source
fixes.description: Verify the outlet/cord connections; brownouts can corrupt control memory.
fixes.warning: On electric dryers the terminal block carries 240V — disconnect power before inspecting.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If F01 returns after a hard reset, replace the main control.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, then restore power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: A board that re-throws F01 after a reset needs replacement — a tech can confirm in one visit.

error_codes.meaning: Communication failure between main control and user interface
error_codes.detail: The dryer's main control board and console UI have lost communication. The console may be dark or unresponsive even though the machine has power.

causes.description: Loose harness between console and main board

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes to force the boards to re-link.

fixes.title: Reseat harness connectors
fixes.description: Open the console and the rear panel; reseat the communication harness at both ends.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening panels.

fixes.title: Replace the failed board
fixes.description: Tech-sheet diagnostics identify whether the UI or the main board dropped the link.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes and check whether the console wakes.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If reseating connectors doesn't restore the console, board diagnosis is a job for a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Motor fault — drum motor not responding
error_codes.detail: The control cannot drive the drum motor correctly: it failed to start, stalled, or its speed feedback is missing. Overloading can trigger it; otherwise suspect the motor or its board.

causes.description: Motor carbon brushes or windings worn

fixes.title: Reduce the load and power cycle
fixes.description: Remove half the laundry, unplug for 10 minutes, and retry.

fixes.title: Check the tacho sensor
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, remove the rear panel, and verify the tacho coil on the motor shaft is seated and reads continuity.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before removing panels.

fixes.title: Replace motor or board
fixes.description: Worn brushes/windings mean a motor; good motor readings point to the main board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 10 minutes, reduce the load, and restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Motor-vs-board diagnosis needs a multimeter — call a tech if a reduced load and reset don't clear F7.

error_codes.meaning: Room temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The indoor (room air) temperature sensor reads open or shorted, so the unit can't regulate to the set point. Many models keep running in a fallback mode with poor temperature control.

causes.description: Loose sensor connector on the indoor PCB

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug (or breaker off) for 5 minutes and restart — a marginal connection sometimes recovers.

fixes.title: Reseat the sensor plug
fixes.description: Open the indoor unit's front panel and electrical cover; reseat the small 2-pin sensor connector on the PCB.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the electrical cover.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: The room sensor is a cheap clip-in probe near the air inlet.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Disconnect power for 5 minutes, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor doesn't clear E1, the indoor board input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Indoor–outdoor unit communication failure
error_codes.detail: On Haier split systems, E7 means the indoor and outdoor units have lost their data link. The system stops cooling because the indoor unit can't command the compressor.

causes.description: Wiring damaged by weather or rodents

fixes.title: Power cycle the whole system
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes so both units reboot and re-establish the link.

fixes.title: Inspect the interconnect cable
fixes.description: Check the terminal blocks at both units for loose screws, corrosion, or chewed insulation.
fixes.warning: Turn off the breaker — terminal blocks carry mains voltage.

fixes.title: Test/replace a control board
fixes.description: If wiring is sound, a tech can scope the comms line to find the dead board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Interconnect and board diagnosis on a split system involves mains wiring outdoors — call an HVAC tech.

error_codes.meaning: Indoor coil (evaporator pipe) temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The sensor clipped to the indoor evaporator coil reads out of range. The unit loses its freeze-protection reference and may short-cycle or stop cooling to protect the coil.

causes.description: Sensor fallen out of its well on the coil
Loose connector on the indoor PCB

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Disconnect power for 5 minutes and restart.

fixes.title: Check the sensor placement
fixes.description: Open the front panel and verify the probe is fully seated in its copper well on the evaporator.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the indoor unit.

fixes.title: Replace the coil sensor
fixes.description: Out-of-spec resistance means a new probe — an inexpensive part.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Disconnect power for 5 minutes, then restart.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new coil sensor doesn't clear F1, the indoor board needs testing.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer compartment sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The freezer temperature sensor is reading outside its valid range, so the control can't regulate the freezer accurately. Fisher & Paykel ActiveSmart models flag the fault on the display and run a fallback duty cycle.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes — a marginal connection occasionally recovers, and the fault re-asserts immediately if genuine.

fixes.title: Test the sensor
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge, access the freezer sensor, and measure its resistance against the service chart; open or shorted readings mean replacement.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before accessing sensors.

fixes.title: Check the harness
fixes.description: Inspect the connector for condensation corrosion before condemning the board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes; the code returns on power-up if the fault is still present.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor doesn't clear F1, the control module input has failed — call a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Fresh food compartment sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The fresh-food (refrigerator) compartment thermistor reads out of range. Temperatures in the food compartment drift while the control runs on defaults.

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and watch whether the fault returns at power-up.

fixes.title: Test and replace the sensor
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge and measure the compartment sensor; replace if out of spec.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before accessing sensors.

fixes.title: Verify temperatures after repair
fixes.description: Give the cabinet 24 hours and confirm the fresh-food zone holds 37–40°F.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes after the repair to clear the fault.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Persistent F2 with a good sensor means control-module diagnosis — tech territory.

error_codes.meaning: Fan motor fault — a circulation fan is not running at commanded speed
error_codes.detail: The control drives the evaporator/circulation fan electronically and monitors its feedback; F40 means the fan failed to reach the commanded speed. Ice on the blade or a dead fan motor are the usual causes, and cooling performance drops quickly.

causes.description: Fan blade iced up and stalled

fixes.title: Check for ice
fixes.description: Unplug the fridge; if frost has locked the fan, defrost fully (8–24 hours, doors open, towels down) — then investigate why the defrost system let ice build.

fixes.title: Test the fan and connector
fixes.description: With the back panel off, verify the fan connector is seated and the blade spins freely by hand.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the evaporator cover.

fixes.title: Replace the fan motor
fixes.description: A fan that's free but won't run on power-up needs replacement.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 5 minutes (or fully defrost if iced), then restore power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the fan was iced over, have a tech check the defrost system — the fan fault will recur otherwise.

error_codes.meaning: Heating fault — heater circuit shut down
error_codes.detail: The control detected a fault in the heating circuit: the heater relay, the heating element, or an over-temperature cutout has interrupted heating. The drum may keep turning with no heat, and the program aborts with F66.

causes.description: Blocked airflow tripping the thermal cutout
Heater relay failure on the power board

fixes.title: Clear the airflow path
fixes.description: Clean the lint filters and the plinth/heat-exchanger filter; restricted airflow overheats the heater and trips the cutout.

fixes.title: Test the element and cutouts
fixes.description: Disconnect power and check the heater element and thermal limiters for continuity; reset or replace a tripped one-shot limiter.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the cabinet — the heater circuit is mains voltage.

fixes.title: Replace the power board
fixes.description: If the element and limiters test good, the heater relay on the board has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Disconnect power for 5 minutes, clean all filters, then restart a program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If F66 returns with clean filters, the heater circuit needs professional testing — Miele boards are expensive to guess at.

error_codes.meaning: Drum drive speed sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control lost the speed feedback signal from the drum drive — the tacho/speed sensor is not reporting, so the program aborts. The motor may briefly start and then stop.

causes.description: Loose sensor connector at the motor

fixes.title: Power cycle
fixes.description: Disconnect power for 5 minutes and retry — an intermittent connection occasionally recovers.

fixes.title: Reseat the motor connector
fixes.description: Disconnect power, open the rear panel, and reseat the harness at the motor and tacho sensor.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening panels.

fixes.title: Replace the speed sensor or motor
fixes.description: Replace the tacho if it reads open; a good tacho with no drive points to the motor or board.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Disconnect power for 5 minutes, then restart a program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If reseating connectors doesn't clear F53, sensor/motor diagnosis needs Miele service documentation — call a tech.

error_codes.meaning: Electronic control fault — safety shutdown
error_codes.detail: The oven's electronic control detected an internal fault and performed a safety shutdown. This is a control-module-level error: heating is blocked until the fault clears or the module is repaired.

causes.description: Memory corruption after a power event

fixes.title: Hard reset
fixes.description: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes so the electronics fully discharge, then restore power.

fixes.title: Check the power supply
fixes.description: Verify the oven's circuit isn't browning out — voltage dips can fault Miele controls.
fixes.warning: Oven circuits are 240V — leave breaker-panel work to an electrician.

fixes.title: Replace the control module
fixes.description: An F33 that returns after a hard reset means the electronic unit needs replacement or factory repair.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Turn off the breaker for 10 minutes, then restore power.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele control modules are model-coded — a returning F33 should go to Miele service or an authorized tech.

error_codes.meaning: Water intake fault (steam/combi models)
error_codes.detail: On Miele steam and combi-steam ovens, F10 indicates the water intake failed — the unit didn't draw water from the tank or plumbed supply within the expected time. Steam programs abort.

causes.description: Water tank empty or seated incorrectly
Scale blocking the intake path or valve

fixes.title: Refill and reseat the tank
fixes.description: Fill the tank to the mark and push it firmly into its dock — a half-seated tank can't be drawn from.

fixes.title: Descale the appliance
fixes.description: Run the descaling program with Miele descaler; scale in the intake path is the most common cause on hard water.

fixes.title: Replace the intake pump/valve
fixes.description: If the tank is full and descaling doesn't help, the intake pump or valve has failed.
fixes.warning: Disconnect power before opening the appliance.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Refill the tank, reseat it, and restart the program; power cycle 5 minutes if the fault persists.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If descaling and a proper tank fit don't clear F10, the intake hardware needs Miele service.

error_codes.meaning: Drain error — water not draining within the time limit
error_codes.detail: 5C is the drain-failure code on current Samsung washers (older models display the same fault as 5E or OE). The control started the drain pump but the water level didn't drop within about 15 minutes, so the cycle stops with water still in the drum. A clogged debris filter is the most common cause by a wide margin.

causes.description: Kinked, frozen, or improperly installed drain hose
Drain hose inserted too far into standpipe (siphon/backflow)

fixes.title: Clean the debris filter
fixes.description: Open the small access door at the bottom front, place towels and a shallow pan, slowly unscrew the filter, and clear coins, lint, and hair. This alone fixes most 5C errors.
fixes.warning: Drain water can be hot right after a cycle.

fixes.title: Check the drain hose
fixes.description: Straighten any kinks behind the washer and confirm the hose enters the standpipe no more than 6 inches with an air gap.

fixes.title: Listen to the drain pump
fixes.description: Start a Spin-only cycle and listen at the lower front. A humming pump with no water movement has a jammed impeller; silence points to a dead pump or board relay.

fixes.title: Replace the drain pump
fixes.description: Remove the lower front panel, disconnect the hoses and harness, and swap the pump assembly.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening any panel.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After clearing the filter and hose, unplug the washer for 60 seconds, then run a Rinse+Spin cycle to confirm draining.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the filter and hose are clear and the pump runs but 5C persists, the pressure sensor or its air hose may be misreading the water level — that diagnosis is worth a technician if you're not comfortable with a multimeter.

error_codes.meaning: Leak detected — moisture sensed in the base pan
error_codes.detail: The leak sensor in the washer's base pan detected water where it shouldn't be. The machine stops and pumps out to protect your floor. The trigger is often a real leak (hose clamp, door boot, dispenser overflow), but oversudsing from too much detergent can push foam past seals and trip it too.

causes.description: Over-sudsing pushing foam past the tub seals
Door boot gasket torn or fouled at the bottom fold
Internal hose clamp or dispenser hose leaking
Tub seal or drain pump housing leak

fixes.title: Reduce detergent and rerun
fixes.description: Use HE detergent only, and less of it (2 tsp is enough for most loads). Run a Rinse+Spin and watch for suds — foam-triggered LC needs no parts.

fixes.title: Inspect the door boot
fixes.description: Pull back the gasket folds and look for tears, pinholes at the bottom, and trapped debris.

fixes.title: Check under the machine
fixes.description: Tip the washer back (two people) or pull it forward and look for drip trails from the dispenser hoses, inlet valve, and pump housing.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer and shut the water supply before tipping.

fixes.title: Dry the base pan
fixes.description: Towel out the base pan so the sensor resets — LC repeats until the pan is dry even after the leak is fixed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Fix the leak source, towel the base pan dry, then unplug for 60 seconds and restart. LC returns immediately if moisture remains at the sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If you can't find the leak source after checking the boot, hoses, and pump, a technician can run a pressurized fill test — persistent unexplained LC sometimes traces to a hairline tub crack.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor short circuit — temperature sensor reading too low resistance
error_codes.detail: The control board reads the exhaust thermistor continuously to regulate heat. tS means the sensor circuit reads as shorted (near-zero resistance), so the dryer can't trust its temperature reading and stops heating. The thermistor itself or a pinched harness wire is almost always the cause.

causes.description: Harness wire pinched or chafed to ground

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer, remove the back or lower front panel, and measure the thermistor on the heater/blower housing: about 10kΩ at room temperature. Near 0Ω confirms tS.

fixes.title: Inspect the harness
fixes.description: Trace the two sensor wires for pinch points where panels were assembled, and check the connector pins.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Two screws and a connector — one of the cheapest, easiest dryer repairs.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before removing panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After replacement, plug back in and run a Time Dry cycle on medium heat to confirm normal operation.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor still reads shorted at the board connector, the harness or main board input has failed — meter the harness before buying a board.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor open circuit — temperature sensor disconnected or failed open
error_codes.detail: The opposite of tS: the control reads infinite resistance on the exhaust thermistor circuit, as if the sensor isn't there at all. Without a temperature reading the dryer refuses to heat. An unplugged connector or a failed-open thermistor are the usual causes.

fixes.title: Reseat the connector
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer, open the access panel, and firmly reseat the thermistor connector — vibration works them loose, especially after a prior repair.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Measure across the sensor: ~10kΩ at room temperature is healthy; an open (OL) reading confirms replacement.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Swap the sensor on the heater/blower housing.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before removing panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Reseat or replace the sensor, then power the dryer and run a short heated cycle to confirm the code is gone.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sensor and harness both meter fine but tO persists, the main board's sensor input circuit is suspect.

error_codes.meaning: Door open — door detected open during operation
error_codes.detail: The dryer's door switch reports the door open while a cycle is running, so the drum stops immediately. If the door is visibly closed, the switch, striker, or catch is the problem — a worn catch that lets the door drift open mid-cycle is common on heavily used machines.

causes.description: Door not fully latched or popped open mid-cycle

fixes.title: Close firmly and restart
fixes.description: Push the door until it clicks solidly, then press Start. If it won't stay closed, the catch is worn.

fixes.title: Replace the door catch
fixes.description: The plastic catch/strike kit is a 5-minute fix with a flat screwdriver.

fixes.title: Test the door switch
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer, remove the top/front panel edge, and check switch continuity: closed door = closed circuit.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before testing the switch.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Close the door firmly and press Start/Pause. If the code persists with the door closed, power-cycle for 60 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Door switch and catch replacements are easy DIY. Call a pro only if a new switch still won't register — that points to the control board.

error_codes.meaning: Water supply error — dishwasher not filling
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher opened its inlet valve but didn't detect water arriving within the fill window. The cycle pauses with 4C (4E on older displays). Almost always a supply problem — a closed valve, low pressure, or a clogged inlet screen — rather than a failed part inside the machine.

causes.description: Supply valve under the sink closed or partially open
Clogged inlet screen at the valve

fixes.title: Open the supply valve fully
fixes.description: Check the shutoff under the sink — it should be fully counterclockwise. Half-open valves drop pressure below the fill threshold.

fixes.title: Check water pressure
fixes.description: The dishwasher needs roughly 20 psi minimum. If the kitchen faucet runs weak too, the problem is upstream.

fixes.title: Clean the inlet screen
fixes.description: Shut the supply, disconnect the fill line at the dishwasher's inlet valve, and clean the small mesh screen of scale and grit.
fixes.warning: Put a towel down — the line holds water.

fixes.title: Replace the inlet valve
fixes.description: If supply and screen are good but no water enters, swap the inlet valve (front lower panel, two wires and two connections).
fixes.warning: Switch off the breaker before opening the panel.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Restore the water supply, then switch the breaker off for 60 seconds and run a short cycle to confirm filling.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If water pressure and the valve check out but 4C persists, the flow sensor or control board needs professional testing.

error_codes.meaning: High temperature heater error — water overheating detected
error_codes.detail: The dishwasher's temperature sensor reported wash water above the safe ceiling (roughly 176°F), so the control halts the cycle to protect the machine and your dishes. A heater relay stuck on, or a drifting thermistor falsely reading high, are the leading causes.

causes.description: Thermistor drifted or failed (false high reading)
Heater relay on the control board stuck closed
Incoming water far above 120°F (water heater set too high)

fixes.title: Check your water heater setting
fixes.description: If the house water heater is set above ~140°F, the dishwasher can overshoot during heated cycles. 120°F is the right setting.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Switch off the breaker, pull the unit, and measure the sump thermistor against its spec (resistance falls as temperature rises). A drifted sensor causes false HC codes.

fixes.title: Suspect the heater relay
fixes.description: If the water is genuinely scalding at mid-cycle, the board's heater relay may be welded closed — the board needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Heater circuits carry line voltage. Switch off the breaker before any testing.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the breaker off for 60 seconds and rerun a normal cycle. If HC returns mid-cycle, stop using the dishwasher until diagnosed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: A genuinely overheating dishwasher is a safety issue — if the tub is scalding hot when HC appears, stop using it and have the heater circuit professionally diagnosed.

error_codes.meaning: Over-level water error — too much water in the tub
error_codes.detail: The water level sensor detected more water than the program ever calls for. The dishwasher stops and runs its drain pump to bring the level down. A sticking inlet valve that doesn't close cleanly, or a misreading level sensor, are the usual causes.

causes.description: Inlet valve not closing fully (debris or worn solenoid)
Drain restriction leaving water from the previous cycle

fixes.title: Confirm it drains
fixes.description: Cancel the cycle and let it pump out. If water remains, fix drainage first (filter, air gap, disposer connection) — leftover water plus a normal fill reads as overfill.

fixes.title: Watch the fill
fixes.description: Start a cycle and watch whether water keeps trickling in after the fill should have stopped — that's a sticking inlet valve. Replace it.

fixes.title: Check the level sensor
fixes.description: Inspect the sensor/air trap at the sump for gunk; a fouled air chamber misreports the level to the board.
fixes.warning: Switch off the breaker before removing the kick plate.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the breaker off for 60 seconds. The unit drains excess water automatically on the next power-up; run a short cycle to confirm normal fill.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the valve and sensor both check out, water may be siphoning back in from the drain connection — a pro can verify the installation in one visit.

error_codes.meaning: Power failure — the cycle was interrupted by a loss of power
error_codes.detail: PF is informational: the washer lost power mid-cycle and is telling you the cycle didn't finish. A one-off PF after a storm or outage is nothing to worry about. Recurring PF codes with no outages point to a loose plug, a failing outlet, or an intermittent connection on the washer's power path.

causes.description: Failing line filter or power cord

fixes.title: Clear and rerun
fixes.description: Press Power, select a cycle, and restart — or use Rinse+Spin if the load was nearly done.

fixes.title: Check the plug and outlet
fixes.description: Make sure the plug seats firmly and the outlet isn't loose or heat-discolored. Move the washer to its own outlet if it shares one.

fixes.title: Watch for a pattern
fixes.description: If PF appears during spin specifically, the motor's current draw may be tripping a weak breaker — have the circuit checked.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Power off and back on, reselect the cycle, and press Start. PF clears as soon as a new cycle begins.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Recurring PF with no household outages deserves an electrician's look at the outlet and breaker before suspecting the washer's control board.

error_codes.meaning: Thermistor error — air temperature sensor out of range
error_codes.detail: The control board reads an implausible value from the air thermistor (tE1 = the exhaust/air sensor circuit; tE2 variants point at the inlet sensor on some models). Without a believable temperature signal the dryer won't heat. The sensor is cheap and the test takes five minutes with a multimeter.

causes.description: Lint packed around the sensor skewing readings

fixes.title: Clean around the sensor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer, remove the lower front or back panel, and vacuum lint from the thermistor on the blower/heater housing.

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Measure resistance at room temperature — roughly 10kΩ on LG dryers. Open or near-zero readings confirm replacement.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: One or two screws plus a connector.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before removing panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: After cleaning or replacement, plug back in and run a Time Dry cycle on medium heat to verify the code is gone.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new thermistor reads correctly at the board connector but tE1 persists, the main board's sensor input has failed.

error_codes.meaning: Residual water in the sump — filter or impeller area blocked
error_codes.detail: E22 means water remains in the sump after the drain step (distinct from E24/E25, which fire during draining). The filter unit is dirty or the area around the impeller is clogged so the last of the water can't reach the pump. Standing water in the filter well after every cycle is the classic symptom.

causes.description: Filter unit clogged with food debris
Impeller cover area blocked (glass shards, fruit pits)
Drain pump weak or partially jammed

fixes.title: Clean the filter unit
fixes.description: Twist out the cylindrical filter in the tub floor, separate the coarse and fine elements, and rinse thoroughly.

fixes.title: Clear the pump well
fixes.description: With the filter out, sponge out the water and check the well for debris. Pop the white impeller cover (it unsnaps) and clear glass or pits around the impeller.
fixes.warning: Glass shards collect here — wear gloves.

fixes.title: Run a hot service wash
fixes.description: Run the hottest short program empty with dishwasher cleaner to flush grease from the sump passages.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the filter and impeller area, then hold Start/Reset for 3–5 seconds to cancel and pump out, and run a short program to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the sump is spotless and E22 continues, the drain pump is weakening — replacement is straightforward for a technician and borderline DIY.

error_codes.meaning: Water flow sensor fault — fill volume not being measured
error_codes.detail: The flow sensor (aquaflow meter) that counts water entering the machine isn't reporting plausible pulses, so the control can't meter the fill. The cycle may abort or fill erratically. Scale or debris jamming the sensor's paddle wheel is the usual cause; the sensor itself fails less often.

causes.description: Scale or debris jamming the flow sensor wheel

fixes.title: Power-cycle first
fixes.description: Switch the unit off, wait 60 seconds, and rerun — transient sensor glitches clear with a reset.

fixes.title: Check the supply path
fixes.description: Confirm good water pressure and clean the inlet screen — weak flow reads like a sensor fault.

fixes.title: Replace the flow sensor
fixes.description: The sensor sits on the side of the unit near the inlet (accessed by removing the side or base panel). It's an inexpensive part but tight to reach.
fixes.warning: Switch off the breaker and water supply before opening panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch off at the unit or breaker for 60 seconds, restore power, and start a fresh program.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Reaching the flow sensor requires partial disassembly on most models — if you're not comfortable opening the side panel, this one's a fair technician call.

error_codes.meaning: Exhaust thermistor short circuit
error_codes.detail: The control reads the exhaust temperature sensor as shorted (near-zero resistance), so it can't regulate heat and stops the cycle. Identical to Whirlpool's F23 — Maytag dryers share the platform. The thermistor is cheap; a pinched harness wire grounding out is the alternative.

causes.description: Harness wire pinched to cabinet ground

fixes.title: Test the thermistor
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer, remove the lower access panel, and measure the sensor on the blower housing: ~10kΩ at room temperature; near 0Ω = replace.

fixes.title: Inspect the harness
fixes.description: Check the two sensor wires along the blower housing for pinch points and chafed insulation.

fixes.title: Replace the thermistor
fixes.description: Two screws and a connector swap.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before opening panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the dryer for 60 seconds after the repair and run a Timed Dry on medium heat to confirm.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor still reads shorted at the board end of the harness, the wiring or board input is at fault — meter before buying a control board.

error_codes.meaning: Moisture sensor open circuit
error_codes.detail: The control lost the signal from the moisture sensor bars inside the drum, so sensor-dry cycles can't tell when clothes are dry. Cycles run far too long or stop early. The same F28 appears on Whirlpool-built dryers. Fabric-softener film insulating the bars, or a broken sensor wire, are the common causes.

causes.description: Sensor bars coated with fabric softener film
Broken wire between sensor bars and board

fixes.title: Clean the sensor bars
fixes.description: Scrub the two metal strips inside the drum (usually below the door or on the lint screen housing) with rubbing alcohol to strip residue.

fixes.title: Check the connections
fixes.description: Unplug the dryer and inspect the sensor harness from the bars to the control — connectors work loose where the bulkhead vibrates.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor assembly
fixes.description: If cleaning and reseating don't restore sensing, swap the bar assembly.
fixes.warning: Unplug the dryer before removing the front panel.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the bars, unplug for 60 seconds, and run a sensor-dry cycle with a medium load to verify it ends when clothes are actually dry.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If timed cycles work but sensor cycles still misbehave after a new sensor, the control board's sensing circuit is suspect.

error_codes.meaning: Primary control board fault — board failed self-check
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool-built Kenmore washers (110-prefix, including the Oasis/Cabrio top-load platform), F1 means the machine control board failed its own self-check or lost contact with a critical sensor input. The infamous 'Kenmore F1 error' on Oasis-era top-loaders was so common that the boards became a staple repair part.

causes.description: Pressure sensor hose disconnected from the board
Connector backed out at the board

fixes.title: Power-cycle the washer
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and retry — a one-off F1 after a surge sometimes clears for good.

fixes.title: Reseat board connectors and the pressure hose
fixes.description: Unplug the washer, open the console/rear panel, and firmly reseat every connector — especially the small clear pressure-sensor hose, which slips off and produces F1.

fixes.title: Replace the control board
fixes.description: If F1 returns every cycle, replace the machine control. On Oasis/Cabrio-era models this is a well-documented 30-minute repair.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before opening the console.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the washer for 5 minutes, plug back in, and start a Drain & Spin. Persistent F1 means the board needs attention — note whether it appears at the same point each cycle.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Board replacement is DIY-feasible, but if your model number isn't matching replacement parts cleanly (Kenmore cross-referencing can be fiddly), a technician will know the equivalent Whirlpool part on sight.

error_codes.meaning: Vent open error — drying vent failed to close
error_codes.detail: Frigidaire dishwashers dry with a motorized vent that opens to release moist air. The 'uo' code means the control sees the vent stuck open when it should be closed — wash performance continues but drying and heat retention suffer, and the cycle flags the fault. A failed vent assembly or wax motor is the usual cause.

causes.description: Debris or grease jamming the vent flap
Wiring fault to the vent assembly

fixes.title: Power-cycle
fixes.description: Switch the breaker off for 60 seconds — if the vent was momentarily stuck, the reset clears it.

fixes.title: Inspect the vent
fixes.description: Find the vent on the inner door (top corner on most models), and check the flap for grease or detergent buildup keeping it open.

fixes.title: Replace the vent assembly
fixes.description: The vent and its wax-motor actuator replace as a unit from the inner door panel.
fixes.warning: Switch off the breaker before removing the inner door screws.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the breaker off for 60 seconds and run a short cycle. The code clears once the control sees the vent close properly.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: The dishwasher remains usable with 'uo' showing, so there's no urgency — but if dishes come out wet and the code persists after a new vent assembly, have the control board checked.

error_codes.meaning: Communication failure between control boards
error_codes.detail: The user-interface board and the main control board stopped talking to each other. The display may freeze, and cooling control becomes unreliable. On Frigidaire refrigerators this is most often a connector or harness problem between the two boards — actual board failures are the minority of cases.

causes.description: Loose or corroded harness connector between boards
Wiring damage in the door hinge loom (dispenser models)

fixes.title: Power-cycle
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes, then restore power — momentary comm glitches after outages clear with a reset.

fixes.title: Reseat the connectors
fixes.description: Unplug the unit, remove the upper hinge cover / rear access panel, and reseat the communication harness connectors at both boards.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before opening any panel.

fixes.title: Replace the failing board
fixes.description: If the code returns, the main board (back of the unit) is the more common culprit and the easier swap.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes and restore power. Allow a few minutes for the boards to re-handshake before judging.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Distinguishing a failed main board from a failed UI board needs a methodical swap or meter test — if a connector reseat doesn't fix it, a technician resolves it in one visit with both parts on hand.

error_codes.meaning: Dispenser system fault — dispenser motor not reaching position
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool front-loaders with motorized detergent dispensers, F8E2 means the dispenser motor couldn't reach its commanded position. Detergent or softener may not dispense, and the cycle pauses. Gummed-up detergent residue physically blocking the dispenser mechanism is the leading cause.

causes.description: Detergent residue gumming the dispenser mechanism

fixes.title: Deep-clean the dispenser
fixes.description: Remove the dispenser drawer, soak it in hot water, and scrub the housing cavity — hardened detergent jams the selector mechanism.

fixes.title: Cycle the dispenser
fixes.description: Run a Clean Washer cycle after cleaning; listen for the dispenser motor stepping through positions at the start.

fixes.title: Replace the dispenser actuator
fixes.description: If the motor doesn't move or grinds, replace the dispenser motor/actuator assembly under the top panel.
fixes.warning: Unplug the washer before removing the top panel.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Clean the dispenser, unplug the washer for 60 seconds, and run a short cycle. F8E2 clears once the dispenser completes its position check.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a cleaned and free-moving dispenser still throws F8E2, the actuator motor or its board driver has failed — the actuator is the cheaper first replacement.

error_codes.meaning: Compressor abnormal current detected
error_codes.detail: The inverter board measured current to the compressor outside its safe envelope and shut the compressor down to protect it. 83E frequently appears after a power outage or voltage sag rather than from a genuinely failed compressor — Samsung's own guidance is to power-reset first and only investigate hardware if it returns.

fixes.title: Power-reset the unit
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator (or breaker off) for 5 minutes, then restore power and give the compressor several minutes to restart.

fixes.title: Check for a pattern
fixes.description: If 83E returns without any household power event, note when — startup vs steady-state points to inverter vs compressor.

fixes.title: Inspect connections
fixes.description: Unplug the unit, remove the rear lower panel, and reseat the inverter-to-compressor connector.
fixes.warning: Capacitors on the inverter board hold charge — don't probe the board itself.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes and restore power. If 83E reappears within a day without a power event, stop resetting and get the inverter/compressor professionally diagnosed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Compressor and inverter diagnosis needs a meter and experience — and Samsung's 10-year sealed-system warranty may cover it. Call Samsung support with your model and serial before paying out of pocket.

error_codes.meaning: Compressor under-voltage error — supply voltage too low
error_codes.detail: The inverter detected supply voltage below its operating floor while driving the compressor. Brownouts, overloaded household circuits, and shared outlets are the common triggers; the refrigerator protects itself and retries. Persistent 85E with healthy household power points at the inverter board.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and restore. 85E after a brownout clears for good once supply stabilizes.

fixes.title: Check the circuit
fixes.description: Make sure the refrigerator has its own outlet — space heaters or microwaves sharing the circuit cause voltage dips under load.

fixes.title: Measure outlet voltage
fixes.description: If comfortable, verify the outlet reads ~115–125V under load. Chronically low readings are an electrician problem, not a refrigerator problem.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes and restore power. Track whether 85E coincides with heavy appliance use elsewhere in the house.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If your outlet voltage is healthy and 85E keeps returning, the inverter board needs professional replacement — and chronic low household voltage deserves an electrician's visit either way.

error_codes.meaning: Compressor over-voltage error — supply voltage too high
error_codes.detail: The inverter detected supply voltage above its safe ceiling and stopped the compressor to protect it. Voltage spikes from grid switching, generator use, or wiring faults are typical triggers. As with the other power codes, a reset clears one-off events; recurrence means the supply or the inverter board needs attention.

causes.description: Generator or backup-power supply running high

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Unplug for 5 minutes and restore power.

fixes.title: Check unusual power sources
fixes.description: If running on a generator or battery backup, verify its output voltage — small generators commonly run high at light load.

fixes.title: Consider surge protection
fixes.description: A surge-protected outlet or whole-home protector prevents repeat spikes from reaching the inverter.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the refrigerator for 5 minutes and restore power. If on generator power, switch back to mains before judging whether the code returns.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Recurring 86E on normal mains power means either a wiring/voltage problem (electrician) or a failing inverter board (appliance technician). Don't keep resetting through it — over-voltage events stress the compressor.

error_codes.meaning: Refrigerator compartment temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The control board is getting an open, shorted, or implausible reading from the fresh-food compartment temperature sensor. Without it, the control can't regulate fridge temperature accurately — expect the compartment to drift warm or cold. The sensor itself or its connector is almost always the cause.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Unplug the refrigerator for 60 seconds and restore power — a transient reading glitch clears with a reboot.

fixes.title: Note the symptoms
fixes.description: Check actual fridge temperature with a thermometer. A compartment running notably warm or cold alongside E01 confirms the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: The fridge-compartment sensor sits behind an interior panel; replacement is a connector swap once the panel is off.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing interior panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 60 seconds and restore power. If E01 returns, the sensor needs testing — its resistance should change smoothly as it warms or cools.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If a new sensor reads correctly but E01 persists, the control board's sensor input has failed — board diagnosis is a technician job.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer compartment temperature sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The freezer's temperature sensor is reading open, shorted, or implausible values. The control falls back to timed cooling, so the freezer may over-freeze or warm up unpredictably. As with E01, the sensor or its connector is the usual culprit and the fix is inexpensive.

causes.description: Connector or harness fault (cold-side connections corrode)

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Unplug for 60 seconds and restore power.

fixes.title: Verify freezer temperature
fixes.description: Use a thermometer — the freezer should hold 0°F (-18°C). Significant drift alongside E02 confirms the sensor.

fixes.title: Replace the sensor
fixes.description: Access is behind the freezer's interior panel; the part clips to the evaporator area and plugs into the harness.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing the freezer panel.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 60 seconds and restore power. Watch freezer temperature for a few hours — drifting temps with the code back means replace the sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Food safety matters here: if the freezer can't hold temperature, move food to a backup freezer and prioritize the repair.

error_codes.meaning: Temperature-controlled drawer sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The sensor for the chiller / VitaFresh temperature-controlled drawer is reading out of range. Main fridge and freezer cooling continue normally, but the drawer can't hold its setpoint — produce or meat stored there may freeze or spoil faster than expected.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Unplug for 60 seconds and restore power.

fixes.title: Relocate sensitive food
fixes.description: Until fixed, move meat/fish out of the drawer to the main compartment shelves where temperature control still works.

fixes.title: Replace the drawer sensor
fixes.description: Behind the drawer's rear trim panel; clip and connector replacement.
fixes.warning: Unplug the refrigerator before removing trim panels.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug for 60 seconds and restore power. The code clears once the control reads a plausible drawer temperature.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Because main cooling is unaffected, this repair isn't urgent — bundle it with any other service visit if you're not DIYing it.

error_codes.meaning: Refrigerator compartment sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The fridge-compartment temperature sensor is returning an implausible signal, so the control can't regulate the compartment accurately. Miele units typically keep cooling on a fallback program, but temperature drifts. Miele documents this as a fault requiring sensor access behind interior panels.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch the unit off, wait 60 seconds, and switch it back on — transient sensor glitches clear with a restart.

fixes.title: Monitor with a thermometer
fixes.description: Confirm whether the compartment actually holds 37–40°F. Drift alongside F1 confirms a real sensor fault.

fixes.title: Book sensor replacement
fixes.description: Miele's compartment sensors sit behind interior panels that aren't designed for customer removal.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the appliance off for 60 seconds and back on. If F1 returns, note the actual compartment temperature for the technician.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Miele recommends authorized service for sensor faults — interior panel access without the right procedure risks damaging the liner, and authorized parts keep the (long) Miele warranty intact.

error_codes.meaning: Evaporator sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The sensor on the evaporator coil is reading open, shorted, or out of range. The control uses this sensor to time defrost cycles, so a failure leads to frost buildup on the evaporator and gradually weakening cooling. A unit that cools poorly with F2 showing has likely already iced up.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch off for 60 seconds and back on.

fixes.title: Check for icing symptoms
fixes.description: Weak cooling, a rear interior wall of frost, or a fan working loudly all suggest the defrost timing has already been affected.

fixes.title: Book service
fixes.description: Evaporator sensor access requires opening the cooling-compartment panels — an authorized-service job on Miele units.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the appliance off for 60 seconds and back on. If cooling is already weak, a full manual defrost (unit off, doors open, 24h) restores temporary performance until service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Evaporator-side work on Miele refrigeration is authorized-service territory. If cooling has degraded, don't delay — running iced-up stresses the compressor.

error_codes.meaning: Freezer compartment air sensor fault
error_codes.detail: The air-temperature sensor in the freezer compartment is returning an implausible value. The control can't verify the freezer is holding temperature, so it runs a fallback program. Food safety is the practical risk — the display can no longer be trusted to reflect real freezer temperature.

causes.description: Connector/harness fault on the cold side

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch off for 60 seconds and back on.

fixes.title: Verify with a thermometer
fixes.description: Place a thermometer in the freezer — it should hold 0°F (-18°C). Use this, not the display, while the fault is active.

fixes.title: Book service
fixes.description: Sensor replacement requires freezer panel access per Miele's service procedure.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the appliance off for 60 seconds and back on. Keep a thermometer in the freezer until the sensor is replaced.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: If the freezer can't verifiably hold 0°F, prioritize the service visit and avoid loading new unfrozen food in the meantime.

error_codes.meaning: Fan motor fault
error_codes.detail: An interior circulation fan isn't running as commanded. Cold air stops moving evenly, so some areas run cold while others warm up — the classic symptom is temperature stratification between shelves. Ice jamming the fan or a failed fan motor are the documented causes.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch off for 60 seconds and back on; listen for the fan resuming.

fixes.title: Manual defrost if iced
fixes.description: If you hear the fan straining or chirping, a full defrost (off, doors open, 24h) frees an ice-jammed blade.

fixes.title: Book fan replacement
fixes.description: If the fan stays silent after a thaw and reset, the motor has failed.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the appliance off for 60 seconds and back on. A 24-hour manual defrost is the one effective self-help step before service.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Repeated fan icing means the defrost system needs diagnosis too — mention the history when booking Miele service so both get fixed in one visit.

error_codes.meaning: Open thermal sensor — cavity temperature not detected
error_codes.detail: GE documents F1 as an open thermal sensor: during convection, combination, or roast cooking the control expects the cavity to reach temperature within 15 minutes, and an open sensor circuit means it never sees the rise. Microwave-only cooking may still work; convection functions stop.

fixes.title: Clear and retry
fixes.description: Press Cancel/Off to clear the code, then retry the convection function once — a one-off glitch clears.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Switch the breaker (or unplug the unit) for 60 seconds.

fixes.title: Book sensor replacement
fixes.description: The thermal sensor mounts on the cavity behind the outer case — microwave case removal carries shock risk even unplugged.
fixes.warning: Microwave capacitors store a lethal charge after unplugging. Do not open the case.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel/Off to clear the display, or power-cycle at the breaker for 60 seconds. F1 returning during convection cooking confirms the sensor.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: All internal microwave repairs are professional territory — the high-voltage capacitor can hold a lethal charge long after unplugging. The fix itself (a sensor swap) is quick for a technician.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted thermal sensor
error_codes.detail: The opposite of F1: the cavity thermal sensor circuit reads shorted, so the control sees a false high-temperature signal. The microwave may refuse convection functions or abort cooking. Sensor or harness replacement is the standard fix per GE's fault table.

fixes.title: Clear and retry
fixes.description: Press Cancel/Off, then retry. If F2 returns immediately, the short is hard.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Breaker off (or unplug) for 60 seconds.

fixes.title: Book service
fixes.description: Sensor and harness access requires opening the case — a technician job on microwaves.
fixes.warning: Do not open a microwave case — the HV capacitor stores a lethal charge.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel/Off to clear, or power-cycle at the breaker for 60 seconds.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Same safety rule as every internal microwave fault: case-off work is for technicians. If the unit is over ~8 years old, replacement often beats the service call on countertop models — OTR models are usually worth fixing.

error_codes.meaning: Shorted touchpad — keypad fault
error_codes.detail: The control detects one or more keypad lines stuck closed, as if a button were held down permanently. Phantom button presses, an unresponsive pad, or continuous beeping accompany it. Moisture or grease wicking into the membrane keypad — common above a range — is the usual cause.

causes.description: Moisture or grease in the membrane keypad

fixes.title: Dry it out
fixes.description: If the code appeared after heavy stovetop steaming or panel cleaning, power off at the breaker for a few hours and let the panel dry.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Breaker off for 60 seconds, then retest each button row.

fixes.title: Replace the touchpad/control panel
fixes.description: If specific buttons stay dead or phantom presses continue, the membrane assembly needs replacement.
fixes.warning: Panel replacement requires case access — leave it to a technician.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Switch the breaker off for 60 seconds (longer if moisture is suspected — give it a few hours to dry), then restore power and test the keypad.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: On over-the-range models the touchpad assembly is usually worth replacing professionally; on budget countertop units the part plus labor often exceeds a new microwave.

error_codes.meaning: Open humidity sensor — sensor cooking disabled
error_codes.detail: GE's sensor-cook features (Popcorn, Reheat, Sensor Cook) read steam through a humidity sensor. F4 means that sensor circuit reads open, so sensor-cook modes fail or abort — manual time cooking keeps working normally. The sensor or its connector has failed.

fixes.title: Use manual modes meanwhile
fixes.description: Time-based cooking is unaffected — the fault only blocks sensor-driven modes.

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Breaker off for 60 seconds and retry a sensor-cook mode.

fixes.title: Book sensor replacement
fixes.description: The humidity sensor mounts in the vent path inside the case.
fixes.warning: Internal access is technician-only — HV capacitor hazard.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Press Cancel/Off to clear the code. The microwave remains fully usable in manual modes while you decide on the repair.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Because manual cooking still works, this is the rare microwave fault you can reasonably live with — repair it opportunistically or when convenience matters.

error_codes.meaning: Inverter / magnetron power fault
error_codes.detail: On Whirlpool inverter-type microwaves, F7 means the control detected a problem delivering power to the magnetron — the inverter board reported a fault or the expected power draw never materialized. The unit may run but not heat, or abort with the code. A failed inverter board is the documented usual cause, sometimes with a visible burn spot on the board.

causes.description: Harness fault between control and inverter

fixes.title: Power-reset
fixes.description: Unplug (or breaker off) for 60 seconds — a transient inverter fault clears with a reboot.

fixes.title: Test heating
fixes.description: Heat a cup of water for 60 seconds on high. Running-but-not-heating alongside F7 confirms the power path to the magnetron.

fixes.title: Book service
fixes.description: Inverter and magnetron replacement are internal, high-voltage repairs.
fixes.warning: Never open a microwave case — the capacitor holds a lethal charge after unplugging.

error_codes.reset_instructions: Unplug the microwave for 60 seconds and retest with a cup of water. If F7 returns or heating is weak, stop using it and have the inverter diagnosed.
error_codes.when_to_call_pro: Magnetron/inverter repairs run $150–$350 with labor — worth it on built-in and OTR models, rarely on countertop units. Whirlpool magnetrons carry longer warranty terms on some models; check yours before paying.
